title: _REDIR_RE over-blocks the greater-or-equal comparison as a redirect (confine hooks FP)
description:
## Goal / Problem
`_REDIR_RE` in the two confinement hooks misreads a SPACE-preceded `>=` (and `>` immediately followed by `=`) as a shell output-redirect to a file named `=`, hard-blocking benign commands that contain a `>=` comparison (awk/jq/arithmetic/`grep -E` alternation) and the agent's own `bd` operations. Fail-CLOSED FP (over-blocks legitimate input, not a security hole) but recurring + high-friction. Found 2026-06-14 (wh-sml follow-up a); CONFIRMED biting repeatedly — it blocked the wave's own `bd close` (reason text had a `>=2`), an `order.md`-review `grep -E` (this session), and the earlier `bd update --description` (order.md DO-NEXT #5 — same class).

## Reproduction
On a plugin-loaded session, each of these is BLOCKED by `confine_*` with reason `redirection writes ...: =`, though none is a redirect:
1. `echo $(( 3 >= 2 ))`
2. `awk '$3 >= 2' file`
3. `jq 'select(.n >= 2)'`
4. `grep -nE "foo|bar >= 2" file`
5. `bd close <id> --reason="... >=2 ..."` / `bd update --description="...>=..."` (metadata text containing `>=`).

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: a `>=` comparison is NOT a redirect — these commands run; only a genuine redirect (`echo x > file`, `cmd >> file`) is gated.
- Actual: `_REDIR_RE` matches the `>` in `>=`, captures `=` as the redirect target, `is_allowed_target("=")`/`is_write_allowed` rejects it, and `decide()` returns BLOCK.

## Suspected Root Cause (file:line)
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/confine_patch_writes.py:55` — `_REDIR_RE = ...>>?\|?\s*(...)` matches `>` even when immediately followed by `=`.
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/confine_self_writes.py:59` — the SAME `_REDIR_RE` pattern (both hooks must be fixed; both mediate `Bash|Write|Edit`).

## Files in Scope (fix surface)
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/confine_patch_writes.py` — add a negative lookahead `(?!=)` after the `>>?` in `_REDIR_RE` so `>=` is not a redirect.
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/confine_self_writes.py` — identical fix.
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests/test_confine_patch_writes.py` + `tests/test_confine_self_writes.py` — regression tests.
> **Operator-gated to APPLY:** these are gate/hook files; the agent cannot edit them in a plugin-loaded session (the hook blocks its own dir) — the operator applies, OR the fix lands in a plugin-unloaded session. Surgical (Policy 3): only the regex + tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both-ways (Policy 9): `decide()` ALLOWS the 5 repro commands above (no false redirect), AND still BLOCKs a genuine redirect to a non-lane path (`echo x > /etc/passwd`, `cmd >> ../outside`) and a `>|` noclobber redirect.
- [ ] Regression tests added in BOTH `test_confine_patch_writes.py` and `test_confine_self_writes.py` enumerating the `>=` cases + the still-blocked real-redirect cases.
- [ ] `_REDIR_RE` change is identical in both hooks (no divergence).

## Severity / Priority
P1 (bumped per the wh-sml handoff). It recurrently blocks the agent's OWN tooling (`bd close`/`bd update`/`grep`/`awk`/`jq`) on common input — a correctness defect in a gate. Fail-closed (no security exposure), but high, recurring friction.

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/hooks --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests -q`
- [ ] `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .`
- [ ] `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker`

## Rollback
Revert the `(?!=)` additions + the regression tests. Pure regex tightening — no migration.

## References
wh-sml report `docs/research/20260614_plugin_hooks_subagent_scope.md` §8(a); handoff `.notes/waves/20260612/wh-5ox.2+wh-sml/handoff.md`. Same FP class as `order.md` DO-NEXT #5. DO-NOT-COPY: N/A. Commit: `fix(hooks): _REDIR_RE stops misreading >= as a redirect (confine_patch/self_writes)`.

title: Durable operator dismissals for Scorecard-posture + fixture-vuln code-scanning alerts
description:
## Goal / Problem

Operator-dismiss the **5 dismiss-classified Scorecard-posture alerts + 2 deliberately-misconfigured PoC-fixture Pinned-Dependencies alerts** with stable-fingerprint rationale (verified durable across the weekly SARIF re-upload), and record the Maintained-check auto-resolve date. **No production-code/config change, no edit to the fixtures or scorecard.yml.**

> **OPERATOR-EXECUTED.** Dismissals are UI/`gh` actions and the Dependabot path may need a PAT — these are operator (operator-gated) actions. An agent can prepare the exact dismissal commands + rationales but **cannot complete this ticket**. Mark agent-prep done; leave the dismissals as the operator step.

## Background / Context

- Scorecard re-uploads a fresh SARIF weekly (Monday). A code-scanning dismissal keyed on the **fingerprint = ruleID + location** survives that re-upload; disabling/scoping the Scorecard SARIF does NOT (and is out of scope).
- The 5 posture checks are policy-level (branch protection, code review, fuzzing, CII best-practices, vulnerabilities) — intentional for a solo public reviewer repo, not defects to fix.
- The 2 PoC-fixture pins live in the deliberately-misconfigured IaC-scan demo input (`docs/research/poc-iac-scan/fixture-infra/Dockerfile:2,:5`) — they are the test's *point*; editing them would break the fixture.
- The Maintained check auto-resolves on repo age — no action, just record the flip date.

> **Machine-data-free (public-repo rule):** keep specific alert ids / dismissal evidence / `gh` output OUT of this ticket and beads — that record goes in gitignored `.notes/security_audit/<date>/` only.

## Steps (with description)

All are **durable manual dismissals** in Security > Code scanning (do NOT disable/scope the Scorecard SARIF):

1. **Branch-protection check** — dismiss "Won't fix": solo public repo, intentional direct-push; review happens in-session pre-push.
2. **Code-review check** — dismiss "Won't fix": same rationale (solo, in-session review).
3. **Fuzzing check** — dismiss "Won't fix": a static reviewer with no fuzz target.
4. **CII-best-practices check** — dismiss "Won't fix": optional badge not pursued (or earn it later on bestpractices.dev).
5. **Vulnerabilities check** — the underlying entries are deliberately-vulnerable eval/PoC fixture deps already auto-handled by the fixture-triage workflow (`.github/workflows/dependabot-fixture-triage.yml`). Dismiss the underlying Dependabot fixture alerts (needs a PAT per that workflow's header, OR dismiss manually once) THEN dismiss the Scorecard Vulnerabilities check alert.
6. **2 PoC-fixture Pinned-Dependencies alerts** (`docs/research/poc-iac-scan/fixture-infra/Dockerfile:2,:5`) — dismiss "Used in tests": deliberately-misconfigured IaC-scan PoC input. **Do NOT edit the fixture.**
7. **Maintained check** — NO action; auto-resolves at the repo-age threshold (+ trailing activity window). Compute the flip date from `gh repo view jaigouk/white-hacker --json createdAt` and record it ONLY in gitignored `.notes/`.

## Files to Modify

**None.** Operator UI/`gh` dismissals + a gitignored `.notes/security_audit/<date>/` record only. No repo file changes; the fixtures stay byte-for-byte unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The 5 Scorecard-posture alerts + 2 PoC-fixture Pinned-Dependencies alerts show state=dismissed with rationale.
- [ ] Fixture files unchanged: `git diff --exit-code docs/research/poc-iac-scan/` (exit 0).
- [ ] The Maintained-check flip date is computed + recorded in gitignored `.notes/` (NOT in beads/the repo).
- [ ] After the next weekly Scorecard re-upload, none of the dismissed alerts re-open (verify post-upload; mark DEFERRED until the Monday re-run).
- [ ] Security > Code scanning shows no open alerts (jointly with `wh-6ci.1` + `wh-6ci.2`; verify post-merge — DEFERRED until both siblings land + the scans re-run).

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

```bash
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
git diff --exit-code docs/research/poc-iac-scan/
```

- [ ] `validate_manifest.py` green
- [ ] `git diff --exit-code docs/research/poc-iac-scan/` exits 0 (fixtures untouched)
- [ ] (pytest) N/A — no repo code/test changes

## Rollback

Re-open the dismissed alerts (reversible via the same UI/`gh` path). No code to revert.

## Risks / Dependencies

- Risk: a dismissal keyed on a non-stable fingerprint re-opens on the Monday re-upload — verify durability before closing (the AC pins post-re-upload non-reopen).
- Dependency: file-disjoint from T1 + T2 (this ticket changes no repo files) — parallel-safe; the joint "no open alerts" AC depends on T1 + T2 also landing (verification timing, not a bd blocker).

## Notes / References

- `.github/workflows/dependabot-fixture-triage.yml` — the fixture-triage workflow header documents the PAT requirement for dismissing fixture Dependabot alerts.
- `docs/research/poc-iac-scan/fixture-infra/Dockerfile` — the deliberately-misconfigured IaC-scan PoC input (read at design; lines 2 + 5 are the unpinned `FROM`/install).
close_reason: Operator-executed durable dismissals complete 2026-06-13: 11 code-scanning alerts dismissed via gh api (5 Scorecard-posture + 4 ACCEPT pinned-deps 'won't fix' + 2 PoC-fixture 'used in tests'); Dependabot fixture alerts already dismissed (not_used). Fixtures unchanged (git diff --exit-code clean). #11 Maintained left to auto-resolve per step 7 (operator dismissing separately for instant green). Durability AC (none re-open after weekly Scorecard re-upload ~2026-06-15) DEFERRED. Evidence: .notes/security_audit/20260613/.

title: Clear CodeQL py/unused-import false positive on validate_watchlist re-exports
description:
## Goal / Problem

CodeQL opens a `py/unused-import` alert each scan for a cross-module re-export in `validate_watchlist.py`. It is a **false positive** — the name is a used re-export — but it keeps the code-scanning tab non-green. Low severity (FP, no runtime impact) but **P1** because it blocks the green-tab goal. Threat model: N/A (no security impact); class: CodeQL FP (reachability vs re-export).

## Reproduction

On a fresh checkout, CodeQL flags `py/unused-import` for `load_malware_db` at:

```
plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/validate_watchlist.py:63
```

The name is a re-export consumed as `validate_watchlist.load_malware_db` / `validate_watchlist.is_known_bad` in `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/test_validate_watchlist.py` (~lines 375-401). The existing `# noqa: F401` is honored by ruff but IGNORED by CodeQL.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** no unused-import alert — the imported name is a used re-export of the module's public surface.
- **Actual:** CodeQL opens the `py/unused-import` alert each scan (the re-export is invisible to its reachability analysis).

## Suspected Root Cause

`validate_watchlist.py:63` — the re-export is NOT declared in an `__all__`, so CodeQL's reachability analysis treats the imported name as dead. The line is:

```python
from malware_db import _accumulate, is_known_bad, load_malware_db  # noqa: E402,F401  (re-exported; ...)
```

A deliberate `sys.path` shim at ~lines 58-61 (the `_DEPS_SCAN_SCRIPTS` insert) forces the import below module top, which is why `E402` is also suppressed.

## Files in Scope (the fix surface)

- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/validate_watchlist.py` — the `__all__` declaration + the narrowed `noqa`.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/test_validate_watchlist.py` — the regression test (assert the public surface is unchanged; if an existing test already exercises `validate_watchlist.load_malware_db`/`.is_known_bad`, extend it rather than duplicate).

> Surgical (Policy 3): no unrelated refactor. Keep `_accumulate` OUT of `__all__` (private, used internally only).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

1. **RED** — add/extend a test asserting the module public surface (every `__all__` name resolves to a real attribute, and `_accumulate` is absent from `__all__`). It should fail before the `__all__` is added if it asserts presence of `__all__`.
2. **GREEN** — insert a blank line + an `__all__` tuple immediately AFTER the line-63 import (before `SCHEMA_PATH`) listing the public surface EXACTLY: `load_malware_db, is_known_bad, ADVISORY_HOSTS, REASONS, SCHEMA_PATH, load_schema, provenance_error, validate_entry, validate_bytes, validate_file, validate_dir, regression_errors, mint_verdict, main`. Narrow the line-63 suppression from `# noqa: E402,F401` to `# noqa: E402` (F401 drops — names now in `__all__`; **E402 MUST STAY** — the sys.path shim at ~58-61 needs it).
3. **REFACTOR** — none beyond the fix.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A regression test asserts the public surface (every `__all__` name resolves; `_accumulate` NOT exported) and passes after the fix.
- [ ] `__all__` lists EXACTLY the 14 names above; `_accumulate` excluded.
- [ ] Line-63 suppression narrowed to `# noqa: E402` (F401 removed, E402 retained).
- [ ] No regression: the validate_watchlist package tests stay green (vw.* surface unchanged).
- [ ] `uv run --with 'ruff==0.15.17' ruff check plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/validate_watchlist.py` reports NO F401 and NO F822 (every `__all__` name resolves to a real attribute).
- [ ] After the next CodeQL run, the `py/unused-import` alert no longer opens (verify post-merge; mark DEFERRED if not yet re-run).

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest \
  pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/test_validate_watchlist.py -q
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest \
  pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests -q
uv run --with 'ruff==0.15.17' ruff check plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/validate_watchlist.py
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

- [ ] Touched package tests pass · ruff reports no F401/F822 · `validate_manifest.py` green · `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] Policy 12 — a non-zero gate keeps the bug `in_progress`

## Severity / Priority

- Severity: **low** (false positive, no runtime impact).
- Priority: **P1** (blocks the green-tab goal — `wh-6ci`).

## Rollback

`git revert` the fix hunk (the `__all__` block + the `noqa` narrowing + the test). No data/migration.

## Risks / Dependencies

- Risk: an `__all__` name that does not resolve trips ruff F822 — the AC pins F822-clean to catch a typo.
- Dependency: file-disjoint from T1 (`.github/`+`docker/`) and T3 (no repo files) — parallel-safe.

## Notes / References

- `validate_watchlist.py:58-63` (the sys.path shim + the re-export import) — read on the live tree at design.
- `test_validate_watchlist.py:375-401` (the `validate_watchlist.load_malware_db`/`.is_known_bad` usage).
close_reason: CodeQL py/unused-import FP cleared in cc8fae1: __all__ added at validate_watchlist.py:67 (re-export surface declared). Regression test test_validate_watchlist.py:830+ pins __all__ both ways (Policy 9: ==14, load_malware_db/is_known_bad in, _accumulate out) - 69 passed 2026-06-13.

title: Pin CI + Docker deps to SHA/digest and add ADR-006 accept-notes
description:
## Goal / Problem

Hash-pin the FIX-classified Scorecard Pinned-Dependencies and add inline ADR-006 rationale to the ACCEPT-classified ones, closing **3 Pinned-Dependencies alerts via real SHA/digest pins** (the 2 uv installs in ci.yml + the Docker base image in docker/deps-scan-sandbox/Dockerfile). The 4 ACCEPT sites (npm/pip) carry inline ADR-006 rationale but do **NOT** auto-close — their durable dismissal is owned by `wh-6ci.3`. No behavior change beyond the pin mechanism.

## Background / Context

- ADR-006 (`docs/ARD.md`): pin and verify whatever IS used; a hash-lock is required where proportionate, an exact-version pin + rationale is acceptable where a hash-lock is disproportionate.
- Scorecard's Pinned-Dependencies check opens an alert per unpinned dependency in CI/Docker.
- **Scorecard does NOT honor inline rationale comments** — an ACCEPT site keeps re-opening every weekly SARIF re-upload unless it is durably DISMISSED (owned by `wh-6ci.3`); the inline note is for human/ADR-006 traceability, not alert closure.
- Verified at design (`file:line` confirmed on the live tree): ci.yml:29-32 + 63-66 (two `Install uv` run-steps), security-review.yml:60/61/94, docker/deps-scan-sandbox/Dockerfile:16/19.
- **Out of scope:** `docs/research/poc-iac-scan/` fixtures (DISMISS-class — owned by the sibling ticket). Do NOT touch them.

## Workflow

This ticket touches only `.github/` workflows + a `docker/` Dockerfile — **no Python package with tests**, so the per-package pytest gate does NOT apply (see Quality Gates). Changes are additive config edits; verify by grep + the manifest/plugin gates.

## Steps (with description)

**FIX (3 hash/digest pins):**
1. `ci.yml` — BOTH `Install uv (pinned 0.11.2)` steps (test job ~29-32 AND lint job ~63-66): replace the `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/0.11.2/install.sh | sh` + `echo "$HOME/.local/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"` run-step with `uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@fac544c07dec837d0ccb6301d7b5580bf5edae39 # v8.2.0 (SHA RE-VERIFY at launch)` and `with: { version: "0.11.2" }`. The action adds uv to PATH, so the GITHUB_PATH echo drops out.
2. `docker/deps-scan-sandbox/Dockerfile:16` — `FROM python:3.13-slim` -> `FROM python:3.13-slim@sha256:f82c96458eedc847b233e582eb31336f4954b39cae020b6dcf5b3ed0e5cbcd74` (digest RE-VERIFY at launch; fulfills the file's own ADR-006 header).

**ACCEPT (4 inline ADR-006 notes, NO behavior change — these notes do NOT close the alerts; durable dismissal is `wh-6ci.3`):**
3. `security-review.yml:60` (npm `claude-code@2.1.167`) — add note: exact+verified pin; hash-lock disproportionate for a single global install.
4. `security-review.yml:61` (`jsonschema>=4.20`) — add note: version-constrained per ADR-006.
5. `security-review.yml:94` (the three CURRENTLY-UNPINNED `pip install pytest jsonschema pyyaml`) — add exact `==` version pins for pytest/jsonschema/pyyaml + an ADR-006 note.
6. `docker/deps-scan-sandbox/Dockerfile:19` (`pytest~=8.3 jsonschema~=4.23`) — already `~=` compatible-release pinned; keep + add a one-line ADR-006 note.

> **RE-VERIFY at launch (Policy 7 — source wins):** the setup-uv v8.2.0 SHA and the python:3.13-slim digest above are design-time values. Confirm against the live upstream (`gh api` / registry) before committing; if upstream moved, use the current verified value and note it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv` remains in `ci.yml` (`grep -c` == 0).
- [ ] `docker/deps-scan-sandbox/Dockerfile:16` is digest-pinned (`@sha256:`).
- [ ] `security-review.yml:94` deps carry exact `==` pins (pytest/jsonschema/pyyaml).
- [ ] The 3 ACCEPT sites (security-review.yml:60, :61, Dockerfile:19) each carry an inline ADR-006 note.
- [ ] After the next weekly Scorecard run, the **3 PIN alerts** (2 uv installs + the Docker base image) no longer open; the **4 ACCEPT alerts** (npm/pip) are durably dismissed by `wh-6ci.3` — an inline code note does NOT clear a Scorecard Pinned-Dependencies finding (verify post-merge; mark DEFERRED if not yet re-run).

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

This ticket touches only `.github/` + `docker/` — **NO pytest gate applies** (no Python package with tests is touched).

```bash
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

- [ ] `validate_manifest.py` green
- [ ] `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] (pytest) N/A — no touched package has tests (config-only change)

## QA Before Push

- [ ] The two `ci.yml` jobs still resolve uv to PATH via the action (read the action's PATH behavior; don't assume).
- [ ] `security-review.yml` still installs the same effective versions (pin tightens, behavior unchanged).
- [ ] No accidental edit to `poc-iac-scan/` fixtures.

## Risks / Dependencies

- Risk: a stale SHA/digest pins to the wrong artifact — RE-VERIFY at launch (gate above).
- Dependency: file-disjoint from the sibling tickets (T2 = validate_watchlist.py; T3 = no repo files) — parallel-safe, no co-wave file collision.

## Rollback

`git revert` the workflow/Dockerfile hunks — additive, no migration.
notes: RATIFIED 2026-06-13 (operator decision): AC3 reinterpreted as a COMMENT-ONLY ACCEPT, not exact version pins. security-review.yml:94 pip gate deps (pytest jsonschema pyyaml) carry an inline ADR-006 ACCEPT note only; a full hash/version pin is disproportionate for CI-only transient gate deps (ADR-006 proportionality). The inline note is traceability, NOT alert-closing; durable Scorecard dismissal is owned by wh-6ci.3 (operator-executed). VERIFIED met 2026-06-13: AC1 (no astral install curl in ci.yml; setup-uv at pinned SHA both jobs), AC2 (Dockerfile:16 digest-pinned), AC3 (the :94 ADR-006 ACCEPT note present), AC4 (ACCEPT notes at sr.yml:60/:61/:94 plus Dockerfile compatible-release pins and the :12 header). Gates green: validate_manifest OK, claude plugin validate passed (config-only, no pytest gate). AC5 (post-Scorecard-rerun alert closure plus ACCEPT dismissals) DEFERRED to wh-6ci.3, not blocking .1. NOTE: a full AC-checkbox rewrite via --description was blocked by the confine_self_writes heuristic Bash parser (the body contains redirect-like literals) - a hook false-positive on bd-metadata writes; ratification recorded here and in the close reason instead.
close_reason: CI/Docker SHA-digest pins shipped in cc8fae1 (verified live 2026-06-13). AC3 ratified by operator as a comment-only ADR-006 ACCEPT (CI-only transient deps; full pin disproportionate) - see notes. Gates green (validate_manifest, claude plugin validate; config-only, no pytest gate). AC5 (Scorecard alert closure + ACCEPT dismissals) DEFERRED to wh-6ci.3 (operator-executed).

title: Take the GitHub code-scanning tab GREEN (Scorecard + CodeQL)
description:
## Goal / Problem

Resolve all 16 open GitHub code-scanning findings so **Security > Code scanning shows no open alerts**:
- 9 Scorecard **Pinned-Dependencies** (7 in production config; 2 deliberately-misconfigured PoC fixtures)
- 6 Scorecard **posture** checks (BranchProtection, CodeReview, Fuzzing, CII-Best-Practices, Vulnerabilities, Maintained)
- 1 CodeQL **py/unused-import** false positive

The design is adversarially-verified: every finding was Read-verified against the live repo (verdict SOUND, all 16 covered, no mis-resolutions). Three resolution classes split cleanly along disjoint file ownership.

## Success Metrics

- Security > Code scanning shows **no open alerts** after the next weekly Scorecard re-upload + next CodeQL run.
- Of the 7 production-config Pinned-Dependencies alerts: **3 close via SHA/digest pins** (the 2 uv installs + the Docker base image); the **4 ACCEPT sites carry inline ADR-006 notes AND are durably dismissed via `wh-6ci.3`** (Scorecard does not honor an inline rationale comment, so a note alone does NOT close a Pinned-Dependencies finding).
- The CodeQL py/unused-import alert no longer opens (the re-export is in `__all__`).
- The 5 Scorecard-posture + 2 PoC-fixture alerts carry durable operator dismissals (survive the Monday SARIF re-upload); MaintainedID flip-date recorded.

## Scope

**In scope**
- Hash/digest-pin the FIX-class Pinned-Dependencies; inline ADR-006 accept-notes for ACCEPT-class.
- One-line `__all__` fix clearing the CodeQL py/unused-import FP.
- Durable operator dismiss-with-rationale for the 5 posture checks + 2 PoC-fixture pins + the 4 ACCEPT Pinned-Dependencies sites (one posture check — MaintainedID — auto-resolves on repo age).

**Out of scope**
- Editing the deliberately-misconfigured IaC-scan PoC fixtures (`docs/research/poc-iac-scan/`) — DISMISS, not fix.
- Editing `scorecard.yml` / disabling the Scorecard SARIF upload.
- Any product/detector behavior change.

## Phases / Milestones

1. Wave 1 (this epic) — three parallel-safe tickets, disjoint file ownership.

## Child Tasks

- `wh-6ci.1` (task, P1) — Pin CI + Docker deps to SHA/digest + ADR-006 accept-notes.
- `wh-6ci.2` (bug, P1) — Clear CodeQL py/unused-import FP on validate_watchlist re-exports.
- `wh-6ci.3` (task, P1) — Durable operator dismissals for Scorecard-posture + fixture-vuln + ACCEPT Pinned-Dependencies alerts.

## Execution Waves (MANDATORY)

All three children are parallel-safe: **no intra-wave dependency** (all LAYER 0 in `bd graph wh-6ci`) AND **disjoint File Ownership** — `wh-6ci.1` owns `.github/workflows/{ci,security-review}.yml` + `docker/deps-scan-sandbox/Dockerfile`; `wh-6ci.2` owns `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/validate_watchlist.py` (+ its test); `wh-6ci.3` owns **no repo files** (operator dismissals + a gitignored `.notes/` record). No two tickets touch a shared file → one wave.

> **Wave 1 — start now (no blockers · 3 parallel tracks):**
> - `wh-6ci.1` — Pin CI + Docker deps to SHA/digest + ADR-006 accept-notes *(code/config)*
> - `wh-6ci.2` — Clear CodeQL py/unused-import FP via `__all__` *(code)*
> - `wh-6ci.3` — Durable operator dismissals for posture + fixture + ACCEPT alerts *(operator-executed)*

Launch with `/launch-team wh-6ci.1 wh-6ci.2 wh-6ci.3`; end with `/handoff wave-1-wh-6ci`.

## Dependencies

- None intra-wave. `wh-6ci.3` is OPERATOR-EXECUTED (UI/gh dismissals + optional PAT) — an agent can prepare exact commands/rationales but cannot complete it.

## Risks / Unknowns

- SHA/digest values (setup-uv v8.2.0 SHA; python:3.13-slim digest) MUST be RE-VERIFIED at launch (upstream may have moved).
- Scorecard re-uploads weekly (Monday) — dismissals must use stable fingerprints (ruleID+location) to survive re-upload.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **Execution Waves defined** — all 3 children in exactly one wave; parallel-safe (no intra-wave dep, disjoint File Ownership); launchable via `/launch-team wh-6ci.1 wh-6ci.2 wh-6ci.3`.

title: Build ide-hygiene skill: ext_scan.py (extension pin/verify + activation-code grep)
description:
## Goal
Implement the spec'd-but-UNBUILT ide-hygiene capability port (ext_scan.py): enumerate installed extensions via the editor CLI, fall back to on-disk ~/.vscode/extensions/*/package.json, pin/verify publisher+version against the watchlist `extension` block (watchlist-entry-schema.json:57-70, target enum already includes 'extension'), degrade-clean (record ide-hygiene in summary.tools_unavailable when no editor present). Add an activation-code grep tier the spec omits: flag environment-cred probes + second-stage fetch-and-exec on activate.

## Context
Highest-value SURFACE gap: the watchlist `extension` block + target:extension enum exist and are LOADER-IGNORED, but no consuming module exists (no ext_scan.py / ide-hygiene anywhere). TTP: TeamPCP/Carnage trojanized OpenVSX/VS Code extensions (Nx-Console-class).
PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE: the Nx-Console GHSA/CVE id + affected version set come from the primary advisory before populating watchlist rows; verify on-disk extensions layout vs official VS Code/OpenVSX docs; confirm activation-probe API names vs a primary sample, not the community rule text.

## Files (File Ownership — NEW skill, disjoint)
- CREATE plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/SKILL.md
- CREATE plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/{ext_scan.py,pyproject.toml,conftest.py,tests/test_ext_scan.py}
- READ-ONLY: _shared/reference/watchlist-entry-schema.json (extension block)

## Workflow
RED: no-editor fixture -> degrades clean (tools_unavailable lists ide-hygiene, no raise); a watchlist extension row match -> finding; an activation-probe fixture -> candidate. GREEN. REFACTOR.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests -q → PASS
- [ ] uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .  → OK ; claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker → valid
- [ ] degrades clean (never blocks) with no editor; ADR-005 caps hold (SKILL.md <500 lines)

## Rollback
Delete the new skill directory; nothing else references it.

## References

notes: GROOM 2026-06-11: READY. New skill is auto-discovered (plugin.json carries no skill list — no marketplace/manifest edit). title: Deterministic IDE/agent config-persistence rule (tasks.json folderOpen / .claude SessionStart)
description:
## Goal
Deterministic **ide-hygiene** config-file floor rule (`config_persist_scan.py`): parse `.vscode/tasks.json`
for tasks with `runOptions.runOn == "folderOpen"` (or auto-run presentation) that exec a script, AND
`.claude/settings*.json` for a `SessionStart` hook running a command — fire high-confidence when either
references a dropper target (`setup.mjs`/`config.mjs`/`ai_init.js`). Emit a `tool_assisted:false`,
signal-not-block finding that `ai-llm-review` §9 triages. Makes §9 enforceable on the deterministic FLOOR
(not LLM-only) and seeds the ide-hygiene config-file arm.

## Background / Context
- `ai-llm-review` §9 (`_shared/reference/ai-llm.md` §9; `ai-llm-review/SKILL.md:42`) enumerates these
  paths but is model-judgment only. This is the deterministic floor ARM; it does NOT fix the `ai_pass`
  gating of §9 (separate known gap). KB: `AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003`.
- HOME = ide-hygiene (corrected by the 2026-06-11 groom from the original sec-detect home: sec-detect is
  detection-only; ai-llm-review is SKILL.md-only with no `scripts/`). The ide-hygiene package + `ext_scan.py`
  exist (wh-5ox.4, CLOSED); package tests green (15 passed baseline).
- PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE (DO-NOT-COPY, wh-5ox): verify `runOn:folderOpen` + `SessionStart` semantics against
  the OFFICIAL VS Code `tasks.json` + Claude Code settings docs (NOT community YARA); confirm the
  `setup.mjs`/`config.mjs`/`ai_init.js` drop-target filenames against a PRIMARY IOC source before
  hardcoding the filename gate. Cite the source in the predicate.

## Prerequisites
- wh-5ox.4 CLOSED ✓ — ide-hygiene skill + package shape (`scripts/{pyproject.toml,conftest.py,tests/}`) +
  `ext_scan.py` on disk.

## Steps (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — in `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests/test_config_persist_scan.py`: a
   malicious `.vscode/tasks.json` (`runOn:folderOpen` → `setup.mjs`) AND a `.claude/settings.json`
   `SessionStart`-hook fixture TRIP (high-confidence finding, `== flagged`); a benign auto-task with no
   dropper ref does NOT (`!= flagged`); malformed JSON degrades clean (no raise, no finding). Run the
   ide-hygiene gate — FAILS (module absent).
2. **GREEN** — create `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/config_persist_scan.py`: parse the
   two config families, apply the structural predicates, emit a `tool_assisted:false` finding (repo-relative
   `file`, schema-valid). Reuse `ext_scan.py`'s degrade pattern (same package); MIRROR the
   repo-relative / realpath-confine pattern from `deps-scan/scripts/ioc_scan.py` (read as a reference — do
   NOT cross-import across packages). Re-run — PASS.
3. **REFACTOR** — surgical; add the 1-line xref in `ai-llm-review/SKILL.md` pointing §9 at the floor rule.

## Files to Modify/Create
- CREATE `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/config_persist_scan.py`
- CREATE `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests/test_config_persist_scan.py`
- MODIFY `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-llm-review/SKILL.md` (1-line xref to the floor rule)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `folderOpen` + dropper-ref → high-confidence candidate (`== flagged`); benign auto-task → none
      (`!= flagged`); a `.claude` `SessionStart` command referencing a dropper trips; malformed JSON
      degrades clean (no raise).
- [ ] Findings are `tool_assisted:false`, carry a repo-relative `file`, and validate against
      `_shared/reference/finding-schema.json`.
- [ ] Structural predicates cite the OFFICIAL config docs (`tasks.json` `runOn` + Claude Code
      `SessionStart`) and a PRIMARY IOC source for the dropper filenames (DO-NOT-COPY).
- [ ] No regression: existing ide-hygiene suite stays green.

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests -q` green
- [ ] `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` green
- [ ] `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker` green
- [ ] Policy 12 — a non-zero gate keeps the task in_progress.

## Rollback
Delete `config_persist_scan.py` + its test, and revert the `ai-llm-review/SKILL.md` xref line. No
data/schema/migration to undo.

## Risks / Dependencies
- Touches ide-hygiene (2 files) + a 1-line `ai-llm-review/SKILL.md` xref — the second "skill" is a doc
  one-liner, not a code package; keep together (not a split).
- SERIAL vs `wh-5ox.13` / `wh-5ox.14` (both also touch the ide-hygiene package — `ext_scan`/`ioc_scan`
  tests / activation-grep). File-disjoint here (`config_persist_scan.py` is new), but NEVER co-wave if
  their file sets overlap — verify at File Ownership mapping.

## References
EPIC wh-5ox; `_shared/reference/ai-llm.md` §9 (`ai-llm-review/SKILL.md:42`); KB `AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003`;
reuse: `ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py` (degrade), `deps-scan/scripts/ioc_scan.py` (repo-rel/realpath
pattern). Depends on wh-5ox.4 (CLOSED).
notes: GROOMED 2026-06-12 (re-groom pre-launch, next wave). VERDICT: READY. APPLIED the 2026-06-11 home correction to the BODY — it still said CREATE under sec-detect/ + the AC gate pointed at sec-detect; rewrote to the task template with HOME=ide-hygiene (config_persist_scan.py + test in ide-hygiene/scripts/; gate now ide-hygiene pkg). VERIFIED vs live tree: ide-hygiene skill+package exist (wh-5ox.4 CLOSED) — SKILL.md + scripts/{ext_scan.py,conftest.py,pyproject.toml,tests/}; config_persist_scan.py ABSENT (correct, this creates it); 15 ide-hygiene tests green baseline. ai-llm-review/SKILL.md:42 references tasks.json/§9/AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003 — xref MODIFY target valid. ioc_scan.py is in deps-scan (NOT ide-hygiene) — reuse as a PATTERN reference (repo-rel/realpath-confine), do NOT cross-import; ext_scan.py degrade is the same-package reuse. wh-5ox.4 dep CLOSED -> UNBLOCKED. Scope OK (ide-hygiene 2 files + 1-line ai-llm-review/SKILL.md xref = doc one-liner, not a split). SERIAL vs wh-5ox.13/.14 on the ide-hygiene package — file-disjoint here but verify at File Ownership.

DROPPER_TARGETS={setup.mjs,setup.js} per primary sources (KB AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003 / Socket+StepSecurity); ticket-body config.mjs/ai_init.js DROPPED (no primary source, DO-NOT-COPY). +1-line ai-llm-review SKILL.md xref. 34 ide-hygiene tests green incl. RecursionError-depth regression (DEFECT-1 fixed). QA PASS + white-hacker CLEAR; gates pytest+manifest+plugin-validate green. title: S6: bun-runtime dropper markers in _DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS [supply_chain.py lane]
description:
## Goal
Add a STRUCTURAL bun-runtime-dropper detector to `supply_chain.py` `signal_s6`: a clean-looking install script that fetches the bun runtime + unzips it by magic-bytes (NO same-line `eval`/`exec`) should still trip S6's >=2-pattern HIGH. The durable detection is the **behavioral SHAPE** (fetch-runtime + manual-unzip), NOT campaign-specific filenames.

## Background / DO-NOT-COPY (binding — epic wh-5ox)
- **The DURABLE spine = STRUCTURAL/generic markers** (no DO-NOT-COPY issue — these are NOT lifted attacker literals, they describe how any bun-fetch-dropper works): `BUN_INSTALL_DIR` (bun's standard env var) · ZIP-magic `PK\x03\x04` manual-unzip handling · a fetch from the official bun release host (`github.com/oven-sh/bun/releases/download`) · bun binary name `bun-(linux|darwin)-(x64|aarch64)(-musl-baseline)?`. Implement THESE; weight so the fetch+unzip shape reaches the >=2-pattern HIGH (essentially absent in legit deps).
- **Campaign-specific entry-script names** (`ai_init.js`, `router_runtime.js`) are **`[example-unverified]`** — illustrative context ONLY, easily renamed. **Do NOT hardcode them as detection literals** unless re-derived from a PRIMARY advisory/artifact and tagged `[primary-sourced: <url>]`. The structural spine carries the detection; a renamed script still trips it.
- `/tmp/.bun_ran` is `[primary-sourced: ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain-3.md:18]` but it is a RUNTIME / post-install IOC — OUT OF SCOPE for the install-script `_DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS` (which match install-script source). Not added here.

## Files to Modify (File Ownership — SERIAL on supply_chain.py; ONE writer at a time)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py` — `_DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS` (`:62`) + `signal_s6` (`:1223`): add the structural bun-dropper patterns + weighting.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_supply_chain.py` — fixtures + both-ways assertions.

## Steps (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — failing tests in `test_supply_chain.py`: a fetch-bun-runtime + unzip-by-magic install-script fixture trips S6 HIGH (>=2 structural patterns); a legit `node-gyp` postinstall does NOT (both ways, Policy 9).
2. **GREEN** — add the structural patterns to `_DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS` (bun-release-host fetch · ZIP-magic manual-unzip · `BUN_INSTALL_DIR` · bun binary name) and wire weighting in `signal_s6` so the shape reaches HIGH. NO `[example-unverified]` campaign filenames hardcoded.
3. **REFACTOR** — both-ways pins; additive only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] malicious fetch+unzip fixture = S6 HIGH; benign `node-gyp` postinstall = no S6 finding (both directions, Policy 9).
- [ ] Detection is STRUCTURAL — NO `[example-unverified]` campaign filename (`ai_init.js`/`router_runtime.js`) hardcoded as a literal (renamed-script-resilient).
- [ ] `tool_assisted:false`; degrade-never-raise (never blocks; ADR-003).
- [ ] `malware_db.py` loader UNTOUCHED (signal-only change).

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q`
- [ ] `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .`
- [ ] `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker`

## Rollback
Revert the `_DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS` additions + the `signal_s6` weighting + the test fixtures. Additive only — no migration to unwind.

## References
EPIC wh-5ox (DO-NOT-COPY: re-derive from a PRIMARY source, not the unlicensed community YARA). `ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain-3.md` (Hades/Shai-Hulud bun-runtime campaign; `/tmp/.bun_ran` IOC). SERIAL lane on `supply_chain.py` (Lane D: wh-8ci/wh-ln2/wh-0c2/wh-5ox.9 landed + pushed).

## Commit Message Format
`feat(deps-scan): structural bun-runtime-dropper detection in signal_s6 (wh-5ox.2)`
notes: RE-GROOMED (wave) 2026-06-13 — VERDICT READY. Re-verified vs live tree: _DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS at supply_chain.py:62 and signal_s6 at :1223 EXACT (file touched by ec58db6 CodeQL fix since the first groom, but the symbols and the 212 baseline are unchanged); bun markers absent (work undone); deps-scan baseline 212 collected; serial lane CLEAR (nothing in_progress). NEW LANE SIBLING: wh-0uy (the F-001 _read_toml RecursionError fix) is now open on supply_chain.py, so wh-5ox.2 and wh-0uy CANNOT co-wave (both edit the file). SEQUENCE priority-first: wh-5ox.2 (P1) lands FIRST, then wh-0uy (P2) in the next lane slot (a fix-first ordering is also fine since wh-0uy is a one-line fix; operator call). wh-5ox.2 adds REGEX patterns to _DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS (NOT TOML parsing), so F-001/wh-0uy does not affect its code; new code must still respect ADR-003 degrade-never-raise. Co-waves file-disjoint with wh-sml (docs/research). DO-NOT-COPY resolved (structural spine only).
close_reason: S6 structural bun-runtime-dropper detection landed. Four additive structural markers in _DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS (oven-sh release host, BUN_INSTALL env var, bun-os-arch asset name, ZIP PK magic). signal_s6 and the two-or-more-pattern HIGH threshold unchanged (purely additive). TDD both-ways pins plus a degrade-never-raise test. Gates green: 215 pytest, manifest valid, plugin-validate passed. Dogfood CLEAN, QA 4-tier PASS. O-1 (asset regex missing a leading word-boundary) is informational and non-blocking, optional future hardening.

title: Inert campaign-IOC grep pack (deterministic floor scan of a target tree)
description:
## Goal
Deterministic floor scan of a reviewed tree for FIXED campaign literals (token-wipe beacon string, commit markers 'thebeautifulmarchoftime'/'A Gift From TeamPCP', double-exec lock filename, ICP-canister C2 host, exfil-staging shape results-<13digit>-<counter>.json[.pN]). Emit tool_assisted:false human-triaged candidates on EXACT match; never block.

## Context / source
Mined from Carnage(CVE-2026-45321)+TeamPCP(CVE-2026-33634) defender repos. Today these literals exist only as KB prose (supply-chain-3.md:18,20) for the model to recall — NO executable detector. Store literals in the DATA/value plane (Gate-2 SEC-Q5) so a feed string can never become an agent instruction.
PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE: re-derive every literal from a primary source (CVE/GHSA, vendor advisory, the actual artifact) — NOT the unlicensed community YARA. Literals not primary-confirmable = low-durability; caveat, don't drop.

## Files to Create/Modify (File Ownership)

- CREATE plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/reference/campaign-iocs.json (DATA plane)
- CREATE plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests/test_ioc_scan.py
- (1-line index entry in sec-detect/SKILL.md — coordination point w/ T3/T7)

## Workflow (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR)
RED: fixture tree with a planted beacon literal -> expect a HIGH candidate; benign tree -> none. GREEN: stdlib grep of tree vs campaign-iocs.json (never raises). REFACTOR.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests/test_ioc_scan.py -q  → PASS
- [ ] exact match emits a finding-schema candidate with a REPO-RELATIVE file path; benign tree = 0 hits; missing data file degrades clean (no raise)
- [ ] every shipped literal cites a primary source in campaign-iocs.json (or is marked low_durability:true)

## Rollback
Delete the new module+data+tests + the SKILL.md index line. No existing behavior changed.

## References

title: EPIC: defender-repo detection learnings — floor IOC/signature detectors + schema spine + ide-hygiene (Carnage/TeamPCP)
description:
## Goal / Problem
Convert verified learnings from two community supply-chain DEFENDER repos — Shai-Hulud "Carnage" (CVE-2026-45321) and the TeamPCP cascade (CVE-2026-33634) — into white-hacker detection coverage. We hold the KNOWLEDGE (KB prose, ai-llm.md model-judgment) but lack the cheap DETERMINISTIC FLOOR; these tickets turn that into near-zero-FP signals + the schema spine + the unbuilt ide-hygiene capability.

## DO-NOT-COPY posture (binding on every child)
Community YARA/IOC sets are UNLICENSED and partly DISPUTED. Every child carries a PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE: re-derive each literal from a primary source (CVE/GHSA, vendor advisory, the actual artifact) — NOT the community YARA; keep DISPUTED claims (the Carnage deadman wiper) DISPUTED; named malicious packages become WATCHLIST rows (S8 catches a resolved version), not hard-coded greps. All fetched content untrusted (Rule of Two).

## Scope
**In:** deps-scan content/signal additions, the ide-hygiene skill (+ its config-file arm), additive schema fields, the sec-report coverage matrix, infra.md CI patterns, a static-vs-EDR scope-boundary doc + host-IR advisory.
**Out:** the two ALREADY-KNOWN gaps tracked elsewhere — currency auto-fire (RC1/RC2, wh-hxt.8) and the ai_pass gating of §9. Runtime/EDR/host telemetry (out of static scope — documented by wh-5ox.6).

## GROOMING (2026-06-11) — homes corrected
sec-detect is capability-detection only (emits SCAN-PLAN.json; read-only — SKILL.md:1-14); ai-llm-review/sec-vuln-scan are SKILL.md-only. Deterministic findings-emitting scanners live in skills WITH a scripts/ package (deps-scan, secrets-scan, sec-policy). Therefore: .1 -to deps-scan (not sec-detect); .3 -to the ide-hygiene config-file arm (blocked by .4); .7 reframed to infra.md patterns (standalone linter = follow-up needing a new iac-scan skill). See each ticket's GROOM note.

## Execution Waves
**STATUS 2026-06-13:** Wave 1 + 1b COMPLETE; now in Wave 2 (the serial supply_chain.py lane). wh-6ci (code-scanning-green) cleared first. NEXT launch = wh-5ox.2 (re-groomed READY), co-waved with the standalone wh-sml spike (file-disjoint). Progress: 9/19 children closed.

**Wave 1 — 4 file-disjoint parallel tracks — DONE (ALL CLOSED):**
- wh-5ox.4 CLOSED — ide-hygiene skill (NEW skill dir)
- wh-5ox.6 CLOSED — static-vs-EDR scope boundary + host-IR advisory (docs)
- wh-5ox.1 CLOSED — campaign-IOC grep pack (deps-scan/scripts/ioc_scan.py + reference/campaign-iocs.json)
- wh-5ox.7 CLOSED — Actions secrets-exfil/OIDC patterns (infra.md)

**Wave 1b — DONE (CLOSED):**
- wh-5ox.3 CLOSED — IDE/agent config-persistence rule (ide-hygiene/scripts/config_persist_scan.py)

**Wave 2 — SERIAL LANE on deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py (one writer at a time; merged with Lane D wh-8ci/wh-ln2/wh-0c2, all closed):**
  order (priority-first): wh-5ox.9 DONE, then **wh-5ox.2 (NEXT, P1, re-groomed READY 2026-06-13)**, then wh-5ox.10, then wh-5ox.5, then wh-5ox.8, then wh-5ox.11.
- .9 watchlist fields + s8 (SPINE) DONE · .2 S6 bun (NEXT — co-wave the standalone wh-sml hooks-firing spike, file-disjoint) · .10 finding att_ck/atlas + disputed FIELDS + matcher (unblocks wh-5ox.19) · .5 S6 crypto/publish · .8 deadman/env-gate · .11 forged-sigstore.
  NOTE: .2/.5/.11 all edit _DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS — may be ONE PR at launch. (.2-before-.10 = priority-first; .2 is P1, .10 is P2.)

**Wave 3 — after wh-5ox.10 (needs the att_ck/atlas finding field):**
- wh-5ox.19 — sec-report coverage_matrix.py (ATT&CK/ATLAS coverage table to SECURITY-REPORT.md; pure aggregation, BLOCKED BY wh-5ox.10). File-disjoint (sec-report/) — co-waves with any non-supply_chain.py track once .10 lands.

**Unscheduled follow-up children (backlog — not yet wave-placed):** wh-5ox.12 (populate campaign-iocs.json from PRIMARY sources — NOTE its parent wh-5ox.1 is now CLOSED, so this is data-fill for a shipped grep pack; re-confirm scope) · wh-5ox.13 (test backfill QA G2-G9 + LOW-2 prose clamp) · wh-5ox.14 (ide-hygiene activation-grep on the editor-CLI path) · wh-5ox.15 (iac-scan skill: deterministic workflow_lint.py). Place each in a wave when scheduled.

## Dependencies / Risks
- Relates to wh-k6l (watchlist file, CLOSED): named-package IOCs + ide-hygiene extension rows land as watchlist rows.
- wh-5ox.3 blocked by wh-5ox.4 (same skill, both CLOSED). supply_chain.py contention (6 children + Lane D) -to strictly serial.
- File-serialization enforced by wave disjointness + .notes/order.md (NOT bd deps — matches Lane D convention); the live bd-deps are .19 depends-on .10 (sec-report coverage matrix needs the att_ck/atlas finding field).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every child in exactly one wave; within-wave parallel-safe (disjoint File Ownership).
- [ ] All children closed with project gates green (per-package pytest + manifest/schema validators).
- [ ] Each detector child has TDD pinning both a malicious trip and a benign non-trip (Policy 9).

Lane order priority-first: .9 done, then .2 (P1), then .10 (unblocks .19), then .5, .8, .11; .2/.5/.11 all touch _DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS so may be one PR. Wave 3: wh-5ox.19 after .10. title: Harden agent to emit repo-relative paths (root-cause of the public-repo path leak)
description: The committed path leak (an absolute $HOME/... path in docs) traced to .claude/agents/white-hacker.md:187 ('All references to files are absolute paths') plus an under-specified finding-schema 'file' field. Fix: agent profiles + report/triage skills + finding-schema must specify REPO-RELATIVE paths only; add a public-repo no-personal-info policy to CLAUDE.md(s). Deterministic scripts already emit repo-relative (policy_detect, sec-init, sec-policy) — no code-logic change needed.
close_reason: Repo-relative path enforcement shipped: finding-schema.json rejects absolute/home paths (^[^/~]) + tests; both white-hacker profiles, sec-report, sec-triage, and both CLAUDE.md carry the public-repo policy. Gates green.

title: Spike: currency-arm mechanism — /schedule cloud routine vs maintainer-repo Actions-cron (claude -p) under the no-local-API-key constraint; emit a scheduling ADR
description:
## Problem statement
The self-improving CURRENCY arm is design-intent, not operational (RC1 of the Hades dogfood RCA,
docs/research/20260610_hades_shai_hulud_pypi.md §5). ARCHITECTURE.md:239-247 §3.6 specifies a **cloud
Scheduled Routine** (`/schedule`, "Anthropic infra, no local machine") to run `sec-kb-refresh`. But: (1)
no `/schedule` skill/command exists in-repo — it is an external Anthropic-platform primitive we do not own;
(2) NO ADR governs the routine or its auth (grep of docs/ARD.md is empty); (3) our standing constraint is
**no local ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — subscription only; a key/OAuth is CI-only** (memory qa-and-auth;
ci/security-review.action.yml). So the documented mechanism may not be provisionable for this operator, and
the currency arm has no verified way to run. This spike DECIDES the mechanism before any "wire it" task.

## Research questions (each -> a decision)
- RQ1 — Is `/schedule` (Anthropic Scheduled Routines) actually available/provisioned to this account, and
  what auth does it need? Does it run on the subscription or require a key/OAuth the operator hasn't set up?
- RQ2 — FALLBACK: a maintainer-repo GitHub Actions `schedule:` cron invoking `claude -p` (the headless path
  that ALREADY has a known auth story in ci/security-review.action.yml). Feeds need no auth (si-07:7-8), so
  fetch+extract+draft-PR is plausibly buildable TODAY this way. Verify: cost, the draft-PR-never-auto-merge
  invariant, confine_self_writes intact, secret scope (OIDC/token least-privilege).
- RQ3 — Which mechanism do we adopt, and does it need an ADR? (No scheduling ADR exists; the decision should
  produce one.) Reconcile with ARCHITECTURE §3.6 (amend if we pick Actions-cron over the cloud routine).
- RQ4 — Hand-off to the impl-task (strand c of wh-hxt.8): the cadence, the trigger wiring, and folding in
  wh-hxt.10's campaign-family re-poll trigger (a 'hot family -> re-poll the first-detector feeds' hook).

## Exit criteria
- A decision recorded: cloud `/schedule` vs Actions-cron `claude -p` (or hybrid), with the auth/feasibility
  evidence. An ADR proposal drafted (none exists for scheduling). The impl-task filed (blocked on this spike),
  carrying the re-poll trigger folded from wh-hxt.10(b). Even a "neither is feasible without operator X"
  outcome is a valid result — it names the blocker.

## Time box
~0.5-1 day (feasibility + decision; no production code). Depends on the operator answering RQ1 (account
provisioning) — flag if blocked on that.

## Likely outcome
Actions-cron `claude -p` in the maintainer repo is the pragmatic answer (known auth, buildable now, no new
platform dependency); the cloud routine is the cleaner long-term shape IF provisioned. Validate, don't assume.

## References
docs/research/20260610_hades_shai_hulud_pypi.md §5 RC1; ARCHITECTURE.md:239-247 §3.6/§3.7; sec-kb-refresh/
SKILL.md:4,40 + scripts/poll_feeds.py (PARSERS={osv,atlas,atom}); ci/security-review.action.yml (the headless
auth story); phase-8-self-improvement.md:107-109 (the capture-hook human gate = wh-hxt.8); si-07-threat-feeds.md.
Siblings: wh-hxt.8 (the human decision it splits from), wh-hxt.9 (the feeds this routine will poll), wh-hxt.10
(the family re-poll trigger to fold into the impl-task).
close_reason: Spike RESOLVED: recommend maintainer-repo Actions-cron (claude -p) NOW + cloud /schedule deferred. KEY: /schedule REQUIRES subscription login + is HIDDEN when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set — so our no-local-key posture ENABLES it (groom premise inverted). ADR-029 PROPOSAL drafted in docs/research/spike-11-currency-mechanism-2026-06.md (literal 0XX, NOT appended — operator ratifies the infra/CI decision + answers RQ1: is /schedule provisioned). Draft impl-spec for the wh-hxt.8(c) currency-arm workflow inside. WIF/OIDC auth, draft-PR-never-auto-merge, SHA-pinned — security CLEAR.

title: Detection class (RC4): ai-llm-review scans target AI-assistant config files for injected exec instructions (.claude/.cursor/.vscode/mcp/copilot/aider)
description:
**ROOT CAUSE RC4** (docs/research/20260610_hades_shai_hulud_pypi.md §5) — the highest-value durable upgrade, confirmed as the live Hades technique. We model what the model READS at runtime (prompts, tool descriptions=tool-poisoning.md, RAG) but NOT the on-disk agent-config that bootstraps execution. Hades poisons .claude/setup.mjs, .vscode/tasks.json/setup.mjs, .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, .cursor/rules/, .github/copilot-instructions.md, .aider.conf.yml, .github/setup.js.

Scope: an ai-llm-review detection (+ a _shared/reference/ai-llm.md checklist line + likely a KB entry/cross-ref) that flags, in a TARGET repo under review, AI-assistant config files containing imperative/exec instructions (bun run bootstrap, postinstall exec, hidden system-prompt-shaped directives). Distinct from tool-poisoning.md (MCP tool DESCRIPTIONS). Treat config content as untrusted data (the agent is itself an injection target — Rule of Two). Valuable regardless of the Hades campaign's longevity. Groom before claiming; verify the exact ai-llm-review extension points first. Refs: ai-llm-review/SKILL.md, _shared/reference/ai-llm.md, tool-poisoning.md, the Hades research doc.
notes:
GROOM 2026-06-10 (tech-lead, read-only) — VERDICT: NEEDS UPDATE (no split; ONE surface).
ai-llm-review is CHECKLIST/MODEL-driven — NO scripts/ package (ls = SKILL.md + empty reference/). Deliverable
= a NEW numbered section in _shared/reference/ai-llm.md (clone §4 MCP-tool-poisoning structure +
tool-poisoning.md:16-20 `detections:` style) + a partition line in ai-llm-review/SKILL.md:34-40. NOT a Python
detector; NO new pytest package; no test baseline to establish. RULE-5 SPLIT: 'is config text imperative/
exec-shaped?' = model JUDGMENT (checklist, correct home); path enumeration (.claude/setup.mjs, .vscode/
tasks.json|setup.mjs, .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, .cursor/rules/, .github/copilot-instructions.md,
.aider.conf.yml, .github/setup.js) = DETERMINISTIC grep/glob lines. MUST NOT mint a KB entry —
AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003 is wh-hxt.10's; CROSS-REF it (config-poisoning = the Hades persistence sub-case,
research §1/§5). technique_class enum is CLOSED (kb-entry-schema.json:22) -> a new `config-poisoning` class
would need its OWN ADR (out of scope here). POSTURE ANCHOR: white-hacker.md:37-41 (Rule of Two) + ADR-018
(target config = UNTRUSTED DATA; this is its defensive mirror) — NO new ADR. DISTINCT from TOOL-POISONING-001
(MCP descriptions) and PROMPT-INJECTION-001 (retrieved/runtime content). DEPENDS-ON wh-hxt.10 (the cross-ref
id must resolve, else validate_findings --check-kb-refs fails, SKILL.md:54-55; the checklist section can land
first). VERIFICATION (Policy 4/9): grep asserts the new ai-llm.md section + the enumerated paths;
lint_skill.py passes (frontmatter untouched); _shared/reference one level deep (ADR-005).
close_reason: Config-poisoning detection — ai-llm.md §9 + ai-llm-review SKILL.md partition line; cross-refs the shipped AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003; all 10 Hades config paths enumerated. Checklist-only (no code, no new KB entry/class). QA PASS, security CLEAR (declarative, no injection surface). lint OK; frontmatter caps intact.

title: Currency arm (RC1, HUMAN DECISION): operator registers the T-8.3 capture hooks in maintainer .claude/settings.json — unblocks the reflection arm
description:
## Goal / Problem

**OPERATOR ACTION REQUIRED** -- this ticket cannot be completed by an AI agent. The maintainer must manually register the T-8.3 capture hooks in their personal `.claude/settings.json` to activate the outer loop's trace-capture arm. Until this is done, `sec-learn` runs without its primary signal source (captured tool traces), degrading to sparse manual corrections only.

Root cause of the Hades miss (forcing function): the Shai-Hulud CHILD wave (Hades) was missed 1-2 days after AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 was tracked because nothing polls the feed. Capture-hook registration is the keystone (RC1) that unblocks the reflection arm and eventually (once wh-hxt.12 resolves the scheduling mechanism) the currency arm.

## Background / Context

- ARCHITECTURE.md:245-250 (verified 2026-06-11): capture scripts (`append_trace.sh`, `log_failed_exploit.sh`, `log_corrections.sh`, `save_learnings_nudge.sh`, `inject_cve_digest.sh`) are BUILT in `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/`. Registration is PENDING human-auth (T-8.3) by design.
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/hooks.json:35-46` (verified 2026-06-11): the SHIPPED plugin hooks register only `PreToolUse` (guardrails) and `SessionStart` (`sessionstart_project_facts.sh`). The capture events -- `PostToolUse`, `PostToolUseFailure`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `SessionEnd`/`Stop` -- are NOT wired in the plugin hooks (intentional: a plugin in others' repos must not auto-capture traces -- privacy gate).
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/capture_hooks.py:1-12` (verified 2026-06-11): 5 capture modes documented -- `trace` (PostToolUse), `failed_exploit` (PostToolUse/error), `correction` (UserPromptSubmit), `cve_digest` (SessionStart), `learnings_nudge` (Stop/SessionEnd).
- Registration must happen in the MAINTAINER's project-scope `settings.json` (`.claude/settings.json` in this repo), NOT in the plugin's `hooks.json` -- that is the design (phase-8-self-improvement.md:107/:123; confine_self_writes.py denies writes to `.claude/settings.json` from agents).
- GROOM NOTE (2026-06-10, tech-lead): wh-hxt.8 was split to strand (a) only -- the human-decision strand. Strand (b) the scheduling mechanism is in spike wh-hxt.12. Strand (c) the impl-task (wire the chosen mechanism) is a FUTURE ticket after wh-hxt.12 closes.

## Steps (the human operator actions)

1. Read `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/capture_hooks.py:1-12` to confirm all 5 capture modes and their hook-event mappings (PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, SessionEnd/Stop, SessionStart for cve_digest).
2. Open `.claude/settings.json` in this repo (the maintainer project-scope settings -- NOT the plugin hooks.json).
3. Add the 4 capture hook registrations:
   - `PostToolUse`: invoke `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/append_trace.sh` with mode=trace (non-blocking, exit 0).
   - `PostToolUse` (on error): invoke `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/log_failed_exploit.sh` (mode=failed_exploit).
   - `UserPromptSubmit`: invoke `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/log_corrections.sh` (mode=correction).
   - `SessionEnd`/`Stop`: invoke `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/save_learnings_nudge.sh` (mode=learnings_nudge).
4. The `inject_cve_digest.sh` (cve_digest / SessionStart) is already wired via a separate SessionStart registration if desired -- check current `.claude/settings.json` state first; do not duplicate.
5. Test: start a new Claude Code session in this repo, run `/security-review` on a small target, then check `evals/traces/` for a new `findings-YYYY-MM.jsonl` line (confirms PostToolUse capture is live).
6. Confirm `sec-learn` harvests at least one trace (`/sec-learn` invocation should no longer report 'no traces found').
7. Ratify an ADR entry in `docs/ARD.md` for the capture-hook registration decision (cadence, what events, privacy framing -- append-only per convention).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.claude/settings.json` contains `PostToolUse`, `UserPromptSubmit`, and `SessionEnd`/`Stop` capture hook registrations pointing to the correct `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/*.sh` scripts.
- [ ] A new Claude Code session in this repo produces at least one trace line in `evals/traces/findings-YYYY-MM.jsonl` after a tool call (PostToolUse capture confirmed live).
- [ ] `/sec-learn` no longer reports 'no traces found' (at least one harvested trace).
- [ ] No duplicate SessionStart registration for `inject_cve_digest.sh` (check existing settings.json before adding).
- [ ] An ADR entry for the capture-hook registration is appended to `docs/ARD.md` (append-only; never supersedes prior ADRs).

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

N/A -- operator action, verified by inspecting `.claude/settings.json` and running the manual smoke above. No pytest gate applies to this ticket.

- [ ] DEFERRED -- no pytest gate (operator action; verification is manual settings.json inspection + session smoke)

## Rollback

Remove the 4 capture hook entries from `.claude/settings.json`. The plugin `hooks.json` is unchanged (never touched here). `evals/traces/` can be cleaned up if desired (JSONL files are local).

## Notes / References

- ARCHITECTURE.md:245-250 (verified 2026-06-11): 'Capture scripts BUILT, registration pending human-auth (T-8.3)'.
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/hooks.json:35-46` (verified 2026-06-11): PreToolUse + SessionStart only in shipped plugin -- no capture events.
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/capture_hooks.py:1-12` (verified 2026-06-11): 5 modes documented.
- Phase-8-self-improvement design constraint: capture is opt-in for the maintainer; capture-hook registration in others' repos is EXPLICITLY NOT shipped in the plugin (privacy).
- Strand (b) scheduling mechanism: spike wh-hxt.12 (open). Strand (c) impl-task: future ticket, blocked on wh-hxt.12.
- `confine_self_writes.py` denies agent writes to `.claude/settings.json` -- this is WHY the operator must do this manually.
notes:
GROOM 2026-06-10 (tech-lead, read-only) — VERDICT: NEEDS SPLIT / REFRAME — NOT claimable as a dev task.
Bundles 3 strands, 3 resolution paths, no shared DoD:
(a) HUMAN DECISION (this ticket reframes TO this) — register the T-8.3 capture hooks in the MAINTAINER's
    .claude/settings.json. An agent CANNOT self-resolve (confine_self_writes denies .claude/settings.json;
    phase-8-self-improvement.md:107/:123). Capture is opt-in BY DESIGN, intentionally NOT in the shipped
    plugin hooks.json (phase-8:109 — privacy: a plugin in others' repos must not auto-capture traces).
    This is the standing order.md:205 'Open HUMAN decision'. DoD: the 4 events present in committed
    maintainer settings + inject_cve_digest on SessionStart; sec-learn harvest finds >=1 trace (no longer DRY).
(b) SPIKE → wh-hxt.12 (created). The /schedule cloud-routine mechanism is UNRESOLVED + likely infeasible
    as written: no /schedule skill in-repo (external Anthropic primitive), NO ADR governs it, our constraint
    is NO local ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (subscription-only; key/OAuth is CI-only, memory qa-and-auth). Feeds need
    no auth (si-07:7-8) — the blocker is /schedule provisioning. Decide cloud-routine vs maintainer-repo
    GitHub Actions schedule: cron invoking `claude -p` (KNOWN auth story, ci/security-review.action.yml).
(c) impl-task — wire the chosen mechanism (cadence, draft-PR-never-auto-merge, confine intact) + fold in
    wh-hxt.10's family re-poll trigger. File AFTER the wh-hxt.12 spike decides the mechanism.
VERIFIED: capture_hooks.py:1-9 (5 modes exist); hooks.json has ONLY PreToolUse(:3)+SessionStart(:30, runs
sessionstart_project_facts NOT inject_cve_digest) — no PostToolUse/SessionEnd/Stop; SKILL.md:4
disable-model-invocation:true; ARCHITECTURE.md:24/:144/:324/:349 'registration pending human-auth T-8.3';
no ADR for scheduling (grep ARD empty). Body DoD 'schedule and/or resolve' is not runnable (Policy 4).
=> wh-hxt.8 is now scoped to strand (a) the HUMAN DECISION. Operator action required before it can close.

title: Registry rewrite — ADR-025 columns + ADR-027 replacement rows + SCANNER_PREFERENCE Trivy removal + lock-regex retirement (wh-xn0 ∪ wh-nvk)
description:
## Goal / Problem

`tool-registry.md` and `detect_tools.py::SCANNER_PREFERENCE` do not yet reflect ADR-025 (ADMIT) and ADR-027 (DIVERSIFY): the registry has NO license/data_egress/gdpr columns and seeds License-gate violators as defaults — Opengrep (`tool-registry.md:24`), govulncheck (`:29`), trufflehog (`:34`), hadolint (`:38`); Trivy is still in active selection (`detect_tools.py:114` sca, `:116` iac; registry `:30,:38,:50-57`) despite the PERMANENT removal (ADR-027 §1); and the doc↔code lock pins a Trivy-specific regex (`test_registry_lock.py:51` `r"0\.7[01]"`) that trips RED when the pin line is deleted.

ONE coordinated change (never two writers — ADR-025 §5 / ADR-027 §5): add the three columns, install the replacement rows with per-tool pin+verify guidance, move violators to TWO distinct Rejected subsections, drop Trivy from SCANNER_PREFERENCE, delete the pin block, retire the lock regex while keeping the lock GREEN + extending it with per-tool present/absent TDD.

**Hard scope boundary:** installs the SAST registry CONTENT but MUST NOT flip any SAST default in detect_tools.py — that flip is a named, gated FOLLOW-UP (AC7).

## Background / Context

- ADR-025 (`docs/ARD.md:313-326`): §2 the admissible set; §5 the columns + Rejected (License-gate) subsection + ONE-writer rule; §4 the SAST supersession MEASURED not asserted.
- ADR-027 (`docs/ARD.md:436-535`): §1 permanent removal + "Rejected (integrity/TeamPCP)" DISTINCT from License-gate; §4 per-tool pin+verify (= ADR-024 §5's admission arm, not a new mechanism); §5 ONE coordinated change incl. the lock-regex retirement; §6 the lens-5 gate (SAST arm only).
- wh-xn0 §11 (`20260609_tool_admissibility_license_gdpr.md:283-318`) — the draft this EXTENDS; wh-nvk RQ6 (`20260609_trivy_replacement_sca_iac.md:265-340`) — rows, edits, ACs #5/#6/#7; §5.1 (`:198-221`) tag→SHA table; §5.2 (`:223-236`) verify primitives; §5.3 (`:238-249`) checkov mutable-image caveat.
- SHAs/artifact facts are 2026-06-10 point-in-time — re-resolve + re-verify at the actual pin commit (ADR-006).
- Depends on NOTHING open (wh-xn0/wh-nvk/wh-d5b closed; ADR-025/027 ratified).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

1. **RED** — extend `test_registry_lock.py` FIRST: per-tool assertions (violators + trivy ABSENT from every SCANNER_PREFERENCE list; admitted tools PRESENT in registry/capability lists — pin both directions, Policy 9); drop the `r"0\.7[01]"` regex from `test_registry_states_illustrative_and_pinning` keeping the neutral "illustrative"+"pin" assertions. New tests FAIL.
2. **GREEN** — the three file edits (registry columns+rows+Rejected+pin-block deletion; SCANNER_PREFERENCE drops; no SAST default wired). Lock + sec-detect suites PASS.
3. **REFACTOR** — registry table readability; changelog APPENDED not edited; surgical diffs only.

## Steps

1. **Columns:** every admitted row gets `license ∈ {MIT, Apache-2.0}` (dual elects permissive — cargo-audit) + `data_egress` + `gdpr` (ADR-025 §5); registry header cites ADR-025/024/027.
2. **Replacement rows** (each with its §5.1 SHA `tag→full-commit-SHA # tag` + §5.2 verify primitive): SCA = OSV-Scanner (SLSA `multiple.intoto.jsonl`+SHA256SUMS) · Grype (cosign keyless; `GRYPE_DB_AUTO_UPDATE=false` + pre-seeded DB — fetch/analyze, ADR-024 §2) · pip-audit · cargo-audit; image+SBOM = Grype+Syft (cosign); IaC = Checkov (pip hash-pin `--require-hashes` OR digest-pinned image; the Docker-action `:3.3.0` mutable-tag caveat noted — prefer binary/pip); secrets = gitleaks (MIT; checksum-only, NO upstream signature → AC5 in-repo checksum-value pin; bus-factor/Betterleaks note) + detect-secrets; k8s opt = kube-linter (Sigstore bundle); GH-Actions = actionlint/zizmor kept; AI-redteam promptfoo row pins `PROMPTFOO_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1`. SHAs verbatim: scan-action@e1165082ffb1fe366ebaf02d8526e7c4989ea9d2 #v7.4.0 · sbom-action@e22c389904149dbc22b58101806040fa8d37a610 #v0.24.0 · gitleaks-action@e0c47f4f8be36e29cdc102c57e68cb5cbf0e8d1e #v3.0.0 · osv-scanner-action@9a498708959aeaef5ef730655706c5a1df1edbc2 #v2.3.8 · checkov-action@a6a5c23963b9d127020ae43a959cd5d8eefc94c7 #v12.3106.0 · kube-linter-action@87802a2f4e01abebb3ee3c67a3002fea71f6eae5 #v1.0.7.
3. **SAST CONTENT only:** lead with admissible per-language linters (gosec · bandit · ruff · eslint-plugin-security) + the floor; record the ADR-011 supersession + the no-admissible-Java-SAST exception (Java taint → floor, ADR-025 §3). NO live default flip (AC7).
4. **Two DISTINCT Rejected subsections:** License-gate (Opengrep & Semgrep CE LGPL-2.1 · CodeQL proprietary · govulncheck BSD-3+CC-BY data · trufflehog AGPL-3.0+egress · hadolint GPL-3.0 · find-sec-bugs LGPL-3.0, each SPDX+reason) · integrity/TeamPCP (Trivy CVE-2026-33634/GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23 — does not return; KICS same campaign).
5. **Delete** the `:50-57` Trivy pin/COMPROMISED block + the `:30`/`:38` Trivy example mentions; APPEND a changelog line (2026-06-10 permanent removal, wh-nvk/ADR-027); the `:66` interim line REMAINS (history).
6. **Absorb wh-4rb's `tool-registry.md:15` stale path ONLY** (`.claude/skills/...` → `plugins/white-hacker/skills/...`); the other 3 refs stay owned by wh-4rb (note in its comment).
7. **SCANNER_PREFERENCE (`:111-118`):** sca — drop trivy + govulncheck; keep pip-audit/osv-scanner, add cargo-audit. iac — drop trivy + hadolint; checkov first + actionlint/zizmor. secrets — drop trufflehog; gitleaks + detect-secrets. sast — drop opengrep/semgrep (violator removal, NOT a default flip); per-language linters remain. NO image-pull default for Grype/Syft (ADR-007 — registry-listed, never auto-selected).
8. **Lock:** retire the `:51` regex; keep capability-level tests green; ADD per-tool present/absent assertions against `dt.SCANNER_PREFERENCE` + the registry text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1:** zero `("trivy"` in detect_tools.py; registry `trivy` hits ONLY in Rejected (integrity/TeamPCP) + 2 changelog lines; the `:50-57` block gone. Greps in Quality Gates.
- [ ] **AC2:** every admitted row carries license/data_egress/gdpr; both Rejected subsections present, distinct, fully populated with SPDX+reason.
- [ ] **AC3:** zero opengrep/semgrep/govulncheck/trufflehog/hadolint inside SCANNER_PREFERENCE; SAST leads with per-language linters.
- [ ] **AC4:** each replacement row carries its tag→full-SHA + verify primitive + the point-in-time/re-resolve note (ADR-006); all six §5.1 SHAs grep-present.
- [ ] **AC5 (GATING — SEC-Q10):** the gitleaks row pins the expected binary checksum VALUE in-repo (human-PR'd at admission) and records the no-upstream-signature gap; NOT merely "sha256 vs upstream checksums.txt".
- [ ] **AC6 (SEC-Q9):** for the five other Actions, the impl asserts action.yml is composite|node20 (NOT docker-over-mutable-image); each row records the resolved wrapper shape; any docker-over-mutable wrapper → binary/digest path recommendation.
- [ ] **AC7 (GATING — lens-5, SAST arm ONLY):** no new cross-language taint engine wired in SCANNER_PREFERENCE["sast"]; the live SAST-default flip is a NAMED FOLLOW-UP gated on the evals/score.py before/after measurement GREEN on a RE-BASELINED JAVA-INCLUSIVE corpus (stale 32-vs-103 first; drift-guard n_cases==len(corpus)). The Trivy/SCA/IaC/secrets changes are NOT blocked by this gate.
- [ ] **AC8:** lock suite GREEN with the regex retired; per-tool present/absent tests added and green; function count rises from 4.
- [ ] **AC9:** sec-detect package suite green.
- [ ] **AC10 (grep-AC):** `grep -rniE "trivy"` over the two files → only the Rejected subsection + changelog lines.

## Quality Gates (must pass before bd close)

```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/test_registry_lock.py -q
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
grep -nE '\("trivy"' plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py            # 0 hits
grep -nE 'opengrep|semgrep|govulncheck|trufflehog|hadolint' plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py  # 0 inside SCANNER_PREFERENCE
grep -rniE 'trivy' plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/tool-registry.md              # only Rejected + changelog
```

## QA Before Push

- [ ] sec-detect still emits a SCAN-PLAN; admitted tools selected when installed; absent → floor degrade (tool_assisted:false) — no hard dependency (ADR-003/015).
- [ ] No-tools repo → floor-only plan, no crash, no Trivy resurfacing.
- [ ] Negative regression: re-adding a violator/trivy to SCANNER_PREFERENCE FAILS the lock; dropping an admitted tool from the registry FAILS the lock.
- [ ] SCANNER_PREFERENCE["sast"] wires no new cross-language engine (AC7).

## Commit Message Format

One coordinated commit: `feat(registry): admissibility columns + Trivy-replacement rows + SCANNER_PREFERENCE Trivy removal (ADR-025/ADR-027)`. Author Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>; no AI attribution; never --no-verify; operator-gated push.

## Risks / Dependencies

**Files to Modify (3, NOTHING else):** `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/tool-registry.md` · `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py` · `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/test_registry_lock.py`
**Depends on:** nothing open. **Single-writer rule:** no concurrent registry/SCANNER_PREFERENCE edit may land (ADR-025 §5/ADR-027 §5).
**Coordinate-with (not blockers):** wh-hxt.1 (staleness columns later) · wh-hxt.4 (machine-readable sidecar mirrors these rows later; no row-writer here) · wh-k6l/ADR-026 (the Trivy watchlist entry is DATA — different gate, don't conflate) · wh-4rb (absorbs ONLY tool-registry.md:15; 3 refs remain its scope).
**R-SHA-drift:** point-in-time SHAs; rows carry re-resolve+re-verify guidance; CI wiring is a separate ticket.
**Rollback:** revert the three files; no migration.

## Notes / Open Questions

- **Named SAST follow-up (AC7):** separate ticket — re-baseline the corpus (Java-inclusive) → run the before/after measurement (docs/qa/<YYYYMMDD>/ cycle, subscription) → flip SCANNER_PREFERENCE["sast"] only if GREEN. This ticket does none of it.
- Grype/Syft are never auto-selected by SCANNER_PREFERENCE (no surprise docker/image pull — ADR-007); registry-listed for explicit image/SBOM scope.
- Changelog append-only; the temporary-vs-permanent reconciliation lives in ADR-027's supersession, not in editing old lines.
