title: Spike (operator-driven): does the harness enforce the tools: allowlist for a plugin SUBAGENT at runtime?
description:
## Problem statement
Does the Claude Code harness actually ENFORCE the `tools:` allowlist for a plugin SUBAGENT at runtime? The white-hacker agent definition grants `tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, SendMessage, ToolSearch` (NO Write/Edit) — verified IDENTICAL in the working tree (`plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md:16`) and the loaded v0.2.0 cache snapshot, with NO drift. But two UNVERIFIED runtime signals suggested Write/Edit might be present at runtime: (i) the session's agent-registry DISPLAY listed Write/Edit for `white-hacker:white-hacker`; (ii) a first white-hacker subagent SELF-REPORTED issuing a `Write` that hit `confine_self_writes`. Both are untrustworthy (the self-report could be synthesized — that subagent had read the hook source; a second subagent correctly REFUSED the injection-shaped probe). This is purely a runtime-ENFORCEMENT question, and it is **load-bearing for C4**: capability-removal (no Write/Edit) is ADR-010/016's spine.

## Research questions
- Q1. At runtime, does a plugin white-hacker SUBAGENT actually LACK the `Write`/`Edit` tools (allowlist enforced), or does it receive a broader default toolset (allowlist NOT enforced)?
- Q2. If not enforced — what is the actual runtime toolset, and does it differ between the local-marketplace install and `--plugin-dir`?

## Why operator-driven (NOT a delegated-reviewer probe)
A delegated reviewer CANNOT settle this: "enumerate/exercise your own tools" is injection-shaped recon, the agent SHOULD resist it (one did, §5.2), and a self-report cannot distinguish a real tool dispatch from a synthesized message. The test must be a CONTROLLED OPERATOR session that observes the actual tool-call surface (e.g. the operator directly inspecting the subagent's available tools / attempting a Write and observing whether it is even dispatchable vs hook-blocked), not the subagent reporting on itself.

## Investigation plan
1. In a controlled operator session (plugin loaded), spawn a white-hacker subagent and determine — by OPERATOR observation, not subagent self-report — whether `Write`/`Edit` are in its actual tool surface.
2. Distinguish "tool absent (allowlist enforced)" from "tool present but every path hook-blocked" — the two have different consequences for C4.
3. Repeat under both load paths (local-marketplace install vs `--plugin-dir`) if they differ.

## Exit criteria (we are done when)
- A recorded verdict: `tools:` allowlist IS / IS NOT enforced for a plugin subagent at runtime, with operator-observed evidence (not a self-report).
- If NOT enforced: a follow-up to re-design C4's capability-removal assumption (ADR-010/016) + an escalation note on follow-up (b) (the teammate-mode gap escalates from Bash-vector to unconfined Write/Edit).
- If enforced: close the open question; ADR-010/016 hold as written.

## Time box
~0.5 day (operator-driven). Docs-only verdict acceptable with a "NOT REPRODUCED" marker if the runtime surface can't be observed (NOT a silent skip).

## Likely outcomes
Either (A) the allowlist IS enforced (the registry display is cosmetic, the self-report was synthesized) — the common/expected case, closes it; or (B) it is NOT enforced — a real C4 weakening that escalates (b) and needs an ADR amendment.

## References
wh-sml report `docs/research/20260614_plugin_hooks_subagent_scope.md` §5 (the runtime open-question box) / §5.2 / §8(d); handoff (d). ADR-010/016 (capability-removal — the spine under test). Co-load-bearing with follow-up (b). DO-NOT-COPY: N/A.

title: Harden white-hacker teammate mode: create permissions.deny set (PreToolUse hooks do not fire for teammates)
description:
## Goal / Problem
The white-hacker's PRIMARY teammate mode runs with NONE of the 5 plugin PreToolUse guards (wh-sml proved hooks DO NOT fire for teammates) AND there is no `permissions.deny` set anywhere to inherit — so only `tools:` capability-removal (no Write/Edit) survives. The Rule-of-Two exfil chain (read a secret via Bash, then `curl` to a non-feed host) and `git push`/`reset --hard` are un-gated for a teammate. Upper-MEDIUM defense-in-depth gap in a primary mode. Goal: restore the C1/C3/C4 Bash backstops on the teammate path + make subagent-mode-for-coverage the documented default.

## Background / Context
- wh-sml report `docs/research/20260614_plugin_hooks_subagent_scope.md` §4.2/§5: teammate tool calls are un-gated; §5.0 verified NO `permissions.deny` exists in `.claude/settings.json`, `.claude/settings.local.json`, or `plugin.json`.
- `confine_patch_writes.py:10` / ADR-016 anticipate pairing the Bash tripwire with a `permissions.deny` set "Claude Code enforces with its own parser" — and "teammates start with the lead's permission settings" (agent-teams doc) → a deny-set is INHERITED by teammates.
- Capability-removal (no Write/Edit) HOLDS in all modes (the load-bearing C4 layer); this ticket restores the Bash-vector backstops only.

## Steps (with description)
1. **CREATE a `permissions.deny` set** (none exists today) in the project's committed settings (`.claude/settings.json`) mirroring the load-bearing Bash backstops: non-feed-host egress (`curl`/`wget`/... to hosts outside the feed allow-list), secret-file reads, git mutation (`git push`/`apply`/`am`/`reset --hard`/`checkout --`), control-file / self-disablement writes. Express each as a Claude Code `permissions.deny` rule.
2. **Document subagent-mode-for-coverage** — white-hacker SHOULD run as a SUBAGENT (all 5 guards fire) for full Harness coverage; reserve teammate mode for adversarial cross-check (per the report §7). Capture the §5.1 tension (subagent mode is itself confined out of ordinary dev).
3. **Note the `--dangerously-skip-permissions` interaction** — the `claude-team`/teammate dev flow commonly launches with `--dangerously-skip-permissions`, which likely BYPASSES `permissions.deny`. VERIFY this; if confirmed, the deny-set does NOT protect a teammate launched that way → the doc guidance (run white-hacker as a subagent) is then the ONLY enforcement for that flow. Record the finding.

## Files to Modify
- `.claude/settings.json` (committed) — the new `permissions.deny` block.
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` and/or `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md` — the subagent-mode-for-coverage guidance (coordinate with follow-up c's §8 doc edit to avoid overlap).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.claude/settings.json` carries a `permissions.deny` set covering: non-feed egress, secret-file read, `git push`/`apply`/`am`/`reset --hard`, control-file writes (`grep -q '"deny"' .claude/settings.json`).
- [ ] A documented note (ARCHITECTURE or the agent profile) states white-hacker runs as a SUBAGENT for full coverage; teammate mode is adversarial-cross-check-only.
- [ ] The `--dangerously-skip-permissions`-bypasses-`deny` question is VERIFIED and the result recorded (if it bypasses, the doc guidance is flagged as the sole teammate-flow enforcement).

## Severity / Priority
P2 — defense-in-depth for an upper-MEDIUM gap in a primary mode; capability-removal already holds, and the gap needs the agent injection-steered to exploit. Highest-value of the wh-sml follow-ups.

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` · `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker` (config/doc only — no pytest package unless a settings-validator test is added).

## Rollback
Revert the `permissions.deny` block + the doc note. Additive — no migration.

## References
wh-sml report §5/§7/§8(b); handoff §"Follow-ups Filed" (b). ADR-016 (Bash tripwire pairs with `permissions.deny`), ADR-010 (capability-removal), ADR-031 (C1/C3/C4/C5). Co-load-bearing with follow-up (d) (runtime `tools:` enforcement). DO-NOT-COPY: N/A.

title: staleness.parse_github_json raises on malformed GitHub-API JSON (degrade-never-raise contract violation, latent)
description:
## Goal / Problem
`staleness.parse_github_json` raises uncaught exceptions on malformed/hostile GitHub-API JSON — directly contradicting the module's own "Degrade-never-raise (ADR-003) … never raises" docstrings. Found by the 2026-06-13 dogfood (F-002), verified against live code. LATENT: `parse_github_json` has ZERO live callers today (only its own def + tests), so the untrusted path is not yet reachable in the shipped product — but it goes live the moment the function is wired into `poll_feeds.py` PARSERS (the stated wh-5es next step). Fix BEFORE that wiring.

## Reproduction
Call `parse_github_json(raw, now=...)` with any UNTRUSTED input (a real GitHub-API response is attacker-influenceable):
1. Malformed/truncated JSON (`'{bad'`) → `json.JSONDecodeError` (uncaught at `:154`).
2. A JSON array of non-dicts (`'[1,2,3]'`) or a bare scalar → `AttributeError` (`.get` on a non-dict, ~`:158`).
3. A present-but-garbage timestamp field (`published_at`/`pushed_at`/`eol_date`) → `ValueError` (`date.fromisoformat`, ~`:41`/`:61`).
4. A non-dict `latest_release` → `AttributeError`.
5. Deeply-nested JSON → `RecursionError`.
All PoC-confirmed raising uncaught in the 2026-06-13 dogfood.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected (the module's `:58-59`/`:145` docstrings): degrade-never-raise — missing/null/malformed fields fall back or yield status `unknown`; the parser NEVER raises.
- Actual: uncaught `JSONDecodeError`/`AttributeError`/`ValueError`/`RecursionError` on present-but-malformed fields — exactly the untrusted-parser threat the docstring claims to handle. Policy 7 doc-vs-code conflict (code and docstring disagree).

## Suspected Root Cause (file:line)
`plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts/staleness.py`:
- `:154` `json.loads(raw_github_json)` — uncaught (JSONDecodeError / RecursionError). VERIFIED 2026-06-13.
- ~`:158` `repo.get(...)` on a per-element value that may not be a dict — AttributeError (dogfood-PoC-verified).
- ~`:41` / ~`:61` `date.fromisoformat(...)` on a present-but-garbage timestamp — ValueError (dogfood-PoC-verified).
The `:58-59`/`:145` docstrings assert degrade-never-raise (VERIFIED present).

## Files in Scope (fix surface)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts/staleness.py` — wrap `json.loads`; `isinstance(repo, dict)` guard each element; make the date helper swallow `ValueError` → None/`unknown`; reconcile the docstrings with the fixed behavior.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts/tests/test_staleness.py` — regression tests per malformed-input class.
> Surgical (Policy 3): robustness hardening only; no behavior change on well-formed input.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `parse_github_json` RETURNS (does NOT raise) on each malformed class: invalid JSON, array-of-non-dicts, scalar, garbage timestamp, non-dict `latest_release`, deep-nested JSON — degrading to `unknown`/`[]` (both ways, Policy 9: a WELL-FORMED response still yields the correct verdicts).
- [ ] The untrusted-parser AC enumerates JSONDecodeError / ValueError / AttributeError / RecursionError (+ OSError/UnicodeDecodeError where applicable) — all degrade.
- [ ] The `:58-59`/`:145` docstrings match actual behavior (no false "never raises" claim for present-but-malformed fields).

## Severity / Priority
MEDIUM (latent) → P2. A degrade-never-raise contract violation on untrusted remote JSON; currently unreachable (no callers) but live on the imminent wh-5es wiring, and the docstrings actively misrepresent safety. Fix before wiring → P2, not P3.

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts/tests -q`
- [ ] `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .`
- [ ] `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker`

## Rollback
Revert the staleness.py hardening + the regression tests + the docstring edits. Additive — no migration.

## References
Dogfood finding F-002 (`.notes/security_audit/20260613/dogfood-review.md`, local-only). In wh-hxt.18's deliverable (staleness.py); MUST land before wh-5es wires `parse_github_json` into poll_feeds PARSERS. File-disjoint from wh-0uy (staleness.py vs supply_chain.py) → parallel-safe. DO-NOT-COPY: N/A. Commit format: `fix(sec-kb-refresh): staleness.parse_github_json degrades on malformed GitHub JSON`.

title: _read_toml misses RecursionError: deep-nested untrusted TOML aborts scan() (ADR-003)
description:
## Goal / Problem
`_read_toml` in `supply_chain.py` catches `(OSError, tomllib.TOMLDecodeError, ValueError)` but NOT `RecursionError`, so a deeply-nested untrusted TOML manifest (gradle/pypi/cargo) propagates an uncaught `RecursionError` out of `parse_*()` and aborts the whole `scan()` — an ADR-003 degrade-never-raise violation. Found by the 2026-06-13 dogfood review (F-001), independently verified against live code. Same defect class as the already-CLOSED `wh-5ox.16` (ext_scan `_read_manifest` RecursionError); that fix never reached `_read_toml`.

## Reproduction
1. Create a project dir with `gradle/libs.versions.toml` whose value is a deeply-nested array (e.g. `a = ` then ~5000 nested `[`...`]`, past the interpreter recursion limit). The same trigger works via `pyproject.toml` or `Cargo.toml`.
2. Run the deps-scan floor over the dir: `parse_gradle(root)` (or the public `scan(project_dir)`).
3. Observe: `tomllib.load` raises `RecursionError`, which propagates uncaught out of `_read_toml` then `_gradle_catalog` then `parse_gradle` then `scan()`, aborting the scan for the whole target repo.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected (ADR-003 degrade-never-raise): a malformed/hostile manifest degrades — `_read_toml` returns `{}` and `scan()` completes; the parser NEVER raises on untrusted input.
- Actual: uncaught `RecursionError` aborts `scan()` for the entire target repo.

## Suspected Root Cause (file:line)
`plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py:354` — the except clause `except (OSError, tomllib.TOMLDecodeError, ValueError):` omits `RecursionError` (a `RuntimeError` subclass that `tomllib.load` raises on deep nesting). VERIFIED 2026-06-13: 0 occurrences of `RecursionError` in the file. `_read_toml` (def `:344`) has 5 callers — `:921` (gradle/libs.versions.toml, the wh-0c2 path), `:485` (pyproject.toml), `:745` (Cargo.toml), `:724` (Cargo.lock), `:429` (lockfile) — so gradle+pypi+cargo are all exposed by the one root cause.

## Files in Scope (fix surface)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py` — the `_read_toml:354` except clause (one-line ROOT fix covering all 5 callers).
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_gradle.py` (or `test_supply_chain.py`) — the regression test.
> Surgical (Policy 3): fix at the root `_read_toml`; do NOT patch individual callers (that leaves the siblings exposed). No unrelated refactor.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_read_toml:354` catches `RecursionError` (e.g. `except (OSError, tomllib.TOMLDecodeError, ValueError, RecursionError): return {}`); `grep -c RecursionError supply_chain.py` is at least 1.
- [ ] Regression test (both ways, Policy 9): a deep-nested `gradle/libs.versions.toml` fixture makes `parse_gradle(root)` AND `scan(dir)` RETURN (not raise); AND a VALID `libs.versions.toml` still parses to its expected catalog (the fix did not break legit parsing).
- [ ] Untrusted-parser AC enumerates the full class on `_read_toml`: ValueError / OSError / UnicodeDecodeError / RecursionError all degrade to `{}` (order.md invariant).

## Severity / Priority
MEDIUM → P2. Blocks one flow (scan aborts on the affected repo) on attacker-controllable input, but offline/self-supplied (the developer's own reviewed tree), no RCE/data-loss → not P0/P1.

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q`
- [ ] `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .`
- [ ] `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker`

## Rollback
Revert the `_read_toml` except-clause change + the regression test. Additive — no migration.

## References
Dogfood finding F-001 (`.notes/security_audit/20260613/dogfood-review.md`, local-only). Same class as CLOSED `wh-5ox.16`. SERIAL LANE on `supply_chain.py` — sequences with `wh-5ox.2` (one writer at a time; do NOT co-wave). DO-NOT-COPY: N/A (no attacker-controlled literals). Commit format: `fix(deps-scan): _read_toml catches RecursionError on deep-nested untrusted TOML`.

title: Verify plugin hooks.json PreToolUse firing for subagent/teammate modes
description:
## Research Question
- Q1. Do `PreToolUse` hooks registered in `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/hooks.json` (matching `Bash|Write|Edit`) fire for a plugin **SUBAGENT's** tool calls, or are they silently ignored?
- Q2. Do those same hooks fire for a **TEAMMATE's** tool calls (agent-team mode)?
- Q3. If hooks do NOT fire in either mode, what Harness-layer enforcement actually holds for C1 (authorized scope), C4 (reversible / no working-tree write), and C5 (proportionate resources) — and does that leave a documented gap needing a follow-up?

## Timebox
**Timebox:** 1 day.

## Background / Context
white-hacker ships as a Claude Code plugin and runs as (1) a plugin subagent and (2) an agent-team teammate — its PRIMARY modes. In both it executes Bash and potentially Write/Edit during a review. The Harness wires five PreToolUse guards via `hooks.json` (`guard_bash`, `confine_patch_writes`, `confine_self_writes`, `gate_kb_edit`, `gate_data_edit`).

`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` §8 already asserts "plugin subagents ignore `permissionMode`/`mcpServers`/`hooks`." The official Claude Code sub-agents doc (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents, retrieved 2026-06-12) confirms: *"plugin subagents do not support the `hooks`, `mcpServers`, or `permissionMode` frontmatter fields … these fields are ignored."* **BUT** that is the **frontmatter** `hooks:` field — NOT the plugin-scope `hooks.json` registration (a distinct path, ADR-017). Whether a plugin-level `hooks.json` fires inside a subagent session is the OPEN empirical question.

- A 2026-06-11 "live bypass" was CONFOUNDED (plugin fully unloaded that session — proves nothing).
- LIVE DATAPOINT (2026-06-12, the wh-0c2/wh-hxt.18 review wave): the white-hacker subagent **could not be spawned at all** because the session lacked `--plugin-dir` (`subagent_type: white-hacker` not in the agent registry) — confirms the plugin isn't loaded by default, but does NOT yet answer whether a LOADED `hooks.json` fires for subagents.
- ADR-031 audit named this **soft-spot #3**. Related (do not re-debate): ADR-004 (Harness-not-Context), ADR-010/016 (capability-removal; `confine_patch_writes` is "a tripwire, not the boundary").

## Investigation Approach
1. **Primary docs:** distinguish (a) the subagent FRONTMATTER `hooks:` field (confirmed ignored) from (b) the plugin-scope `hooks.json` wired by the plugin mechanism (ADR-017). Record the exact quote + URL from the hooks reference re: hook scope/inheritance.
2. **Live test — subagent path:** `claude --plugin-dir ./plugins/white-hacker`; spawn a white-hacker subagent; have it run a `guard_bash`-blocked command (a `curl` to a NON-feed host, or `git push`); observe exit-2. PASS = blocked; FAIL = proceeds. Record exact command, output, Claude Code version.
3. **Live test — teammate path:** same, via an agent-team teammate (requires `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1`). If unavailable, mark DEFERRED with a reason.
4. **State the consequence:** if hooks don't fire, name the C1/C4/C5 controls that remain (capability-removal via `tools:` = the primary C4 enforcement); assess whether a follow-up is needed (a git pre-commit / CI integrity gate, a `permissions.deny` pairing, or an ARCHITECTURE §3.5/§8 doc correction).

## Expected Deliverables
- [ ] Report `docs/research/<YYYYMMDD>_plugin_hooks_subagent_scope.md`
- [ ] TWO verdicts (subagent / teammate): hooks DO / DO NOT fire — each cited + reproduced
- [ ] Consequence for C1/C4/C5; whether ARCHITECTURE §8's blanket "ignore" needs qualification
- [ ] Follow-up ticket title + one-line goal (if a gap is confirmed), or "none needed" with rationale

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Q1 answered with a cited source AND a live test result (exact command, output, Claude Code version)
- [ ] Q2 same standard; DEFERRED-with-reason acceptable if agent teams unavailable
- [ ] Consequence for C1/C4/C5 stated for each mode lacking coverage
- [ ] Follow-up(s) identified or "none needed" with one-line rationale
- [ ] Timebox respected; a docs-only verdict is acceptable with a "TEST DEFERRED" marker (NOT a silent skip)

## Notes / Open Questions
- The docs Note covers the frontmatter `hooks:` field; the plugin-scope `hooks.json` path is the main empirical gap.
- Agent teams are experimental (`CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1`). If unavailable, the teammate test is DEFERRED.
- Capability-removal (`tools:` has no Write/Edit) is the primary C4 enforcement even at top level; this spike clarifies whether the heuristic tripwire is ALSO absent in subagent mode.

Priority: P2.
notes: RE-GROOMED (wave) 2026-06-13 — VERDICT READY (next wave, co-waved with wh-5ox.2, file-disjoint). SCOPE NARROWED by the 2026-06-13 dogfood: Q1 (subagent path) is LARGELY ANSWERED — plugin hooks.json PreToolUse guards DO FIRE for a plugin subagent (the white-hacker subagent rm -rf was blocked by guard_bash via confine_self_writes). REMAINING: (a) Q1 full-rigor confirmation — a deliberate guard_bash test from a subagent (curl to a non-feed host, or git push, expect exit-2); (b) Q2 the TEAMMATE path (agent-team mode, CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1; DEFERRED-with-reason acceptable if unavailable); (c) the docs-cited verdict report docs/research/<date>_plugin_hooks_subagent_scope.md with the consequence for C1/C4/C5 and any follow-up. Cited artifacts RESOLVE: 5 guards in hooks.json, ADR-017 (ARD.md:199), ARCHITECTURE section 8 (:617). Evidence: .notes/security_audit/20260613/dogfood-review.md. Timebox 1d.
close_reason: Spike resolved. Plugin hooks.json PreToolUse guards FIRE for subagents (reproduced this session, a subagent curl to a non-feed host was blocked) but DO NOT fire for teammates (live-proven, confound ruled out). No permissions.deny exists anywhere, so teammate mode keeps only tools capability-removal, an upper-MEDIUM defense-in-depth gap in the primary teammate mode. Capability-removal (no Write or Edit) verified identical in working tree and the v0.2.0 cache snapshot (no drift). Runtime tools-enforcement for plugin subagents is a named open question (follow-up d). Report at docs/research/20260614_plugin_hooks_subagent_scope.md. Four follow-ups identified.

title: Flag active-scan + cloud-mutation verbs in guard_bash (ADR-031 C5/C1)
description:
## Goal / Problem
Harness-enforce ADR-031 criteria C5 (proportionate resources) + C1 (authorized scope) by extending `guard_bash.py` to flag/deny **active-scan** and **cloud-mutation** verbs.

Today `guard_bash.py` guards destructive Bash, git mutation, secret-file reads, and exfil-shaped egress — but has NO rule for active network scanners (`nmap`/`masscan`/`zmap`) or cloud-mutation CLIs (`aws`/`gcloud`/`az`/`terraform`/`kubectl`/`helm`/`pulumi`). These are outside the authorized read-only review posture (ADR-031 C1: own working tree/diff only; no external-host scanning) and are the over-provisioning pattern the DN42 incident surfaced. The `confine_self_writes.py` egress allow-list (`confine_self_writes.py:62` `NET_VERBS`) is scoped to data-transport tools (curl/wget/nc) and does NOT cover these higher-level verbs. ADR-031 ratifies a PreToolUse tripwire as the enforcement surface — implemented here inside the existing `guard_bash.py` `_check_sub`/`decide` pattern.

Sub-capability (2) — bounding concurrent subagent FAN-OUT — is DEFERRED to a spike (stateless hooks; spawn tool_name unverified; Policy 2). NOT in scope here.

## Background / Context
- ADR-031 (`docs/ARD.md`, last ADR) — adopts C1–C8; ratifies action (1): a PreToolUse tripwire for active-scan/cloud verbs. C5 is Context-advisory only today (`white-hacker.md:150-188`); this makes it Harness-enforced for the verb class.
- ADR-004 (guardrails in the Harness, not Context) · ADR-016 (PreToolUse = tripwire, not boundary; document residual risk in the hook header) · ADR-003 (never block the floor — prefer flag/advise where a verb has legit review uses; hard-deny only clearly out-of-scope).
- `guard_bash.py` wrapper-strip loop (`guard_bash.py:55-60` `_verb`): iterates while `tokens[i] in WRAPPERS`; `WRAPPERS` (`guard_bash.py:28`) includes `sudo`, `nice`, `nohup`, `timeout`, `env`, `stdbuf`, `command`, `builtin`, `xargs`, `\\` — so `sudo nmap` and `nice -n 10 terraform apply` both resolve to the bare verb. The new deny-class checks go **after the git-mutation block** (`guard_bash.py:79-82`) inside `_check_sub`.
- `confine_self_writes.py:62` `NET_VERBS` — data-transport layer (curl/wget/nc/ssh/…); does NOT cover nmap/aws/terraform/kubectl (the gap wh-evr fills).
- `guard_bash.py:27` `_SEPARATOR_RE` — splits compound commands before `_check_sub` is called; no change needed.
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/hooks.json` — PreToolUse wiring (`Bash|Write|Edit`); NO wiring change needed (the check lives in `_check_sub`).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

Per-package, stdlib-first. Package: `hooks`.

1. **RED** — add failing tests to `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests/test_guard_bash.py`: deny active-scan (`nmap`/`masscan`/`zmap`) + cloud-mutation (`aws`/`gcloud`/`az`/`terraform`/`kubectl`/`helm`/`pulumi`); allow legit review (`grep`/`uv run pytest`/`git status`/`git diff`/`cat`) — pin BOTH ways (Policy 9). Include a wrapper-deny test (`sudo nmap`, `nice -n 10 terraform`).

   Run: `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/hooks --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests/test_guard_bash.py -q` — tests should FAIL.

2. **GREEN** — add `ACTIVE_SCAN_VERBS` + `CLOUD_MUTATION_VERBS` module-level constants after `guard_bash.py:29` (`NET_VERBS` line); extend `_check_sub` with two verb-check blocks after `guard_bash.py:82` (the git-mutation block), hard-deny; update the module docstring to name the new deny class (ADR-016, retain the RESIDUAL RISK notice).

   Re-run the package gate — tests should PASS.

3. **REFACTOR** — block messages consistent with existing style (`\`verb ...\` blocked during review` pattern); floor allow-tests stay green; no unrelated changes.

## Steps

1. **Add failing tests** — `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests/test_guard_bash.py`: two new deny-blocks (`test_denies_active_scan_verbs`, `test_denies_cloud_mutation_verbs`) + one wrapper-deny test (`test_denies_via_wrapper_sudo_nice`) + floor-preserve allow assertions pinned BOTH ways (Policy 9).
2. **Add constants** — `guard_bash.py` after `guard_bash.py:29`: `ACTIVE_SCAN_VERBS = {"nmap", "masscan", "zmap"}` and `CLOUD_MUTATION_VERBS = {"aws", "gcloud", "az", "terraform", "kubectl", "helm", "pulumi"}`.
3. **Extend `_check_sub`** — two new blocks after `guard_bash.py:82` (the `git` block), before the secret-file loop: one for `ACTIVE_SCAN_VERBS`, one for `CLOUD_MUTATION_VERBS` (with `az`-specific deny message: "Azure CLI `az`").
4. **Update module docstring** — name the new deny class; retain the RESIDUAL RISK notice (ADR-016).
5. **Verify green** — run the full hooks test suite (not just guard_bash); confirm no regressions.

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/guard_bash.py` — add two constants + two `_check_sub` blocks + docstring update
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests/test_guard_bash.py` — add deny/allow tests for the new verb classes

No other files change. `hooks.json`, `guard_bash.sh`, `confine_self_writes.py`, `confine_patch_writes.py` are NOT touched (additive-only; no wiring change).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_check_sub("nmap -sV 10.0.0.0/8")` → non-None reason
- [ ] `_check_sub("masscan --rate 100 10.0.0.0/8")` → non-None
- [ ] `_check_sub("aws ec2 run-instances ...")` → non-None
- [ ] `_check_sub("terraform apply")` / `_check_sub("kubectl apply -f deploy.yaml")` → non-None
- [ ] `_check_sub("grep -rn TODO src/")` → None (floor preserved)
- [ ] `_check_sub("git diff HEAD")` / `_check_sub("uv run pytest")` → None
- [ ] `sudo nmap ...` and `nice -n 10 terraform apply` also denied (wrapper-stripping via `guard_bash.py:55-60` `_verb`)
- [ ] Both sides pinned per invariant (Policy 9)
- [ ] Module docstring names the new deny class AND retains the RESIDUAL RISK notice (ADR-016)
- [ ] All pre-existing `test_guard_bash.py` cases still pass (no regressions)

## Quality Gates
```
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/hooks --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

- [ ] Hooks package tests pass (`uv run`, `nice -n 10`, no `-n auto`)
- [ ] `validate_manifest.py` green
- [ ] `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] Outer-loop edits scored + gated (N/A — inner-loop code change)

## Rollback
Revert `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/guard_bash.py` and `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests/test_guard_bash.py` to HEAD. The new deny-class is purely additive (new constants + two `_check_sub` blocks + tests); no other file is touched, so revert of the two files fully restores prior state. No `hooks.json` or schema change to unwind.

## Risks / Dependencies
- **Over-blocking (ADR-003 trade-off — intentional):** `kubectl`/`terraform`/`aws` have legit READ sub-commands (`kubectl get`, `aws s3 ls`). This design hard-denies the whole verb — intentional, because the white-hacker reviews the working tree/diff only, not live infra. A future sub-command allowlist is a SEPARATE ticket, NOT bundled here (Policy 3).
- `az` name collision — keep the deny message specific ("Azure CLI `az`").
- No wiring change: `hooks.json` + `guard_bash.sh` unchanged.
- Sub-capability (2) (subagent fan-out bound) DEFERRED to a spike.
- File ownership disjoint from wh-5ox.9 (`_shared/`/`deps-scan/`/`ai-attack-kb` — no overlap with `hooks/`).

## Notes / Open Questions
- Spike (separate) for sub-capability (2): can a PreToolUse hook observe subagent-spawn ops (tool_name?) + maintain a counter? Design via `/design-ticket --type=spike` after the D-spike verdict (wh-sml).
- Wrapper-strip feasibility confirmed (`guard_bash.py:55-60` `_verb` loop; WRAPPERS at `guard_bash.py:28` includes sudo/nice/nohup/timeout/env/stdbuf/command/builtin/xargs): `sudo nmap` and `nice -n 10 terraform apply` both resolve to the bare verb before the deny-class check fires. Baseline green: 10/10 existing tests pass.

Priority: P2.
close_reason: All ACs met: guard_bash active-scan (nmap/masscan/zmap) + cloud-mutation (aws/gcloud/az/terraform/kubectl/helm/pulumi) hard-deny + _verb wrapper-strip fix (sudo/nice -n N); floor preserved. Gates green: hooks 199, manifest + plugin-validate. QA brute-forced 1078 cmds, 0 regressions; TL reviewed (ADR-016 tripwire posture). Follow-up: wh-evr-fu1 (positional/sh-c/substitution hardening).

title: ext_scan _read_manifest RecursionError: deep-nested manifest crashes scan() (ADR-003 violation)
description:
## Goal / Problem
`ext_scan.scan()` CRASHES (does not degrade) on an attacker-controlled extension `package.json`,
violating ADR-003 "degrade, never raise" in shipped code. Threat model: white-hacker reviews UNTRUSTED
repos / extension trees; a malicious extension manifest that is deeply NESTED (but small) aborts the
whole extension scan. Class: CWE-674 (uncontrolled recursion / resource exhaustion) → DoS of the
reviewer on the standing threat model.

## Reproduction
On a fresh checkout (must force the on-disk path with `editor_cli=None` — on a machine WITH an editor
CLI, `scan(dir)` takes the CLI branch and never reaches `_read_manifest`, masking the bug):

```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts python - <<'PY'
import os, sys, tempfile
sys.path.insert(0, "plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts")
sys.path.insert(0, "plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts")
import ext_scan
payload = '{"a":' * 100000 + '1' + '}' * 100000   # nested OBJECT, ~600 KB — under the 2 MB byte cap
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as root:
    d = os.path.join(root, "evil.pkg-1.0.0"); os.makedirs(d)
    open(os.path.join(d, "package.json"), "w").write(payload)
    ext_scan.scan(root, editor_cli=None)   # raises RecursionError instead of degrading
PY
```

Verified 2026-06-12: prints `RecursionError`. `json.loads` on depth ~100000 raises `RecursionError`
(a `RuntimeError` subclass, NOT `ValueError`); depth 2000 parses fine — so the trigger is parse-DEPTH,
not byte-SIZE.

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** the over-nested manifest is skipped (`_read_manifest` → `None`, the dir is skipped like
  any unparseable manifest); `scan()` returns a finding-schema doc and NEVER raises (ADR-003).
- **Actual:** `RecursionError` propagates out of `_read_manifest` → `_enumerate_ondisk` → `scan()`,
  aborting the scan.

## Suspected Root Cause
`plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py:134` — `_read_manifest`'s except tuple is
`(OSError, ValueError, UnicodeDecodeError)`, OMITTING `RecursionError`. `json.loads` (line 133) on a
deeply-nested manifest raises `RecursionError`, which is a `RuntimeError` subclass (not a `ValueError`),
so it is NOT caught. `_read_manifest` is called at `ext_scan.py:156` inside `_enumerate_ondisk`'s loop,
which is NOT wrapped in a try that catches it (the only try, 148-151, is `except TypeError` around
`Path(extensions_dir)`). The docstring at `ext_scan.py:119-121` FALSELY promises "a multi-GB /
deeply-nested manifest can't blow up RAM ... NEVER raises" — the byte cap (line 124) defends RAM-by-SIZE,
not parse-DEPTH. Same class of gap was already fixed in the sibling `config_persist_scan.py:125`
(wh-5ox.3 DEFECT-1), which DOES include `RecursionError`.

## Files in Scope (the fix surface)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py` — add `RecursionError` to the `_read_manifest`
  except tuple (line 134) + correct the depth claim in the docstring (119-121).
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests/test_ext_scan.py` — the regression test(s).

No other file. Surgical (Policy 3) — the fix does NOT touch the `_enumerate_cli` path (wh-5ox.14) or the
prose-clamp (wh-5ox.13).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — add a regression test: a deeply-nested-object manifest makes `scan(root, editor_cli=None)`
   DEGRADE (return, not raise). Confirm it FAILS on the current tree (RecursionError). Mirror the
   DEFECT-1 mutation-test pattern in `test_config_persist_scan.py:221`.
2. **GREEN** — add `RecursionError` to the `ext_scan.py:134` except tuple + fix the docstring. Re-run the
   package tests.
3. **REFACTOR** — none beyond the docstring correction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A regression test FAILS before the fix (RecursionError) and PASSES after (`test_deeply_nested_manifest_degrades_no_raise`).
- [ ] Both directions pinned (Policy 9): nested manifest → `scan()` returns/degrades AND a normal legit
      manifest still parses + enumerates (the fix doesn't suppress real parsing).
- [ ] No regression: `ide-hygiene` package tests stay green.
- [ ] (Security) threat-model probe closed — the malicious nested manifest no longer aborts `scan()`.

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts --with pytest \
  pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```
- [ ] Touched package tests pass · `validate_manifest.py` green · `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] Policy 12 — a non-zero gate keeps the bug `in_progress`; never check an AC whose probe is SKIP not PASS

## Severity ↔ Priority
**P2** — a live security defect: a DoS (scanner crash) reachable on the standing threat model (review of
untrusted extension trees), in shipped code. Not P0 (no missed/false vuln; the scanner aborts loudly,
it doesn't silently pass). Not P3 (real behavioral impact on a real scan path).

## Commit Message Format
```
fix: catch RecursionError in ext_scan _read_manifest (degrade on deep-nested manifest)
```
Security-relevant fix → gets a dogfood review (QA flow).

## Rollback
`git revert` the fix hunk (one except-tuple entry + docstring). No data/migration to undo.

## Risks / Dependencies
- **Serialize vs wh-5ox.13** (test backfill + LOW-2 prose clamp for ioc_scan/ext_scan) and **wh-5ox.14**
  (ide-hygiene activation-grep on the editor-CLI enumeration path) — both touch `ext_scan.py`. Both are
  OPEN, not in-progress, so no live collision; this fix lands FIRST and .13/.14 groom against the
  post-fix tree (NEVER co-wave on `ext_scan.py`).
- Risk: the added `RecursionError` catch must not mask a legitimate deep-but-valid manifest — mitigated
  by the both-directions AC (a normal manifest still enumerates).

## Notes / References
- Discovering signal: wh-5ox.3 wave handoff (`.notes/handoff-wh-5ox.3.md` Follow-ups + dogfood/Retro);
  the sibling fix `config_persist_scan.py:125`.
- Read 2026-06-12: `ext_scan.py:114-136` (`_read_manifest`), `:148-172` (`_enumerate_ondisk`),
  `:390-448` (`scan`/`_collect` — the CLI-vs-on-disk branch that masks the bug when an editor CLI exists).
close_reason: FIXED via TDD. RED: test_enumerate_skips_deeply_nested_manifest_no_raise failed with RecursionError at json.loads (ext_scan.py:133). GREEN: added RecursionError to the _read_manifest except tuple (ext_scan.py:134) + corrected the docstring depth claim (119-121). Both directions pinned (Policy 9): nested manifest -> scan() degrades/returns; well-formed sibling still enumerated. ide-hygiene 35 passed (was 34); manifest + plugin-validate green. Serialize note: ext_scan.py shared with OPEN wh-5ox.13/.14 -> they groom against post-fix tree (never co-wave on ext_scan.py). No git commit (operator-gated).

title: sec-detect detect_kernel_adjacency unbounded YAML read — sibling DoS to wh-e1d (CWE-400)
description:
## Goal / Problem
`detect_kernel_adjacency` reads every `*.yml`/`*.yaml` via an UNBOUNDED `read_text()` — an OOM DoS (CWE-400) when reviewing untrusted repos. Sibling to wh-e1d (which hardened `_read_manifest_text`); this is the same DoS class one function over, on the standing threat model "review untrusted repos." Found by white-hacker dogfood (F-2) during wh-e1d review.

## Reproduction
On a fresh checkout, a committed multi-GB YAML at repo root:
```bash
d=$(mktemp -d); python3 -c "open('$d/big.yml','w').write('a: '+'x'*(100*1024*1024))"
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts python -c "
import detect_tools as t; from pathlib import Path
print(t.detect_kernel_adjacency(Path('$d')))"   # peak alloc ~200 MiB (file size, doubled by .lower())
```

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** the YAML read is bounded (≤ a fixed cap); a multi-GB file does not balloon RSS.
- **Actual:** `path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\").lower()` loads the whole file; 100 MiB → ~200 MiB peak alloc; a multi-GB committed YAML OOMs the scanner. Runs on every `build_scan_plan`.

## Suspected Root Cause
`plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py:326` — `text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\").lower()` over each yielded `*.yml`/`*.yaml`, no size cap. NOT a hang (`_iter_files` already filters `is_file()`, so a FIFO/device `*.yml` is never yielded — PoC confirms no hang) → OOM-only, bounded by RAM.

## Files in Scope (the fix surface)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py` — the bounded read at :326.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests/test_detect_tools.py` — the regression test.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. RED — a `>cap` YAML with a privileged-container token BEYOND the cap → currently matched (or OOMs); should NOT be matched after the cap.
2. GREEN — `with path.open(encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\") as fh: text = fh.read(_AI_MANIFEST_READ_CAP).lower()` (the 64 KiB cap at detect_tools.py:125; privileged-container tokens sit at the top so 64 KiB suffices; `is_file()` already guaranteed by `_iter_files`).
3. REFACTOR — none beyond the fix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Regression test FAILS before / PASSES after: a token WITHIN the cap is matched (`==`), a token BEYOND the cap is not (`!=`).
- [ ] No regression: existing detect_kernel_adjacency tests stay green.
- [ ] The threat-model probe is closed — a multi-GB YAML no longer balloons alloc.

## Quality Gates
- [ ] sec-detect package tests green · `validate_manifest.py` green · `claude plugin validate` green

## Severity ↔ Priority
P2 — a live DoS reachable on the standing threat model (review untrusted repos); OOM-only (not a hang), MEDIUM severity per the discovering review.

## Notes / References
- Discovered by white-hacker dogfood (F-2) during wh-e1d review; evidence local-only in `.notes/security_audit/2026-06-11/wh-e1d-review.md` (gitignored).
- Surgical sibling to the landed wh-e1d fix (`_read_manifest_text` is already hardened); do NOT bundle — separate ticket per Policy 3.
- Verify via /design-ticket against detect_tools.py before implementation (operator-gated).
notes: GROOMED 2026-06-11 (re-groom pre-launch, next-wave with wh-ezc). VERDICT: READY — no body change (full bug-template, P2 matches rubric: live DoS on standing threat model, OOM-only/MEDIUM). RE-VERIFIED vs live tree AFTER wh-e1d landed (279dcd2 edited detect_tools.py) — every cited line still ACCURATE, no drift: detect_kernel_adjacency def detect_tools.py:282; unbounded read at :326 'text = path.read_text(encoding=utf-8, errors=ignore).lower()'; it sits INSIDE 'for path in _iter_files(root):' (:316) wrapped in try/except (OSError, ValueError) (:325-328) -> is_file() guaranteed by _iter_files (:272/:276) -> OOM-only NOT a hang (confirmed). Cap _AI_MANIFEST_READ_CAP=65536 (64 KiB) at :125 (reuse, no new constant). Mirror target _read_manifest_text:417 + IN-FUNCTION precedent already at :408 ('text = fh.read(_AI_MANIFEST_READ_CAP).lower()') — fix = swap :326 to 'with path.open(encoding=utf-8, errors=ignore) as fh: text = fh.read(_AI_MANIFEST_READ_CAP).lower()' (fits the existing try-block). test_detect_tools.py exists (19 kernel/adjacency tests); package shape OK; NO bd blockers -> LAUNCHABLE NOW. File ownership: detect_tools.py + test_detect_tools.py — DISJOINT from wh-ezc (supply_chain.py); serialize vs wh-hxt.1 (both detect_tools.py, later wave).
close_reason: detect_kernel_adjacency unbounded YAML read bounded to _AI_MANIFEST_READ_CAP (64 KiB) at detect_tools.py:326-327, mirroring the wh-e1d _read_manifest_text fix; CWE-400 OOM DoS on untrusted repos closed. 90 sec-detect tests green; regression pins within-cap matched / beyond-cap not matched; QA 4-tier PASS; dogfood 0 findings (advisory-metadata detection-gap acknowledged, not exploitable).

title: Always-on bundled watchlist: supply_chain.scan loads the curated file by default
description:
## Goal / Problem
The bundled curated watchlist (`plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/known-compromised.osv.json`,
seeded by wh-k6l — `litellm` 1.82.7/1.82.8, GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76) is consulted ONLY if a caller builds a db
via `load_malware_db` and passes it to `scan(malware_db=...)`. In a default scan (no external OSSF snapshot,
no caller-supplied db) `supply_chain.scan` defaults `malware_db=None`, so S8 degrades and the shipped
watchlist is NEVER consulted. Wire `scan` to load the bundled curated file BY DEFAULT so the watchlist is
always-on. Value-completing step: without it wh-k6l ships a tested mechanism + seed, NOT active protection.

## Background / Context
- wh-k6l (CLOSED `977f8fc`) extends `load_malware_db(snapshot_dir=None, *, curated_path=_CURATED_WATCHLIST)`
  (`plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/malware_db.py:48`) to fold the bundled curated file FIRST
  (degrade-if-absent, never raises), then UNION an OPTIONAL OSSF snapshot dir. `_CURATED_WATCHLIST`
  (`malware_db.py:40`) resolves to `_shared/reference/known-compromised.osv.json` — the canonical Gate-2
  path (ADR-026). The loader CAPABILITY + the seed exist; only the wiring into `scan` is missing.
- `supply_chain.py:47` imports ONLY `is_known_bad` (not `load_malware_db`). `scan` (`supply_chain.py:1239`)
  defaults `malware_db: dict | None = None`; `signal_s8` (`:1016`) returns `[]` when `not malware_db` and the
  caller records `malware-db` unavailable; `_build_doc` (`:1419`) just threads `malware_db` through. So
  `load_malware_db` is called NOWHERE in production today.
- `reference/MALWARE-DB.md` documents the operator-builds-db flow; this ticket makes the curated floor
  always-on without requiring that flow.
- ADR-003 degrade contract: a missing/odd file degrades (never raises); the ~895 MB OSSF tree stays opt-in
  (bundled single-doc file ONLY by default).

## Prerequisites
- wh-k6l CLOSED + committed (`027dddc` loader fold + seed; `977f8fc` close). `load_malware_db`'s default
  `curated_path` + the bundled `known-compromised.osv.json` are on disk. ✓ satisfied.

## Steps (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — In `tests/test_supply_chain.py` add: (a) always-on — `scan(project_dir)` on a project pinning
   `litellm==1.82.7`, NO `malware_db` arg + NO snapshot, flags it via S8: assert the finding present
   (`== flagged`) AND a clean sibling pinning `litellm==1.82.6` (or another package) NOT flagged
   (`!= flagged`); (b) degrade — curated path pointed at a missing/odd file → S8 `[]` +
   `summary.tools_unavailable` records `malware-db`, no raise; (c) union — a caller-supplied `malware_db`
   dict is honored AND unioned with the bundled floor (not replaced). Run deps-scan package tests — (a)/(c)
   FAIL today.
2. **GREEN** — In `scan` (`supply_chain.py:1239`), at the TOP (before BOTH the merged-early-return at
   `:1262` and the main path's `signal_s8` at `:1277`): when `malware_db is None`, set
   `malware_db = load_malware_db()` (curated floor ONLY — snapshot stays opt-in); when a caller passes a db,
   UNION it with `load_malware_db()`. Add `load_malware_db` to the `:47` import. Preserve degrade:
   `load_malware_db` never raises and may return empty → S8 returns `[]` and `tools_unavailable` records
   `malware-db`. Re-run — tests PASS.
3. **REFACTOR** — Keep surgical (Policy 3): only `scan`'s default/union + the import change; touch no other
   deps-scan path. Confirm all gates green.

## Files to Modify/Create
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py` — add `load_malware_db` to the `:47`
  import; default/union the curated floor at the top of `scan` (`:1239`).
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_supply_chain.py` — add the always-on / degrade /
  union tests (a/b/c above).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] With NO external snapshot and NO caller-supplied db, `scan(project_dir)` consults the bundled curated
      watchlist (S8 flags `litellm@1.82.7`) — pin `== flagged` AND a clean sibling `!= flagged`.
- [ ] Degrade preserved: a missing/odd bundled file → S8 returns `[]`, `summary.tools_unavailable` records
      `malware-db`, never raises.
- [ ] A caller-supplied db (incl. one built from an external snapshot) still UNIONs with the bundled floor —
      not silently replaced.
- [ ] No regression: existing deps-scan suite + `test_supply_chain.py` green.

## Quality Gates
- [ ] deps-scan package tests green — `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q`
- [ ] `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` green
- [ ] `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker` green
- [ ] Outer-loop gate N/A (inner-loop deps-scan code; no KB/registry/eval-corpus edit) — Policy 12: a
      non-zero gate keeps the task in_progress.

## Rollback
- Revert the `scan` default/union + the `:47` import addition and drop the added tests; behavior returns to
  "curated watchlist consulted only when a caller passes a db." No data/schema/migration to undo — the
  wh-k6l bundled file + loader stay in place.

## Risks / Dependencies
- Depends on wh-k6l (loader fold + seed) — CLOSED ✓.
- SERIAL lane (`supply_chain.py`) — NEVER co-wave with another deps-scan/supply_chain.py edit. CAN co-wave
  with wh-qu0 (`detect_tools.py`, disjoint).
- Always-on default must NOT surprise the ADR-003 degrade contract or auto-load the ~895 MB OSSF tree
  (bundled single-doc file only; external snapshot stays opt-in).

## Notes / Open Questions
- AC#3 interface note: `scan` takes a `malware_db` DICT, not a `snapshot_dir`; the "external snapshot" union
  is exercised at the `load_malware_db(snapshot_dir=...)` boundary, so at `scan`'s boundary the union is
  "caller db ∪ bundled floor." Test at that interface.
- Filed mid-wave by tech-lead per team-lead ruling (batch ① wh-k6l follow-up); re-groomed on-template
  2026-06-11.
notes: GROOMED 2026-06-11 (re-groom pre-launch, next-wave with wh-qu0). VERDICT: READY. Blocker CLEARED — wh-k6l CLOSED + committed (027dddc loader+seed, 977f8fc close); wh-ezc now in bd ready. Re-groomed ON-TEMPLATE: was missing Steps / Files-to-Modify / Rollback (filed mid-wave, never /design-ticket'd) — rewrote body to the task template (added Prerequisites, Steps R/G/R, Files to Modify/Create, Rollback). VERIFIED vs live tree (no drift): supply_chain.py:47 imports only is_known_bad; scan():1239 defaults malware_db=None; signal_s8():1016 degrades on 'not malware_db'; _build_doc():1419 threads it through. load_malware_db(snapshot_dir=None, *, curated_path=_CURATED_WATCHLIST) confirmed at malware_db.py:48; _CURATED_WATCHLIST->_shared/reference/known-compromised.osv.json at :40; bundled seed on disk (litellm 1.82.7/1.82.8, GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76). test_supply_chain.py EXISTS (28 tests) — AC claim accurate. Design clarification recorded: scan() takes a malware_db DICT not a snapshot_dir, so AC#3 union is 'caller db U bundled floor' at scan()'s boundary. Fix point: default the floor at the TOP of scan() so both the merged-early-return (:1262) and main path (:1277) see it. SERIAL lane (supply_chain.py) — co-wave only with wh-qu0 (detect_tools.py, disjoint).
close_reason: Always-on bundled watchlist wired into scan(): load_malware_db() defaulted/unioned at top of scan (supply_chain.py:1259) so the curated floor is consulted in every default scan; caller db unioned with the floor (set-normalized via _as_set, never raises; degrade preserved per ADR-003). 161 deps-scan tests green; manifest+plugin-validate green; QA 4-tier PASS; dogfood 0 findings (N-1 union list-type TypeError hardened + regression test test_s8_caller_db_list_typed_value_unions_without_raise).

title: Multi-entry watchlist layout + Gate-2 hook extension (unblocks the feeder + Trivy-rows)
description:
## Goal / Problem
wh-k6l ships the watchlist as a SINGLE file = ONE entry (one `id`, one `target`), forced by the frozen Gate-2 hooks. The OSSF feeder (from the wh-5es spike) and the Trivy-replacement rows (from the wh-nvk spike) both need to add MULTIPLE entries of MIXED targets — which the single-file/one-entry validator + the single-path Gate-2 hooks cannot hold. This ticket designs + lands the multi-entry layout (a directory of `*.osv.json`) and extends the Gate-2 hooks to gate it, so those consumers are unblocked WITHOUT a false-merit merge.

## Background / Context
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/watchlist-entry-schema.json:8,19-21` — one entry = one id + one target enum.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/validate_watchlist.py:245` rejects a non-dict (array) top level; `validate_dir` already supports a dir of one-entry `*.json` files.
- FROZEN Gate-2 hooks (ADR-026 SEC-Q12, do NOT weaken): `gate_data_edit.py:52` (`DATA_SEGMENTS`) and `gate_kb_edit.py:27` (`DATA_PATHS`) hardcode the single suffix `/_shared/reference/known-compromised.osv.json`. A per-entry file in a sibling dir is writable (`confine_self_writes.py:48` ALLOW_SEGMENTS covers `/_shared/reference/`) but NOT Gate-2-gated → it falls into `gate_kb_edit` GATED_SEGMENTS and is admitted by the eval-J KB verdict = the false-merit merge ADR-026 §1/§3 forbids.
- ADR-026:331-332 names the consumers: wh-5es (feeder), wh-hxt.4 (registry sidecar), wh-nvk (Trivy rows).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A directory layout (e.g. `_shared/reference/known-compromised/*.osv.json`) is specified + validated by `validate_watchlist.py <dir> --check-regression` exit 0 (multi-entry).
- [ ] The Gate-2 hooks gate EACH entry file under the dir (content-bound DATA verdict per file), with a test that an ungated per-entry write is BLOCKED and a verdict-backed one is allowed.
- [ ] No regression to the existing single-file path; `confine_self_writes`/`gate_kb_edit`/`gate_data_edit` test suites stay green.
- [ ] The bootstrap path is documented (how the FIRST per-entry file lands under the gate — operator/human-PR per ADR-012).

## Quality Gates
- [ ] hooks + _shared package tests green · `validate_manifest.py` green · `claude plugin validate` green

## Risks / Dependencies
- Touches FROZEN security hooks (ADR-026 SEC-Q12) — design carefully; an ADR addendum may be required (append-only).
- The future feeder-implementation + Trivy-rows-implementation tickets (derived from the CLOSED spikes wh-5es / wh-nvk) MUST depend on THIS ticket. (Cannot bd-dep a closed spike; wire the dep when those impl tickets are created.)

## Notes / Open Questions
- Re-groom/verify via /design-ticket against the real hook code before implementation (operator-gated).
- Filed mid-wave by tech-lead per team-lead ruling (batch ① wh-k6l follow-up).

title: sec-detect: _read_manifest_text root-level uncapped read_text — same DoS class as wh-7u7 F-1 (CWE-400)
description:
## Goal / Problem
`_read_manifest_text` (sec-detect) reads root manifests with an uncapped `read_text()` and no file-type guard — a DoS (CWE-400) when reviewing untrusted repos. Same class as the wh-7u7 F-1 finding (white-hacker dogfood), but at the ROOT-level primitive that runs on every scan. **Threat model: review untrusted repos** — the repo author controls root manifest filenames.

## Reproduction
On a fresh checkout, point a scan at a repo whose root `requirements.txt` is a FIFO/char-device:
```bash
d=$(mktemp -d); mkfifo "$d/requirements.txt"   # or: ln -s /dev/zero "$d/requirements.txt"
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts python -c "
import detect_tools as t; from pathlib import Path
t.build_scan_plan(Path('$d'))   # HANGS — _read_manifest_text blocks on the FIFO/device read
"
```

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** `_read_manifest_text` returns `''` for a non-regular-file (FIFO/device/broken symlink) and reads at most a bounded number of bytes for a giant manifest — the scan completes.
- **Actual:** `p.read_text()` blocks forever on a FIFO/`/dev/zero` (the read never returns, so the `except (OSError, ValueError)` never fires); a multi-GB committed manifest OOMs (`.lower()` doubles RSS).

## Suspected Root Cause
`detect_tools.py:417` `_read_manifest_text(root, filename)`: `p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").lower()` with NO `is_file()` guard and NO size cap. It runs on EVERY `build_scan_plan` via `_match_signals`:432 (FRAMEWORK_SIGNALS + AI_FRAMEWORK_SIGNALS) and `detect_kernel_adjacency`:307 (Makefile) / :311 (go.mod). The wh-7u7 F-1 fix in `detect_ai_agent_infra` (`is_file()` guard + `fh.read(_AI_MANIFEST_READ_CAP)`, `=65536`) is the worked template — this is the same primitive at a different call site.

## Files in Scope (the fix surface)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py` — `_read_manifest_text` (add `is_file()` + bounded read; reuse `_AI_MANIFEST_READ_CAP` or a sibling constant). Hardens ALL its callers at once.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests/test_detect_tools.py` — regression tests.
- Keep the existing `except (OSError, ValueError): return ""`.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — a FIFO root `requirements.txt` test (via `os.mkfifo`, skip where unavailable) that currently HANGS/should return `''`; a `>cap` test where an AI/framework token sits beyond the cap → not matched. Both FAIL pre-fix.
2. **GREEN** — `is_file()` guard + `fh.read(<cap>)` mirroring the F-1 fix.
3. **REFACTOR** — share the cap constant with `detect_ai_agent_infra` if natural.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A root `requirements.txt`/`go.mod`/etc. that is a FIFO/device/symlink-to-special → `_read_manifest_text` returns `''` WITHOUT hanging (`is_file()` guard); test via `os.mkfifo` (POSIX; skip elsewhere).
- [ ] A `>cap` root manifest is read only up to the cap — a token BEYOND the cap is not matched (`!=`), a token WITHIN is (`==`).
- [ ] No regression: all current `detect_languages`/`detect_frameworks`/`detect_kernel_adjacency` tests stay green.
- [ ] sec-detect package suite green.

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

## Severity ↔ Priority
**P2** — a live security defect (DoS reachable on the standing "review untrusted repos" threat model), same CWE-400/MEDIUM class as F-1; runs on every scan, so reachability ≥ F-1.

## Commit Message Format
`fix: bound _read_manifest_text read (is_file + cap) — root-level DoS twin of wh-7u7 F-1` (author Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>, no AI attribution).

## Rollback
`git revert` the fix hunk (no data/migration).

## Risks / Dependencies
- **Serialize vs wh-hxt.1** — both edit `detect_tools.py`; NEVER co-wave (wh-hxt.1 is a later wave).
- Risk: the cap must be large enough that real AI-SDK tokens (near the top of a manifest) still match — 64 KiB is the F-1-validated value.

## Notes / References
Surfaced by dogfooding wh-7u7's diff (white-hacker F-1). Out-of-diff for wh-7u7 (Policy 3 — a fix never bundles a neighbour), filed as a separate stale-neighbour task. The wh-7u7 F-1 fix (`detect_ai_agent_infra` `is_file()`+`fh.read(65536)`) is the worked template.
notes: PRIORITY BUMP P3→P2 (2026-06-11, dogfood review of the wh-7u7 close). Rationale: this is the SAME CWE-400 DoS class the white-hacker rated MEDIUM as F-1 (≈P2 in this scheme), NOT lower-severity. VERIFIED live: _read_manifest_text (detect_tools.py:417) is uncapped `p.read_text(...)` with no is_file() guard — a FIFO/device read BLOCKS (never raises OSError, so the except clause doesn't help). It runs on EVERY build_scan_plan: via _match_signals (:432, reads each root manifest for FRAMEWORK_SIGNALS + AI_FRAMEWORK_SIGNALS) AND detect_kernel_adjacency (:307 Makefile, :311 go.mod). So a malicious repo with a ROOT `requirements.txt -> /dev/zero` (or go.mod/Makefile/pyproject.toml/package.json/Pipfile) hangs the scanner on a normal scan. Reachability is AT LEAST as high as F-1 (a root file is easier to plant than a nested one); "narrower" in the body refers only to attack surface (fixed names, no walk), not impact. Deferral stays correct (Policy 3 pre-existing neighbour; must serialize vs wh-hxt.1 on detect_tools.py) — only the severity was wrong.
close_reason: Fixed _read_manifest_text (detect_tools.py:417) — is_file() guard + bounded fh.read(_AI_MANIFEST_READ_CAP=65536), kept except(OSError,ValueError). Gates green (TL-run): sec-detect 88 passed, validate_manifest OK, claude plugin validate passed. QA PASS (4 tiers incl. real-caller FIFO/symlink + cap-boundary); white-hacker PASS on fix (empirical: 200MB→198KiB alloc, no hang). New sibling finding F-2 (unbounded YAML read at :326) routed as a separate follow-up (out of scope, Policy 3).

title: Populate campaign-iocs.json from PRIMARY sources (do-not-copy data step for wh-5ox.1)
description:
## Goal / Problem

Populate `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/campaign-iocs.json` with REAL, primary-source-verified literals for the Carnage (CVE-2026-45321) and TeamPCP (CVE-2026-33634) campaigns. wh-5ox.1 shipped the IOC mechanism and schema with an EMPTY data plane (all entries are `EXAMPLE_PLACEHOLDER` with blank literals). This ticket fills that data plane.

**DO-NOT-COPY CONSTRAINT (load-bearing, from epic wh-5ox):** Every literal MUST be re-derived from a PRIMARY source (CVE/GHSA record, vendor advisory, or the actual artifact) — NOT from community YARA or disputed secondary sources. Any literal that cannot be primary-confirmed MUST be marked `low_durability: true`. Named malicious packages become watchlist rows (wh-k6l), NOT IOC-pack literals.

## Background / Context

- wh-5ox.1 (DONE): shipped `ioc_scan.py` + `campaign-iocs.json` schema at `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/campaign-iocs.json`. The file currently contains only 2 EXAMPLE_PLACEHOLDER entries with `literal: ''` and `primary_source: 'TODO-primary-source-required'` (verified by reading campaign-iocs.json:12-29, 2026-06-11).
- `campaign-iocs.json:2-11` (verified 2026-06-11): schema documents the 5 IOC kinds (beacon, commit-marker, exfil-filename, c2-host, lock-marker), the `primary_source` URL requirement, and the `low_durability` flag.
- `test_ioc_scan.py:278-289` (verified 2026-06-11): `test_shipped_data_file_has_no_real_literals` asserts the file currently ships with NO real literals — this test MUST be UPDATED or REMOVED as part of this ticket (it will fail once real literals are added).
- Community YARA/IOC sets (e.g. from GitHub threat-intel repos): UNLICENSED and partly DISPUTED (the Carnage deadman wiper is contested per epic wh-5ox background). Do NOT copy them.
- Relates to wh-k6l (named package IOCs → watchlist rows). Named packages (e.g. nx-console impostors) go in the watchlist, NOT in this file.
- Primary sources to consult: NVD/NIST CVE-2026-45321, GHSA record for CVE-2026-33634, vendor advisories, the actual PyPI/npm artifact diffing (for commit markers / beacon strings that appear in the artifact source).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

N/A — this is a DATA-sourcing ticket, not a code-change ticket. The workflow is RESEARCH → VERIFY → POPULATE, not Red/Green/Refactor. However, the existing test `test_shipped_data_file_has_no_real_literals` must be updated:

1. **UPDATE TEST** — `test_shipped_data_file_has_no_real_literals` in `deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_ioc_scan.py:278` currently asserts that all entries have `literal == ''`. After this ticket, that assertion is false. Either:
   - Rename and invert it: `test_shipped_data_file_has_only_primary_sourced_literals` — assert every entry with a non-empty literal has a non-`TODO` `primary_source` URL. Pin: `assert entry['primary_source'] != 'TODO-primary-source-required'` and `assert entry['primary_source'].startswith('http')`.
   - AND add `test_shipped_ioc_scan_finds_no_match_in_empty_tree` to cover the degenerate clean case previously caught by the old test.

2. **RESEARCH + POPULATE** — For each entry added:
   - Locate the primary source URL (CVE/GHSA/vendor advisory).
   - Extract the literal from the primary source text (not from secondary/community repos).
   - Assign kind, severity, low_durability per schema.
   - Disputed claims (Carnage deadman wiper): mark `low_durability: true` with a note in a comment or `_note` field.

3. **VALIDATE** — After populating, run `test_ioc_scan.py` to confirm the new test passes and no existing tests regress.

## Steps (with description)

1. Consult CVE-2026-45321 (NVD/NIST) and the GHSA record to extract the Carnage campaign literals: beacon strings, commit markers, exfil-filename shapes, lock markers that appear in the primary advisory text.
2. Consult CVE-2026-33634 (NVD/NIST) and the TeamPCP vendor advisory for the ICP-canister C2 host string and related campaign literals.
3. For each extracted literal: confirm it appears verbatim in the primary source (not inferred). Mark `low_durability: true` for anything inferred or disputed.
4. Populate `campaign-iocs.json` replacing EXAMPLE_PLACEHOLDER entries with real entries; keep the schema structure intact.
5. Update `test_shipped_data_file_has_no_real_literals` → `test_shipped_data_file_has_only_primary_sourced_literals` with dual-pin assertion (Policy 9).
6. Run the deps-scan test suite; confirm green.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/campaign-iocs.json` — MODIFY (replace EXAMPLE_PLACEHOLDER entries with primary-source-verified literals)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_ioc_scan.py` — MODIFY (update test_shipped_data_file_has_no_real_literals to reflect populated data)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `campaign-iocs.json` contains at least 3 non-placeholder entries with non-empty `literal` fields.
- [ ] Every non-placeholder entry has a `primary_source` URL beginning with `http` (not `TODO-primary-source-required`).
- [ ] Disputed literals (e.g. Carnage deadman wiper claims) are marked `low_durability: true`.
- [ ] Named malicious packages are NOT present as IOC literals (they belong in the watchlist per wh-k6l).
- [ ] The updated test `test_shipped_data_file_has_only_primary_sourced_literals` passes with dual-pin (primary_source URL present AND not the TODO placeholder).
- [ ] `test_shipped_ioc_scan_finds_no_match_in_empty_tree` (new or existing equivalent) passes.
- [ ] All other `test_ioc_scan.py` tests pass (no regression).

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

- [ ] deps-scan package tests pass (updated test suite)
- [ ] `validate_manifest.py` green
- [ ] `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] Outer-loop gate: N/A (data file, not KB/eval-corpus — Gate-2 GHSA/OSV provenance applies; `ioc_scan` tests serve as the Gate-2 regression check here)

## Rollback

Restore the original EXAMPLE_PLACEHOLDER entries in `campaign-iocs.json` (committed state from wh-5ox.1). Revert the test change.

## Risks / Dependencies

- **PRIMARY-SOURCE AVAILABILITY:** CVE-2026-45321 and CVE-2026-33634 NVD records may not yet be fully published (these are 2026 CVEs). If a literal cannot be primary-source-confirmed, it MUST be left blank or marked `low_durability: true`. Do NOT lower the primary-source bar.
- **DISPUTED CARNAGE CLAIM:** The deadman/wiper behavior in Carnage is disputed per epic wh-5ox background. Any related literal must be `low_durability: true`.
- **TEST BREAKAGE:** `test_shipped_data_file_has_no_real_literals` (test_ioc_scan.py:278) asserts blank literals — it WILL fail after this ticket; updating it is MANDATORY before closing.
- Depends on wh-5ox.1 (DONE): the mechanism and schema exist.
- Relates to wh-k6l (watchlist): named package rows go there, not here.
