title: att_ck/atlas + disputed finding fields + KB-xref matcher propagation [supply_chain.py lane]
description:
## Goal / Problem
Add TWO additive, optional finding-schema fields and propagate them from matched KB entries — the schema SPINE for ATT&CK/ATLAS attribution + disputed-claim tracking on findings. (Part 2 — the sec-report coverage matrix — was SPLIT OUT to a separate sec-report ticket BLOCKED BY this one; wh-5ox.10 GROOMED 2026-06-12 → NEEDS SPLIT.)

1. **att_ck[] + atlas[]** (string arrays) on finding-schema `$defs/finding`: ATT&CK enterprise ids (`T*`) + MITRE ATLAS ids (`AML.*`), populated by the kb_refs matcher copying the matched KB entry's `xref[]` MITRE subset.
2. **disputed** `{claim, dispute_source, status: enum[unresolved,refuted,confirmed]}` on finding-schema AND kb-entry-schema; matcher/triage propagate it; cap confidence when `status==unresolved` (the `supply-chain-3.md` DISPUTED wiper is the first migration case).

PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE: map each technique to the canonical MITRE id (don't invent ids); `disputed.status` reflects the actual primary-source split (stay `unresolved` until a primary source resolves it).

## Background / Context
- `_shared/reference/finding-schema.json:39` `$defs.finding` is `additionalProperties:false` (verified 2026-06-12) — att_ck/atlas/disputed MUST be added as EXPLICIT named optional properties (alongside `owasp`/`kb_refs`, already string arrays at :58/:67). No new `required` (strictly additive).
- `kb-entry-schema.json` is at `ai-attack-kb/kb-entry-schema.json` (NOT `_shared/reference/`), `additionalProperties:false`, `required` at :8 — add `disputed` as an optional named property.
- DRIFT (wh-5ox.9, landed 2026-06-12): KB `xref[]` now holds `"<ID> [primary-sourced: <url>]"` strings MIXING OWASP (`LLM03:2025`) + MITRE (`AML.*`/`T*`). The matcher MUST (a) FILTER to MITRE — `T*`/`T*.*` → att_ck, `AML.*` → atlas; (b) EXCLUDE OWASP (already in the finding `owasp` field); (c) STRIP the ` [primary-sourced: <url>]` suffix to the bare id. Source: `ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain-1.md` xref.
- kb_refs matcher (where the new fields get populated): `deps-scan/scripts/normalize_deps.py:44-64` (builds `kb_refs` into the finding dict), `deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py` `_make_finding`, and the sec-triage matcher. A SHARED filter helper (xref[] → {att_ck, atlas}) belongs in `_shared/scripts` (≥2 callers — ADR-015 / Policy 2).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — failing tests: finding-schema accepts att_ck/atlas/disputed AND REJECTS a bad `disputed.status` enum (both ways); the xref→MITRE filter maps `T*`→att_ck, `AML.*`→atlas, drops `LLM*`, strips the `[primary-sourced:]` tag; matcher populates att_ck/atlas from a matched KB entry; disputed propagates; confidence capped when unresolved, uncapped otherwise (both ways).
2. **GREEN** — add the fields to both schemas; add the shared xref→MITRE helper; wire into the 3 matcher points; propagate disputed; cap confidence.
3. **REFACTOR** — additive; both-ways pins (Policy 9).

## Steps
1. Schema: add `att_ck` (string[]) + `atlas` (string[]) + `disputed` (object: claim/dispute_source/status enum) to `finding-schema.json` `$defs.finding.properties` (NOT required). Add `disputed` to `ai-attack-kb/kb-entry-schema.json` properties (NOT required).
2. Shared helper in `_shared/scripts`: `mitre_from_xref(xref: list[str]) -> {"att_ck": [...], "atlas": [...]}` — filters `T*`/`AML.*`, strips ` [primary-sourced: ...]`, drops OWASP.
3. Matcher: at each kb_refs population point (`normalize_deps.py`, `supply_chain.py` `_make_finding`, sec-triage matcher) also set att_ck/atlas via the helper from the matched KB entry's xref, copy `disputed` if the KB entry carries it, and cap `confidence` when `disputed.status=="unresolved"`.
4. validate_findings + tests green.

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/finding-schema.json` (add att_ck/atlas/disputed — additive)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/kb-entry-schema.json` (add disputed — additive)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/` (new `mitre_from_xref` helper) + `_shared/scripts/tests/`
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/validate_findings.py` + tests (if field-specific checks needed)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/normalize_deps.py` + `deps-scan/scripts/tests/` (matcher propagation)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py` (`_make_finding` only — SERIAL lane, light) + tests
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-triage/scripts/` (matcher) + `sec-triage/scripts/tests/`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] finding-schema accepts att_ck/atlas + disputed; REJECTS a bad `disputed.status` value (`== valid` vs `!= valid`, Policy 9); `validate_findings` green.
- [ ] `mitre_from_xref(["LLM03:2025","AML.T0010 [primary-sourced: <url>]","T1195.002 [primary-sourced: <url>]"])` → `{att_ck:["T1195.002"], atlas:["AML.T0010"]}` (drops OWASP, strips tag) — both ways.
- [ ] matcher populates att_ck/atlas from a matched KB entry's xref; a finding with no KB match → empty att_ck/atlas (both ways).
- [ ] disputed propagates from the KB entry; `confidence` capped when `status==unresolved`, unchanged when confirmed/absent (both ways).
- [ ] strictly additive — no new `required` on either schema (frozen-by-reference posture).

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests -q`
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q`
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-triage/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-triage/scripts/tests -q`
- [ ] `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .`
- [ ] `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker`

## Rollback
Revert the schema additions (both schemas), the shared helper, and the matcher propagation in the 3 files. All additive (new optional fields + a helper + copy-on-match) — no migration to unwind.

## Risks / Dependencies
- SERIAL lane (`supply_chain.py`) — light touch in `_make_finding` only; never co-wave with another supply_chain.py ticket.
- Frozen-by-reference schemas — additive-only (no new required, no constraint removal).
- **BLOCKS** the sec-report coverage-matrix ticket (part 2, split out) — it needs att_ck/atlas to exist + be populated.

## Commit Message Format
`feat(deps-scan): att_ck/atlas + disputed finding fields + KB-xref matcher propagation (wh-5ox.10)`
notes:
GROOMED 2026-06-12 — VERDICT: NEEDS SPLIT (Policy 3: 5 skills — _shared, ai-attack-kb, sec-report, sec-triage, deps-scan; 3 coupled parts).

RECOMMENDED 2-WAY SPLIT (execute via /design-ticket, never ad-hoc bd create; confirm granularity with operator):
- (A) KEEP wh-5ox.10 = parts (1)+(3): att_ck/atlas + disputed FIELDS on _shared/reference/finding-schema.json + ai-attack-kb/kb-entry-schema.json + matcher propagation (sec-triage + deps-scan/scripts/normalize_deps.py + supply_chain.py) + _shared validators/tests. Cohesive: 2 fields, one schema pass + one matcher pass.
- (B) NEW sec-report ticket = part (2): coverage_matrix.py (ATT&CK/ATLAS coverage matrix -> SECURITY-REPORT.md, pure aggregation reusing evals/score.py:94 by_category). BLOCKED BY wh-5ox.10 (needs the att_ck/atlas field).
- Alt 3-way by layer (schema / matcher / report) if (1)+(3) too big.

CORRECTIONS:
1. kb-entry-schema.json is at ai-attack-kb/kb-entry-schema.json (NOT _shared/reference/ — ticket hedged it).
2. finding-schema.json:39 additionalProperties:false CONFIRMED accurate — att_ck/atlas/disputed must be added EXPLICITLY ($defs.finding at :37).
3. DRIFT (Policy 7, post-wh-5ox.9 landed today): KB xref[] now holds '<ID> [primary-sourced: <url>]' strings MIXING OWASP (LLM03:2025) + MITRE (AML.*/T*). Matcher MUST filter to MITRE codes (AML.*/T*) AND strip/handle the [primary-sourced: url] suffix (source: ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain-1.md xref).
4. Template gap: ADD explicit Steps + Quality Gates sections (currently thin/off-template).

BASELINES GREEN: _shared 174, deps-scan 212, sec-report 22, sec-triage 9.

title: Additive watchlist row fields: last_safe_version + related_kb + mitre_techniques [supply_chain.py lane]
description:
## Goal / Problem
Add **three additive, loader-NEUTRAL fields** to the watchlist entry schema so each compromised-package row carries richer remediation + attribution, WITHOUT touching the `malware_db.py` loader:
1. `affected[].database_specific.last_safe_version` (string) — last known-safe version, surfaced in `signal_s8`'s recommendation (weave into the `_F4["S8"]` remediation-ladder entry at `supply_chain.py:93-97`).
2. `database_specific.related_kb` (relative path to `ai-attack-kb/reference/<class>.md`) with a resolve-check (mirror `validate_findings --check-kb-refs`) — a dangling ref FAILS CI.
3. `database_specific.mitre_techniques` (string[]) mirroring the linked KB entry's xref; PLUS add supply-chain ATLAS/ATT&CK codes (`AML.T0010`; `T1195.002` / `T1552.005` cross-refs) to the supply-chain KB pages' `xref` (zero schema change).

**Loader-neutral, additive:** `database_specific` already has `additionalProperties:true` (`watchlist-entry-schema.json:88`) and `affected[].items` already has `additionalProperties:true` (`:33`), so `malware_db.py` is UNTOUCHED. NOTE: `affected[].database_specific` is schema-valid TODAY only via that `additionalProperties:true` — it is NOT a currently-named property; this ticket ADDS it as a new named OPTIONAL property under `affected[].items.properties`. Strictly additive (no new `required`, no constraint removal) per ADR-026 §2 (frozen-by-reference — do NOT fork the shape).

## Background / Context
- `watchlist-entry-schema.json` + `validate_watchlist.py` are FROZEN gate files (`confine_self_writes.py` `FROZEN_BASENAMES`, ADR-026 §3) — the outer-loop agent is blocked from editing them; a human-gated DEV wave editing them is intentional + operator-visible. The change is additive-only and does NOT weaken the DATA gate (it can only TIGHTEN — a dangling `related_kb` fails CI).
- `signal_s8` is at `supply_chain.py:1232`; its recommendation surfaces from the F4 remediation-ladder entry `_F4["S8"]` at `supply_chain.py:93-97` — that is where `last_safe_version` is woven into the "pin to X" guidance. (Corrected from the stale `normalize_deps.py:43`, which is the no-fixed-version fallback, not a pin-to-X pattern.)

## PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE (binding)
Per row, `last_safe_version` + the `affected` set come from the PRIMARY advisory (GHSA/OSV/CVE), NOT a community version list. Verify each MITRE technique id against the CANONICAL matrix before hardcoding — ATLAS `AML.T0010` ≠ ATT&CK enterprise `T1195.002`/`T1552.005` (map deliberately). Every MITRE id is `[primary-sourced: <canonical MITRE url>]` or to-be-verified; never an unsourced copy.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — failing tests: `validate_watchlist.py` rejects a dangling `related_kb` AND accepts a valid one; a row carrying all 3 new fields validates; `signal_s8` recommendation includes `last_safe_version` when present, unchanged when absent.
2. **GREEN** — add the 3 optional fields to the schema (under `database_specific` + a new named `affected[].items.properties.database_specific`); add the optional-field + `related_kb` resolve checks to `validate_watchlist.py`; weave `last_safe_version` into `_F4["S8"]`; add the MITRE xrefs to the 3 KB pages.
3. **REFACTOR** — keep additive; both-ways test pins (Policy 9).

## Steps
1. Schema: add `last_safe_version` (string) + `related_kb` (string) + `mitre_techniques` (string[]) optional under `database_specific`; add a named optional `database_specific` under `affected[].items.properties`.
2. Validator: `validate_watchlist.py` gains optional-field type checks + a `related_kb` resolve-check (dangling → fail).
3. signal_s8: surface `last_safe_version` in the `_F4["S8"]` recommendation (`supply_chain.py:93-97`).
4. KB: add the primary-source-verified MITRE codes to `supply-chain-{1,2,3}.md` `xref`.
5. Tests + gates green.

## Files to Modify
- `_shared/reference/watchlist-entry-schema.json` (additive optional fields)
- `_shared/scripts/validate_watchlist.py` + `_shared/scripts/tests/`
- `deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py` (signal_s8 / `_F4["S8"]` recommendation only) + `deps-scan/scripts/tests/`
- `ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain-{1,2,3}.md` (additive `xref` MITRE codes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A row with `last_safe_version` + `related_kb` + `mitre_techniques` validates (`== valid`); a dangling `related_kb` FAILS (`!= valid`) — both ways (Policy 9).
- [ ] `signal_s8` recommendation includes `last_safe_version` when present; unchanged when absent (both-ways).
- [ ] `malware_db.py` loader UNTOUCHED (no loader test regresses).
- [ ] Each MITRE id primary-source-verified against the canonical matrix (ATLAS vs ATT&CK mapped deliberately).
- [ ] Strictly additive — no new `required`, no constraint removal (ADR-026 §2).

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q`
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests -q`
- [ ] `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .`
- [ ] `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker`

## QA Before Push
- [ ] A real (primary-sourced) watchlist row round-trips through `validate_watchlist.py` + a `scan()` that surfaces `last_safe_version` in the S8 recommendation.
- [ ] No `[example-unverified]` MITRE id hardcoded.

## Rollback
Revert the 4 files; all changes are additive (new optional schema fields + validator checks + a recommendation string + KB xref entries) — no migration to unwind.

## Risks / Dependencies
- FROZEN gate files — additive-only; the DATA gate is not weakened (only tightened). Operator-visible wave.
- Scope **KEEP-AS-ONE** (do NOT split): the schema field, its validator check, its `signal_s8` surfacing, and the KB xref are load-bearing for each other — splitting yields broken intermediate states (a schema field with no validator, or a validator for an undefined field). The KB-xref piece is independently splittable but too small to warrant its own ticket.
- Primary-source gate on per-row data + MITRE ids.

## Commit Message Format
`feat(deps-scan): additive watchlist fields last_safe_version + related_kb + mitre_techniques (wh-5ox.9)`
notes: GROOMED 2026-06-12 — VERDICT: READY (re-groomed to template + corrections applied). Corrected stale normalize_deps.py:43 to supply_chain.py:93-97 (_F4 S8 recommendation); clarified affected[].database_specific is a NEW additive named property (valid today via additionalProperties true at schema :88/:33, not currently named); added full Quality Gates + Steps + Rollback. FROZEN schema/validator change is ADDITIVE-only (does NOT weaken the DATA gate; ADR-026 §2). Scope KEEP-AS-ONE (schema/validator/signal_s8/KB load-bearing for each other). Primary-source gate on per-row data + MITRE ids (ATLAS AML.T0010 distinct from ATT&CK T1195.002/T1552.005). Baselines green: deps-scan 204, _shared 161. File-disjoint from wh-evr (hooks).
close_reason: All ACs met: additive watchlist fields (last_safe_version + related_kb resolve-check + mitre_techniques) + S8 surfacing + KB MITRE xref (primary-sourced). Gates green: _shared 173, deps-scan 211, manifest + plugin-validate. QA 4-tier + TL reviewed; strictly additive (ADR-026 §2), loader-neutral. Follow-ups: wh-5ox-fu2 (mitre pattern + CLI kb-dir). white-hacker dogfood DEFERRED (no --plugin-dir this session).

title: Deadman-switch + environment-gated-payload + kill-switch detections [supply_chain.py lane]
description:
## Goal
(1) Static install-script SIGNATURE in supply_chain.py for the deadman-switch installer: gh-token-monitor / com.user.gh-token-monitor + a TTL/interval pair + an api.github.com/user Bearer token-check + a 40[0-9] branch + loginctl enable-linger / systemd/user / LaunchAgents + rm -rf.
(2) KB detection bullets (supply-chain-3.md) + a 1-line core-checklist.md §8 gate+sink note so sec-vuln-scan sweeps APP code (not just install scripts) for: an environment-gated destructive payload (locale/TZ/region read — Intl.DateTimeFormat, process.env.TZ/LANG, navigator.language, geoip — GUARDING a destructive sink, as a gate+sink CORRELATION, not a lone-sink HIGH); a destructive sink on an auth-failure/HTTP-4xx/token-revoked path; an execution-skip conditional keyed to attacker-controlled response content (keyword kill-switch / sandbox-evasion).
PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE: the wiper is DISPUTED (StepSecurity vs Socket) — KEEP it disputed, ship signal-only, do not adjudicate; verify trigger conditions vs a primary sample; tune the gate+sink correlation on a labeled corpus before raising severity (lone-sink is too FP-heavy).

## Files (File Ownership — SERIAL on supply_chain.py)
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py + tests/test_supply_chain.py
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain-3.md [SHARED w/ T6 -> serialize]
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/core-checklist.md (§8 one-liner) [SHARED w/ T6,T11 -> serialize]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q → PASS
- [ ] deadman-installer fixture trips the signature; a benign locale read ALONE does not; gate+sink correlation documented (signal-only)
- [ ] the wiper stays labeled DISPUTED in the KB bullet

## Rollback
Revert the signature + KB/checklist edits + tests.

## References
EPIC wh-5ox; supply-chain-3.md:18,20; backlog #8. SERIAL with Lane D + sibling S6 tickets.

title: GitHub Actions secrets-exfil + OIDC-exchange workflow-lint (signal-not-block)
description:
## Goal
Deterministic workflow-lint over .github/workflows/*.yml (routed via sec-detect, which already scopes .github/workflows): (a) flag toJSON(secrets) whole-context serialization co-located with actions/upload-artifact or an outbound POST; (b) flag a release/publish job combining permissions.id-token:write + an OIDC token-exchange URL (npm/v1/oidc/token/exchange) + a publish step naming a hardcoded target package. Signal-not-block (toJSON(secrets) + trusted-publishing have rare legit uses); route to triage; frame 'provenance/OIDC present != safe'.
PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE: confirm the malicious action pin + named target package list vs the primary GitHub/npm advisory; named packages land as WATCHLIST rows (S8 catches a resolved hijacked version), NOT hardcoded greps; toJSON(secrets) + oidc/token/exchange are durable structural tells — verify the legit-matrix-use FP rate before tuning severity.

## Files (File Ownership)
- CREATE plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/workflow_lint.py
- CREATE plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests/test_workflow_lint.py
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/infra.md ('what to grep for' entry)

## Workflow
RED: toJSON(secrets)->upload-artifact fixture trips; a benign matrix workflow does not. GREEN. REFACTOR.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests/test_workflow_lint.py -q → PASS
- [ ] malicious workflow = signal; benign matrix = none; malformed yml degrades clean
- [ ] signal-not-block; named-pkg IOCs filed as watchlist rows (not in the grep)

## Rollback
Delete the new module+tests + revert the infra.md entry.

## References
EPIC wh-5ox; infra.md:39-55; supply-chain-2.md:20; backlog #7.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-11: REFRAME (Policy 2). No deterministic IaC-scan skill exists — IaC today = the agent applying _shared/reference/infra.md via sec-vuln-scan's IaC partition (sec-vuln-scan is SKILL.md-only). PRIMARY deliverable = add the toJSON(secrets)+upload-artifact and OIDC-token-exchange patterns to infra.md (matches current architecture). The standalone workflow_lint.py is an OPTIONAL follow-up that needs a NEW iac-scan skill home — split it out if pursued. Stays Wave 1 (infra.md doc; disjoint). Named-pkg IOCs -> watchlist rows, not greps.
close_reason: Reframed per groom: toJSON(secrets)-exfil + OIDC-token-exchange patterns added to infra.md (signal-not-block; named pkgs->watchlist). Manifest green. Standalone workflow_lint.py = follow-up (needs iac-scan skill).

title: Static-source-vs-EDR scope boundary + host-IR advisory (docs/process)
description:
## Goal
Document the boundary between STATIC-source review and runtime/EDR/host indicators, and capture a host-IR recovery advisory — both DOCUMENTATION only (no floor-signal change).
(1) sec-threat-model (+ a clause in exclusion-rules.md/core-checklist.md): file-write-hash telemetry, live C2 network/DNS, and process-memory scraping (/proc/<pid>/mem) are runtime/EDR/host indicators OUT of static-source scope and must route to a host/CI check — never claimed as static coverage. Add a containment note: DNS-block is structurally insufficient vs a decentralized canister dead-drop (*.raw.icp0.io, canister-id ending -cai) — egress-allowlist is the only lever.
(2) Append a host-IR recovery note to supply-chain-3.md (tagged 'host-level — advisory only'): isolate -> confirm daemon dead -> revoke from another host -> don't power off (preserve memory); LaunchAgent + systemd-user removal-before-rotate; rotation scope (GitHub PAT -> npm publish tokens + registry-log audit -> AWS Secrets Mgr all regions); immutable-config trap (chattr +i / chflags uchg); exfil-repo discovery.

## PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE (Policy 7)
IR ordering comes from a single DISPUTED community repo — adopt isolate-FIRST as primary; any third-party 'safe-revocation endpoint' is C2-shaped and AGAINST our no-egress posture — do NOT recommend it (one cautionary footnote max). Verify the canister host pattern + runtime IOCs vs a primary source; label UA/60s-poll low-durability. NOTE: the watchlist IOC-TTL idea is DEFERRED (no infra/C2 target kind exists yet — nothing to expire); record as design intent only.

## Files (File Ownership)
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-threat-model/SKILL.md (scope-boundary note)
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain-3.md (advisory recovery note) [SHARED w/ T8 -> serialize]
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/core-checklist.md (1-line) [SHARED w/ T8,T11 -> serialize]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] grep -q 'EDR' / 'static-source' scope statement in sec-threat-model ; supply-chain-3.md carries the 'advisory only' recovery block ; no third-party revocation endpoint recommended
- [ ] uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .  → OK (docs-only, no code/signal change)

## Rollback
Revert the doc edits.

## References
EPIC wh-5ox; ADR-024 (no-egress); supply-chain-3.md:20 (isolate-before-rotate); backlog #6.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-11: READY. All MODIFY targets exist (sec-threat-model/SKILL.md, supply-chain-3.md, core-checklist.md). Docs-only, surgical. Shares supply-chain-3.md/core-checklist.md with Wave-2 .8/.11 (already serialized; .6 lands first).
close_reason: Docs: static-vs-EDR scope boundary (sec-threat-model) + host-IR advisory (supply-chain-3.md, tagged advisory-only, isolate-first, no egress endpoint, disputed stays disputed) + core-checklist xref. Manifest green; QA+WH PASS.

title: Harden Gate-2 Bash write-channel: structural token-match (close the >| / xargs heuristic residual) — before wh-k6l
description:
## Goal / Problem
From the wh-hxt.6 round-2 security re-verify (2026-06-11). `gate_data_edit`'s Bash write-target extractor
(`_bash_write_targets`/`_REDIR_RE`/`_SEPARATOR_RE`, ~gate_data_edit.py:48-106) is a **heuristic tripwire**
(the ADR-016 family — "Bash-command parsing is heuristic… a tripwire, not the boundary", ARD.md:186-188).
It blocks the common redirect/launder forms but leaves **2 verified full-chain evasions** that land poison
on the canonical watchlist inode:
1. **`>|` noclobber-override** (HIGH as a clean one-token bypass): `echo POISON >| <watchlist>` — the path
   class stops at `|` and `_SEPARATOR_RE` splits on `|`, so the redirect+path is shredded. Same regex in all
   three gates → none catch it.
2. **`xargs` arg-laundering** (MED): `echo <watchlist> | xargs -I{} cp /tmp/p {}` — the path is an arg to
   `echo`, never a lexical write target; `xargs` is a skipped wrapper.

**ACCEPTABLE to ship wh-hxt.6** (the security seat's ratified verdict): mooted in practice by HARD-ORDERING
(the watchlist file does not exist until wh-k6l), `confine_self_writes`'s `realpath` write boundary
(confine_self_writes.py:69), and the terminal human-PR gate (ADR-012, never auto-merge). The tests are
honest (the gap is xfail-visible, not false-green). But it MUST be hardened before wh-k6l makes the file live.

## Scope (preferred = STRUCTURAL, not more heuristic)
Replace the redirect-pattern arms race with a structural rule in `gate_data_edit`: for a Bash command that
contains ANY write/redirect verb (`>`/`>>`/`>|`/`&>`/tee/cp/mv/install/ln/rsync/dd/sed -i/perl -i/truncate/
xargs-launder), match the DATA suffix (normpath'd) against **EVERY shlex token** in the command — there is no
benign reason for an in-session Bash WRITE to name the watchlist path → fail-closed block. Caveat: scope to
write-verb commands so a legitimate `cat known-compromised.osv.json` READ isn't blocked (reads go via the Read
tool anyway). This collapses the entire `>|`/xargs/launder/redirect evasion class to one rule and removes the
tripwire residual for this specific path. Files: `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/gate_data_edit.py` + its tests
(flip the 2 xfail cases from wh-hxt.6 to pass).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `echo POISON >| <watchlist>` and `echo <watchlist> | xargs … cp /tmp/p {}` → BLOCKED (exit 2), no verdict.
- [ ] A legitimate `cat <watchlist>` (read) is NOT blocked; a canonical validator+Write under a bound KEEP still admits.
- [ ] The wh-hxt.6 xfail tests (`>|`, xargs) flip to passing (strict xfail → un-xfail).
- [ ] Hooks suite green; manifest + plugin validate green.

## Notes / Dependencies
- **MUST land before wh-k6l** creates `known-compromised.osv.json` (wired as a wh-k6l blocker). Sibling to wh-hxt.6 (which shipped the acceptable-residual version).
- Refs: the wh-hxt.6 round-2 security report (gate_data_edit.py:48-106 the gap; ARD.md:186-188 / ADR-016 the tripwire-class precedent); confine_self_writes.py:69 (the realpath boundary that moots it pre-wh-k6l).
notes:
GROOMED 2026-06-11 (tech-lead, EMPIRICAL — ran the regexes/shlex against all 4 shapes). VERDICT: NEEDS UPDATE → corrections below; READY to implement once applied (dep wh-k6l←.15 wired, confirmed).

DESIGN DECISION — PreToolUse PREVENT, layered (reject pure-A token-match AND pure-B "just add >|"):
(i) GRAMMAR FIX for >|: "add >| to _REDIR_RE" is INSUFFICIENT alone — `_SEPARATOR_RE` splits the bare `|` in `>|` FIRST (shreds `echo X >| w` into ['echo X >',' w'] before `_REDIR_RE` runs). Need BOTH: `_SEPARATOR_RE` lookbehind `(?<!>)\|` + `_REDIR_RE` `>>?\|?`. Apply to ALL THREE hooks (same shape): gate_data_edit.py:48-49, gate_kb_edit.py:28, confine_self_writes.py:55-56.
(ii) STRUCTURAL fail-closed (the real catch-all): for a Bash command containing a write-verb (> >> >| &> tee cp mv install ln rsync dd sed-i perl-i truncate) OR an interpreter/launder verb (python/python3/perl/ruby/node/sh/bash -c, eval, xargs, ed), SUBSTRING-scan EVERY shlex token for a DATA_SEGMENTS suffix → fail-closed block. SUBSTRING (not exact-token): catches `python3 -c "open('…/known-compromised.osv.json','w')"` — the path is INSIDE the quoted -c token, exact-token match misses it. SCOPE to write/interpreter verbs so a bare `cat <watchlist>` READ is NOT blocked (verified false-positive). One rule collapses >| + xargs + python-c + ed.
(iii) PostToolUse content-binding backstop = OPTIONAL defense-in-depth → SPLIT to a separate ticket (wh-hxt.16). PostToolUse fires AFTER the write = DETECT not PREVENT; never the primary net for a shape PreToolUse can block; needs a NEW hook + hooks.json(CONTROL) + a last-consumed-verdict-bytes side-channel. NOT in this ticket; NOT a wh-k6l blocker.

STRIKE the body's now-FALSIFIED claims (dogfood + this groom): "mooted by confine realpath (confine_self_writes.py:69)" is WRONG — the watchlist JSON is inside confine ALLOW_SEGMENTS (:48, /_shared/reference/); only the validator/schema BASENAMES are FROZEN (:29-37), so confine ALLOWS the write by design → gate_data_edit is the SOLE Bash net. Moot reduces to HARD-ORDERING (no watchlist file until wh-k6l, gated on THIS) + ADR-012 human-PR only. Residual = 4 shapes (>|, python-c, xargs, ed), NOT 2.

FILES TO MODIFY (one ticket): gate_data_edit.py (regex :48-49 + the write/interpreter substring-scan in the Bash branch :85-156) · gate_kb_edit.py (:28 regex) · confine_self_writes.py (:55-56 regex) · tests/test_gate_data_edit.py (un-xfail the 2 existing :384/:390; add python-c + ed as PASS tests; add a `cat <watchlist>` READ-not-blocked test) · tests/test_gate_kb_edit.py + tests/test_confine_self_writes.py (a >| block test each). NOT hooks.json / a new hook (that's the split). Editing confine_self_writes.py is IN-LANE for a dev (FROZEN_BASENAMES constrains the OUTER LOOP self-editing its harness, not a human-PR'd dev change — ADR-016).

ACs (runnable, both directions): all 4 shapes → BLOCKED (gd.decide exit 2); the 2 strict-xfails un-xfail'd and PASS (suite shows no xpassed/xfailed); `cat <watchlist>` READ → allowed; a canonical validator+Write under a bound KEEP still admits; full hooks suite green; manifest + plugin validate green; `bd dep tree wh-k6l` still lists wh-hxt.15 as blocker.

— RE-VERIFY 2026-06-11 (pre-launch groom, post wh-g2v): cited file:line still hold (±2 drift): gate_data_edit _SEPARATOR_RE:50 _REDIR_RE:51 _bash_write_targets:85; confine _SEPARATOR_RE:55 _REDIR_RE:56, ALLOW_SEGMENTS:48 confirmed includes `/_shared/reference/` (STRIKE stands), FROZEN_BASENAMES:29-37; xfail tests at :384/:391 strict=True. Hooks suite green NOW: 182 passed, 2 xfailed (the >| + xargs residuals) — exactly the two ACs flip to pass. REFINEMENT to fix-(i): gate_kb_edit.py has NO _SEPARATOR_RE — it runs `_REDIR_RE.finditer` on the WHOLE command (line 61, no split), so for THAT hook only the `_REDIR_RE` `>>?\|?` arm applies (no lookbehind needed); the lookbehind+REDIR pair is for gate_data_edit + confine_self_writes only. Dep wh-k6l←wh-hxt.15 still wired (bd blocked). VERDICT: READY.
close_reason: Gate-2 Bash write-channel hardened: >| grammar fix in all 3 hooks (gate_data_edit/confine both-arm; gate_kb_edit redir-arm only) + structural token-match catch-all in gate_data_edit with _collapse_path_seps normpath-parity (FINDING-1 closed). Hooks 195 passed (no xpass/xfail), manifest + plugin validate OK. QA PASS + WH security sign-off.

title: Doc honesty: qualify the 'stays-current' currency claims (README/PRD) to ARCHITECTURE's build-state discipline (Hades RCA)
description:
## Goal / Problem
The Hades dogfood RCA (docs/research/20260610_hades_shai_hulud_pypi.md §5) proved the "self-improving /
keeps-current" claim is **operationally aspirational**: the currency arm never self-fires. ARCHITECTURE.md is
already HONEST about this (build-state block :13-24 "registration pending human-auth T-8.3"; :247 "design
intent, not yet wired"), but README + PRD still read as if the currency arm is DELIVERED:
- `README.md:6` "keeps its AI-attack knowledge current over time" (front-door promise, unqualified).
- `README.md:79,144-145` the self-improving / "refreshes from authoritative threat feeds" bullets.
- `docs/PRD.md:243` FR-16 "A scheduled routine polls…" + `:389` M3 "refresh routine processes feed deltas on
  cadence" — present-tense, reads as a met requirement.

Per Rule 12 (fail loud — "completed" is wrong if a step was skipped) + the ADR-028 precedent (we corrected
docs that claimed an unshipped distribution), qualify these to match ARCHITECTURE's build-state discipline.
NOT self-flagellation — README stays a confident front door WITH an honest "what's operational vs designed"
note, exactly as ARCHITECTURE carries one.

## Scope (Files to Modify)
- `README.md` — qualify the opening promise (:6) + the self-improving bullets (:69,79,144) with a one-line
  build-state note: the loop is DESIGNED; autonomous currency is human-triggered today, pending the wh-hxt.12
  mechanism decision + the wh-hxt.8 capture-hook registration. Keep the aspirational framing, add the truth.
- `docs/PRD.md` — mark FR-16/FR-17/M3 status (designed/pending-wiring), mirroring ARCHITECTURE's build-state
  rather than reading as delivered. (FRs are requirements/targets — keep them, but don't present them as MET.)
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — additive: add the NEW designed-pending items the RCA surfaced to §3.6/§5/§7.1 — the
  AI-config-poisoning detection (wh-hxt.11), campaign-lineage tracking (wh-hxt.10 + the campaign_family field),
  and the first-detector feeds (wh-hxt.9). Do NOT claim them built.
- `.claude/CLAUDE.md` (Architecture at a glance) — only if it overclaims; light.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README + PRD no longer present the scheduled currency arm as operational; each carries a build-state /
      status note consistent with ARCHITECTURE (grep: README has a "build state"/"designed vs operational"
      qualifier near the self-improving claim; PRD FR-16/M3 carry a status marker).
- [ ] No NEW capability (config-poisoning detection, campaign-lineage, new feeds) is claimed as DONE anywhere —
      they appear only as designed/pending with their ticket refs (wh-hxt.9/.10/.11/.12).
- [ ] Cross-references the Hades research doc + the RCA root causes.
- [ ] Rule 12 honesty: a reader cannot conclude the agent autonomously stays current today.

## Notes
Driven by the Hades RCA (RC1 the open loop). Distinct from building the fix (wh-hxt.8/.9/.10/.11/.12) — this
is the HONESTY pass over the claims. Pairs with ADR-028 (the manual-install honesty precedent). Groom before
claiming; verify the exact README/PRD line numbers (they drift). Refs: docs/research/20260610_hades_shai_hulud_pypi.md,
README.md:6, docs/PRD.md:243,389, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:13-24,247.
close_reason: Closed

title: KB AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003 (Hades/Miasma PyPI) — dated entry, lineage-as-PROSE + xref to -002, DISPUTED wiper in body (RC3)
description:
**ROOT CAUSE RC3** (docs/research/20260610_hades_shai_hulud_pypi.md). Two parts.

(a) A dated KB entry AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003 for the Hades/Miasma PyPI wave: technique_class supply-chain; campaign_family: shai-hulud (link to SUPPLY-CHAIN-002); the AI-config-poisoning persistence vector (.claude/setup.mjs etc.); the union IOC package set (Socket+StepSecurity — neither is complete); the wiper marked **DISPUTED** (StepSecurity says rm -rf on token-4xx/72h TTL; Socket says label-only credential-stealer — adjudicate at payload level, do NOT pick a side). PROVENANCE WRINKLE: no GHSA yet → source = the vendor posts + OSV.dev malicious-packages; the validate_kb provenance rule must accept these (coordinate with the wh-hxt.5 Gate-2 provenance allowlist note in the research doc §5).

(b) A re-poll trigger for ACTIVE campaign FAMILIES — a static review_by (90d) can't track a family that re-waves in days. Design a 'family is hot → short review_by / re-poll the first-detector feeds for new waves' mechanism (feeds RC1's routine). Goes through Gate-1 (KB) + the validator. Groom before claiming.
notes:
GROOM 2026-06-10 (tech-lead, read-only) — VERDICT: NEEDS SPLIT.
(a) the KB entry AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003 = READY NOW with ONE change: DROP `campaign_family`. It FAILS the
    closed schema (kb-entry-schema.json:7 additionalProperties:false) — per ADR-019 (ARD.md:247 'closed
    vocabulary grows by an appended ADR, never silently') a closed-schema FIELD addition needs an APPENDED
    ADR, NOT an inline DATA edit. Express lineage as PROSE in title/body + an `xref` to AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002
    (mirrors supply-chain-2.md:40). technique_class `supply-chain` is already a valid enum member (:22).
    PROVENANCE PASSES validate_kb TODAY — rule is presence + `^https?://` only (schema:37), NO host allowlist
    (test_validate_kb.py:94 blocks only ftp://). A Socket/StepSecurity/OSV-sourced entry validates fine; the
    GHSA-vs-vendor allowlist concern is wh-hxt.5's validate_watchlist (WATCHLIST path, ADR-026 §1 SEC-Q4),
    NOT here. DISPUTED wiper -> state in BODY + a detections[] line (StepSecurity 4xx/72h `rm -rf` vs Socket
    label-only stealer; UNRESOLVED; isolate-before-rotate); do NOT average into `confidence` (Rule 7).
    review_by ~30d (2026-07-10) as an honest 'hot family' staleness flag (a flag, not a mechanism).
    Directory-globbed (_kb_entries.py:29) — no registry to update.
(b) the campaign-family RE-POLL mechanism -> FOLD into the wh-hxt.8 currency-arm lane (impl-task post-spike);
    nothing fires it until the routine is wired. SPUN OUT: `campaign_family` typed field = a deferred P3
    (its own ADR + kb-entry-schema.json + SKILL.md mirror).
.11 depends-on THIS entry (wh-hxt.11 cross-refs AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003; .10 OWNS it).
=> wh-hxt.10 is now scoped to (a): ship the entry (no campaign_family) — READY to claim.
close_reason: Closed

title: Feed gap (RC2): add Socket.dev + StepSecurity + Phylum (supply-chain campaign first-detectors) to si-07 feed catalog
description:
**ROOT CAUSE RC2** (docs/research/20260610_hades_shai_hulud_pypi.md §5). The two firms that BROKE Hades — Socket.dev + StepSecurity — are absent from docs/research/si-07-threat-feeds.md, even though we hand-cited StepSecurity in the ADR-024 strategy doc without wiring them as a feed. OSV/GHSA lag campaign detection by days; the 33-vs-37 package split between the two vendors proves single-source IOC lists are partial.

Scope (doc-only): add to si-07 — Socket.dev research blog (RSS), StepSecurity blog (RSS), Phylum/ReversingLabs (evaluate). Note each is a supply-chain-CAMPAIGN tier feed (distinct from advisory-tier OSV/GHSA and AI-technique-tier Embrace The Red). These flow into sec-kb-refresh's poll list (RC1). Verify each feed URL. Refs: si-07, the Hades research doc.
notes:
GROOM 2026-06-10 (tech-lead, read-only) — VERDICT: READY (doc-only, single file; Policy 3 clean).
CORRECTIONS: si-07:5 columns are |Priority|Name|Type|Machine-readable|Cadence|Covers|URL| (NOT the body's
'tier|source|type|format|cadence|notes|url'). Priority vocab is FIXED {core,optional,recommended} — map the
new feeds as Priority=core (RC2 closes the OSV/GHSA lag; not optional), Type=blog/tracker (cf. MS/Google
:20-21), and put 'supply-chain campaign first-detector' DESCRIPTIVELY in the Covers cell (no new tier token).
KEY: sec-kb-refresh does NOT consume si-07 in code (poll_feeds.py:69 PARSERS={osv,atlas,atom}, CLI-driven;
never reads si-07). RSS->parse_atom already handles these — NO new parser. The fetch-loop wiring is wh-hxt.8's
job; adding rows is valid zero-risk PREP. VERIFICATION BAR (Policy 11): every si-07 row carries an inline
'VERIFIED: <live RSS/atom + recent dated item>' note (15/16 rows) — impl MUST WebFetch each new URL and write
it. OPEN CHOICE: Phylum was acquired by ReversingLabs (2025) — pick the LIVE feed (likely ReversingLabs blog
RSS; Phylum = historical name); Socket.dev + StepSecurity unambiguous (both WebFetch-verified in the Hades
doc :10-12). Expected URLs: socket.dev/blog, stepsecurity.io/blog, ReversingLabs research (verify RSS).
SOFT DEP wh-hxt.8 (consumed once .8 wires fetch; NOT a hard blocker — .9 lands independently).

title: Wire the vendor-lane scrub guard (install/tests) into CI — it never runs, masking ADR-022 leaks
description:
## Goal / Problem
The vendor-lane scrub guard — install/tests/test_scrub_vendored.py (11 tests, incl.
test_real_vendored_tree_has_zero_leaks, the ADR-022 leakage drift-guard) — NEVER runs in CI. Wire it
into .github/workflows/ci.yml so any future dev-repo-token leak into the vendored payload fails the
build (Policy 12: fail loud) instead of shipping with CI green.

## Background / Context
- The escape (proof): wave-1a shipped supply-chain-2.md:12 with a forbidden docs/research/… token in
  metadata.source and CI was GREEN. Caught only by a manual run during wave-1b launch prep — now the
  content-fix ticket wh-tqy.
- Root cause — gate-coverage gap: ci.yml:37-52 discovers test packages with
  `find plugins/white-hacker evals packaging -name pyproject.toml …` (:40). install/ is missed TWICE:
  not under the three roots AND deliberately flat (conftest.py sys.path shim at :5, no pyproject) —
  run as `uv run --with pytest pytest install/tests/` (documented at test_scrub_vendored.py:3).
- ADR-022 (docs/ARD.md:271, :279 "the real-tree leakage guard, 11 tests"): an unrun drift-guard is no guard.
- Design choice — option (a), a dedicated CI step (CHOSEN): mirrors the local invocation exactly;
  minimal (Policy 2). REJECTED option (b) — adding install/pyproject.toml so the find-loop auto-sweeps
  it — wrong shape for a deliberately-flat installer dir and changes its documented invocation. The
  test job's steps (manifest validate :34-35, per-package loop :37-52) make a sibling step the natural
  insertion point; no new job.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
CI config, not application code — NO new test is authored (the 11-test suite IS the artifact being wired).
1. RED — demonstrate the gap: `grep -n "install/tests" .github/workflows/ci.yml` → no match; the suite
   standalone is currently `1 failed, 10 passed` (the wh-tqy leak — do NOT fix it here).
2. GREEN — add the step (literal block below); after wh-tqy: suite → 11 passed; the grep matches.
3. REFACTOR — none (one step, surgical; the find-loop, manifest step, permissions/concurrency untouched).

## Steps (with description)
1. Verify wh-tqy is merged (or lands in the same push) so the first CI run with the step is green.
2. Add the step INSIDE the existing `test` job, AFTER the per-package loop step (insertion point ≈
   after ci.yml:52), reusing the pinned-uv setup (:29-32). Literal block:
```yaml
      - name: Vendor-lane scrub guard (install/)
        run: uv run --with pytest pytest install/tests/ -q
```
   Do NOT add `nice -n 10` in CI (runners are dedicated; resource discipline is a dev-host rule).
3. Local verification per the ACs.
4. Operator-observable: the next push shows the CI run green with the new step in the log.

## Files to Create/Modify
- Modify: .github/workflows/ci.yml (one step in the test job). NO other files. Specifically NOT:
  install/pyproject.toml (rejected option b); install/scrub_vendored.py; supply-chain-2.md (wh-tqy's — do not bundle).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `grep -n "install/tests" .github/workflows/ci.yml` returns the new step
- [ ] The step lives in the existing test job (one top-level `jobs:` block unchanged; no new job)
- [ ] AFTER wh-tqy: `uv run --with pytest pytest install/tests/ -q` → 11 passed (blocked on wh-tqy)
- [ ] `test ! -f install/pyproject.toml` exits 0 (confirms option (a), not (b))
- [ ] (Operator-observable) the next push/PR shows CI green with the step present in the run log

## Quality Gates (must pass before bd close)
ci.yml-only change — the standard per-package Python gates do not apply; the gates here:
- `uv run --with pytest pytest install/tests/ -q` → 11 passed (post wh-tqy)
- `grep -n "install/tests" .github/workflows/ci.yml` → the new step
- YAML parses: `uv run --with pyyaml python -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/ci.yml')); print('yaml ok')"`
  (actionlint/yamllint NOT installed on this host — verified absent; the stdlib-floor probe needs
  --with pyyaml since pyyaml is not stdlib. Adopting actionlint = a separate toolchain decision.)

## QA Before Push
- [ ] Ordering: wh-tqy merged or same-push (first CI run must not be red)
- [ ] Scope: the diff touches ONLY ci.yml
- [ ] No regression: the per-package loop, manifest step, permissions/concurrency (:14-19) unchanged
- [ ] Negative reasoning check: a reintroduced docs/research token in a vendored-source .md would now
      fail this step (the point) — reason it through; do not commit a leak

## Commit Message Format
Repo rule: author Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>, NO AI attribution. Type from the template list
(beads-ticket-template.md:93-99 — `ci:` is NOT listed): suggested
`chore: wire the vendor-lane scrub guard (install/tests) into CI`.

## Risks / Dependencies
- DEPENDS ON wh-tqy (hard ordering: the suite is red at baseline from the supply-chain-2.md:12 leak;
  landing this first turns CI red). Wired at creation: bd dep add <this> wh-tqy.
- Do NOT bundle the content fix (wh-tqy's file scope).
- Future flat (no-pyproject) test dirs would be missed the same way — out of scope; file a follow-up
  only if a second flat dir appears (no speculative generalization, Policy 2).
- The bare `uv run --with pytest` (no --project) invocation relies on install/conftest.py:5's sys.path
  shim — exactly the documented local invocation; verified standalone.

## Rollback
Revert/delete the one added step in ci.yml. No state, no migration; the suite still runs locally.

## Notes / References
ci.yml:37-52 (loop), :40 (the find), :29-32 (pinned uv), :34-35 (manifest step), :14-19 (perms/
concurrency — unchanged) · install/conftest.py:5 · install/tests/test_scrub_vendored.py:3 (the
invocation), :120-127 (the drift-guard) · ADR-022 (docs/ARD.md:271,274-275,279) · wh-tqy (the content
fix this depends on) · .notes/team-launch-wh-5es-wh-xn0-wh-0vx.md:17-26 (the RED-GATE block) ·
ADR-005 caps: N/A (no SKILL.md).
notes:
GROOMED 2026-06-11 (pre-launch, next-wave batch ① with wh-7u7). VERDICT: READY — dependency satisfied, suite green.
- Dependency wh-tqy CLOSED ✓; `uv run --with pytest pytest install/tests/ -q` → 11 passed NOW (the RED baseline from the supply-chain-2.md:12 leak is resolved). So the first CI run with the new step will be GREEN — no same-push ordering risk remains.
- GAP CONFIRMED: `grep -n "install/tests" .github/workflows/ci.yml` → NO MATCH (the scrub guard never runs in CI).
- ci.yml citations accurate: the per-package "Run test suite (per package)" loop is at :37-52, the `find plugins/white-hacker evals packaging -name pyproject.toml -not -path '*/.venv/*'` at :40 (misses install/ — not under those 3 roots AND deliberately flat/no-pyproject). Insertion point = a new step AFTER the loop (≈:52), reusing the pinned-uv setup.
- SCOPE: ci.yml ONLY (one step). NOT install/pyproject.toml (rejected option b — keep install/ flat). File-disjoint from wh-7u7 (sec-detect).
- The standard per-package gates N/A (ci.yml-only); the gates are the grep + the 11-passed suite + a YAML parse (`--with pyyaml`).
