title: Qualify ARCHITECTURE section 8: frontmatter hooks vs plugin hooks.json + 3-way firing table
description:
## Goal / Problem
`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:617` §8 asserts "plugin subagents ignore `permissionMode`/`mcpServers`/`hooks`." This CONFLATES two distinct mechanisms and is misleading: it is true of the subagent FRONTMATTER `hooks:` field, but FALSE for the plugin-scope `hooks.json` (which DOES fire for subagents), and it is SILENT on the operationally critical teammate-vs-subagent divergence (plugin guards fire for subagents but NOT for teammates). Goal: qualify §8 with the correct distinction + the three-way firing table from the wh-sml report. Append-only correction; do NOT re-debate ADR-017.

## Background / Context
wh-sml report `docs/research/20260614_plugin_hooks_subagent_scope.md` §4.3/§4.4/§6 establishes, with live evidence:
- Frontmatter `hooks:` field — ignored for plugin subagents (official docs).
- Plugin-scope `hooks.json` (ADR-017) — FIRES for subagents (§4.1), DOES NOT fire for teammates (§4.2).
- Three-way firing table (top-level / subagent / teammate) — §4.4.

## Steps (with description)
1. Edit `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` §8 (`:617` region): replace/extend the blanket "subagents ignore … hooks" with the two-mechanism distinction (frontmatter `hooks:` field vs plugin-scope `hooks.json`).
2. Add the three-way PreToolUse firing table (top-level FIRES · subagent FIRES · teammate does NOT) from the report §4.4.
3. State the consequence: the white-hacker's primary teammate mode runs un-gated by the plugin guards (cross-link follow-up b).

## Files to Modify
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` (§8, `:617` region) — doc-only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] §8 distinguishes the frontmatter `hooks:` field (ignored for plugin subagents) from the plugin-scope `hooks.json` (fires for subagents, not teammates) — `grep -q "hooks.json" docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` in the §8 region.
- [ ] The three-way firing table (top-level / subagent / teammate) is present.
- [ ] No re-debate of ADR-017 (append-only qualification); cite the wh-sml report.

## Severity / Priority
P3 — documentation correctness; no runtime impact, but the current §8 actively misleads on a security-relevant fact.

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` · `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker` (doc-only; no pytest package touched).

## Rollback
Revert the §8 edit. Doc-only — no migration.

## References
wh-sml report §4.3/§4.4/§6/§8(c); handoff (c). ADR-017 (plugin distribution — NOT re-debated). DO-NOT-COPY: N/A. Commit: `docs(architecture): qualify §8 plugin hooks.json firing (subagent vs teammate)`.

title: Deterministic ATT&CK/ATLAS coverage matrix -> SECURITY-REPORT.md [sec-report]
description:
## Goal / Problem
Add a PURE-aggregation script `sec-report/scripts/coverage_matrix.py` (NO LLM — Policy 5) that joins findings → their `att_ck`/`atlas` ids (added by wh-5ox.10) → groups by ATT&CK/ATLAS technique id → emits a `Technique | ID | covered? | modality | finding-refs` matrix into SECURITY-REPORT.md. An empty cell (a KB technique in scope with ZERO covering finding) is flagged a recall/degradation gap that `/sec-learn` can harvest (outer-loop signal). Reuse the `evals/score.py:94` `by_category` grouping shape.

Split out of wh-5ox.10 (part 2 of its original 3-part scope) — GROOMED 2026-06-12.

## Prerequisites
- **wh-5ox.10 CLOSED** (BLOCKED BY, bd dep wired) — the finding `att_ck`/`atlas` fields must EXIST and be populated by the matcher. Without them this script has nothing to group by.

## Background / Context
- Mirror the existing sec-report script pattern: `sec-report/scripts/ci_gate.py:49` (`main(argv)` + `argparse` + `json.loads(path.read_text())`). Deterministic, stdlib-first (Policy 5; ADR-015 floor).
- Grouping: reuse the by-id aggregation shape from `evals/score.py:94` (`by_category`).
- Input: a findings doc validating against `_shared/reference/finding-schema.json` (now carrying att_ck/atlas). KB technique universe: the MITRE `xref[]` ids across `ai-attack-kb/reference/*.md` (post-wh-5ox.9 these are `"<ID> [primary-sourced: <url>]"` — reuse wh-5ox.10's `mitre_from_xref` helper to extract bare ids).
- Output: a markdown table written into SECURITY-REPORT.md (the sec-report artifact).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — failing tests: coverage_matrix reads a findings doc + the KB technique set, groups findings by att_ck/atlas id, emits the table deterministically; a covered technique shows its finding refs; an in-scope-but-uncovered technique shows an empty/gap cell flagged for `/sec-learn`; no LLM / no network.
2. **GREEN** — implement coverage_matrix.py (argparse + json input, by-id grouping, markdown table emit, gap flagging).
3. **REFACTOR** — both-ways pins (Policy 9).

## Steps
1. CREATE `sec-report/scripts/coverage_matrix.py` — `main(argv)` (mirror `ci_gate.py:49`): load findings JSON, collect att_ck/atlas ids per finding, build the KB technique universe from `ai-attack-kb/reference/*.md` MITRE xref ids (via `mitre_from_xref`), group + emit the `Technique|ID|covered?|modality|finding-refs` markdown table, flag uncovered in-scope techniques.
2. CREATE `sec-report/scripts/tests/test_coverage_matrix.py` — both-ways pins (covered shows refs; in-scope-uncovered flagged as a gap).
3. (optional) note the script in `sec-report/SKILL.md`.

## Files to Create
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-report/scripts/coverage_matrix.py`
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-report/scripts/tests/test_coverage_matrix.py`

## Files to Modify
- (optional) `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-report/SKILL.md` — mention coverage_matrix.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `coverage_matrix.py` emits the `Technique|ID|covered?|modality|finding-refs` table DETERMINISTICALLY (no LLM, no RNG, no network — Policy 5).
- [ ] A technique covered by ≥1 finding shows its finding-refs (`== expected`); an in-scope KB technique with ZERO covering finding is flagged a recall/degradation gap (`!= covered`) — both ways (Policy 9).
- [ ] groups by att_ck/atlas id (reuses the `score.py` `by_category` shape); reads a finding-schema-valid doc.
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-report/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-report/scripts/tests -q` → PASS

## Quality Gates
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-report/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-report/scripts/tests -q`
- [ ] `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .`
- [ ] `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker`

## Rollback
Delete `coverage_matrix.py` + its test (and revert the optional SKILL.md mention). New files only — nothing to unwind.

## Risks / Dependencies
- BLOCKED BY wh-5ox.10 (needs the att_ck/atlas finding field). Cannot start until .10 is closed.
- Pure aggregation — NO LLM (Policy 5); the empty-cell gap is the `/sec-learn` recall signal (outer loop).
- File-disjoint from wh-5ox.10 (`sec-report/` vs `_shared`/`deps-scan`/`sec-triage`) — once .10 lands, this can run solo or co-wave with any non-sec-report ticket.

## Commit Message Format
`feat(sec-report): deterministic ATT&CK/ATLAS coverage_matrix.py (wh-5ox.NN)`

title: Spike: source-or-genericize the gitleaks/Betterleaks successor claim
description:
## Research Question
- Q1. Does an AUTHORITATIVE primary source confirm "Betterleaks" exists as the gitleaks successor/fork, and that the gitleaks maintainer is behind it? (maintainer announcement, gitleaks README/CHANGELOG, a Betterleaks repo with credible provenance, or a reputable advisory — NOT a forum/LLM-synthesized mention)
- Q2. If yes: the canonical URL, and does it confirm BOTH (a) successor-status AND (b) maintainer authorship/endorsement?
- Q3. If no authoritative source: do we (a) genericize every reference to "track a successor" WITHOUT the name, (b) downgrade to clearly-hypothetical phrasing, or (c) keep `[example-unverified]` — which is consistent with the DO-NOT-COPY rule for a SHIPPED reference file?

## Timebox
**Timebox:** 0.5 day (<=4h). If not found in a focused search, that IS the answer (-> Q3); do not extend hunting.

## Background / Context
- The "gitleaks maintainer renamed/forked to **Betterleaks**" claim recurs across ~6 docs + the **SHIPPED** `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/tool-registry.md:90`, where it drives guidance ("track Betterleaks via `sec-kb-refresh`"). An unsourced tooling claim in a shipped reference is exactly what the project's provenance discipline targets.
- wh-hxt.18's dev tagged it `[example-unverified]` in `docs/research/20260612_staleness_signal.md` (no external URL found) and did NOT hardcode it — the staleness signal fires on the deterministic `feature_complete` flag, never the name.
- **Verified vs unverified:** the cadence/feature-complete/MIT/bus-factor facts are ALREADY primary-sourced (`20260609_trivy_replacement_sca_iac.md:72,93`). ONLY the "Betterleaks successor exists + maintainer authored it" narrative is unverified. ("Betterleaks" appears in THIS ticket as the research subject — `[example-unverified]` — not a literal to hardcode.)
- **All occurrences to reconcile** (grep 2026-06-12): `tool-registry.md:90` (SHIPPED); `20260609_trivy_replacement_sca_iac.md:95/97/347/348/527`; `20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md:11/105`; `20260609_tool_admissibility_license_gdpr.md:270/271`; `20260612_staleness_signal.md:96/211/221/225`; `ARD.md:497/534`.
- **Blocks nothing:** wh-hxt.18 closes clean; the code is name-independent. Provenance-tightening only.

## Investigation Approach
1. Targeted 2026 web search: "Betterleaks" + gitleaks successor/fork; check gitleaks GitHub (README/CHANGELOG/pinned issues/maintainer profile) and any Betterleaks repo for a provenance link back to the maintainer — prefer the maintainer's own words over secondary coverage.
2. If found: record canonical URL + confirming quote; verify it supports BOTH Q2 conditions -> deliverable becomes a retag across all 6+ locations.
3. If NOT found in timebox: valid recordable answer -> recommend genericize / downgrade / keep-tagged (pick the shipped-file-provenance-compliant option) and apply consistently.

## Research Guidelines for AI Agents
Web-search recent (2026); structured findings; clear recommendation; do NOT accept LLM-synthesized/forum mentions as primary (the Hades-RCA discipline); the name must NOT be hardcoded anywhere regardless of outcome.

## Expected Deliverables
- [ ] Report `docs/research/<YYYYMMDD>_betterleaks_provenance.md` with verdict SOURCED (URL) or UNSOURCED.
- [ ] A single consistent decision (source / genericize / downgrade) applied across all 6+ locations — or handed to a follow-up `/design-ticket --type=task` if the edit sprawls.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Q1 answered: canonical URL + quote, OR explicit "no authoritative source within timebox".
- [ ] One consistent decision recorded + applied (or routed to a follow-up task) — no location left inconsistent.
- [ ] SHIPPED `tool-registry.md:90` brought into provenance-compliance (sourced or genericized).
- [ ] No occurrence hardcodes "Betterleaks" as a detection literal (prose/example only).
- [ ] Timebox respected (<=0.5d) or extended with justification.

## Notes / Open Questions
- Likely outcome: cadence/feature-complete facts hold; the *specific* successor may lack a single canonical announcement -> genericize-with-tracking is the cleanest fix for the shipped file.
- NON-BLOCKING; wh-hxt.18 can close first. Keep design (this ticket) and execution (the search) separate.
close_reason: Betterleaks provenance spike — deliverable docs/research/20260612_betterleaks_provenance.md shipped (d5cc851). 2026-06-13 dogfood review found NO findings in its scope. Clean.

title: RETIRE/SUPERSEDE: tool-retire + watchlist-supersede paths extending staleness_check.py --archive
description:
## Goal / Problem
Add the RETIRE/SUPERSEDE codepaths the lifecycle lacks: today `staleness_check.py --archive` ages out KB
ENTRIES only — there is NO codepath to retire a stale/compromised TOOL (from SCANNER_PREFERENCE /
tool-registry) nor to supersede a WATCHLIST entry on a newer advisory (e.g. gitleaks→Betterleaks). Add both,
extending the KB-only `--archive` precedent. Split from wh-hxt.1 (part 3 of 3).

## Background / Context
- `ai-attack-kb/scripts/staleness_check.py:26-52`: `archive_entry` + the `--archive` flag MOVE stale KB
  entries to `archive/` (content preserved, never deleted) — KB-only today.
- Watchlist edits go through Gate-2 (the deterministic DATA gate, wh-562 CLOSED) — a supersede/remove MUST
  be Gate-2-validated (per-entry GHSA/OSV provenance + OSV-schema + write-lane).
- Human-gated proposals only (ADR-012, never auto-swap). Consumes wh-hxt.2's retire→replace runbook.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
RED — tests pinning a tool-retire proposal + a watchlist-supersede proposal (`==` proposed, `!=` auto-applied)
→ GREEN — the two paths extending the `--archive` precedent → REFACTOR — surgical.

## Steps
1. Add a tool-retire path: propose retiring a tool from SCANNER_PREFERENCE / tool-registry (consuming the
   wh-hxt.2 runbook), human-gated.
2. Add a watchlist-entry supersede/remove path in the shared OSV shape, Gate-2-validated.
3. Mirror the `staleness_check.py --archive` discipline (preserve, never silently delete; print the proposal).

## Files to Create/Modify
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts/staleness_check.py` — the tool-retire + watchlist-supersede paths (extending the KB-only `--archive`).
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts/tests/` — tests for both paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A tool RETIRE path + a watchlist-entry SUPERSEDE path exist, extending `staleness_check.py`'s KB-only `--archive` precedent.
- [ ] Watchlist edits are Gate-2-validated (per-entry provenance + OSV-schema + write-lane).
- [ ] Human-gated — never auto-swaps (ADR-012); pinned by a test (`==` proposes, `!=` applies).
- [ ] Package gate green · `validate_manifest.py` green · `claude plugin validate` green.

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

## Rollback
`git revert` the new paths + tests.

## Risks / Dependencies
- **DEPENDS ON wh-hxt.2** (the retire→replace runbook the tool-retire path consumes) — sequence after it (bd dep).
- Gate-2 is a FROZEN gate — a watchlist supersede goes THROUGH it, not around it (wh-562).

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G6 — `staleness_check.py:26-30/50-52` is KB-only); `staleness_check.py:26-52`; wh-hxt.2, wh-562 (Gate-2), ADR-012. Split from wh-hxt.1.

title: MONITOR: define the staleness signal (release-age + archived/EOL GitHub-API fields + thresholds) for the wh-5es feed arm
description:
## Goal / Problem
DEFINE the MONITOR staleness signal — the thresholds + GitHub-API fields a poller arm uses to flag a tool as
going stale (latest-release age, archived / EOL / feature-complete flags) BEFORE it becomes a liability
(would have caught gitleaks→Betterleaks and trivy-mcp). The poller PLUMBING is wh-5es's job (one feed
mechanism, don't fork it); this ticket produces the DEFINITION the wh-5es feed arm consumes. Split from
wh-hxt.1 (part 2 of 3).

## Background / Context
- `sec-kb-refresh/scripts/poll_feeds.py:69`: `PARSERS = {osv, atlas, atom}` — there is NO GitHub-release /
  staleness parser today. This ticket DEFINES the staleness signal (the parser's input fields + thresholds +
  output shape); wh-5es (CLOSED) owns the poller plumbing that implements it.
- Deterministic (a staleness verdict from release/commit dates — Policy 5). Human-gated swap proposals only
  (ADR-012, never auto-swap).
- Threat-intel / sourced definitions belong in `docs/research/` (repo convention).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
Primarily a DEFINITION deliverable. If a pure threshold function + a parser-interface stub land, they get
RED/GREEN tests; the live GitHub fetch is wh-5es's plumbing (OUT of scope).

## Steps
1. Write the staleness-signal spec to `docs/research/<date>_staleness_signal.md`: the GitHub-API fields
   (latest_release.published_at, archived, pushed_at, …), the thresholds (what age = stale; archived/EOL =
   immediate flag), and the output shape (a staleness verdict per tool / watchlist row).
2. Specify the `poll_feeds.py` `PARSERS` interface contract the wh-5es plumbing must satisfy (raw input →
   the shared verdict shape) WITHOUT implementing the network poller here.
3. (optional) a pure, deterministic threshold function + tests (no network) if it clarifies the contract.

## Files to Create/Modify
- `docs/research/<date>_staleness_signal.md` — the signal definition (thresholds + GitHub-API fields + output shape).
- (optional) a pure threshold helper + tests; NOT the poller (wh-5es owns plumbing).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A staleness-signal DEFINITION (thresholds + GitHub-API fields + output shape) handed to the wh-5es feed arm exists in `docs/research/`.
- [ ] The poller PLUMBING is NOT duplicated here (the live fetch stays wh-5es's; this is the contract only).
- [ ] If a threshold function lands, it is deterministic (Policy 5) + tested both directions (`==` stale / `!=` fresh).

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
- Research `.md` under `docs/` (repo convention). If a helper lands: `nice -n 10 uv run --project <pkg> --with pytest pytest <pkg>/tests -q` · `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` · `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker`.

## Rollback
`git revert` the doc (+ any helper/tests).

## Risks / Dependencies
- Don't fork the feed mechanism — wh-5es owns ONE poller; this defines the signal it carries.
- Independent of child A (different files); can run the same wave.

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md (MONITOR stage), 20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G6); `poll_feeds.py:69`; wh-5es; ADR-012. Split from wh-hxt.1.
close_reason: MONITOR staleness signal shipped (d5cc851, staleness.py + tests + docs/research/20260612_staleness_signal.md). Primary deliverable works. Dogfood F-002 (parse_github_json degrade-never-raise violation) is LATENT (zero live callers until wh-5es wiring) — tracked as wh-fpy (P2), MUST land before wh-5es wires parse_github_json into poll_feeds PARSERS. Closed with F-002 tracked per operator 2026-06-13.

title: ADMIT: maintenance/health dimension — tool-registry health column + deterministic maintenance-score
description:
## Goal / Problem
Add the MAINTENANCE/HEALTH dimension to tool ADMIT — an unmaintained wrapper pinning a stale binary is a
supply-chain risk distinct from compromise (the trivy-mcp / gitleaks→Betterleaks lesson). Extends wh-xn0's
admissibility gates (license + telemetry, ADR-025) with a maintenance criterion: a tool must be actively
maintained (release cadence / last-commit recency; NOT EOL / archived / feature-complete) — or admitted
only WITH a flagged caveat + a tracked successor. Split from wh-hxt.1 (part 1 of 3).

## Background / Context
- wh-xn0 (CLOSED, ADR-025): the admissibility gates (MIT/Apache-2.0 license + local/no-telemetry) + a
  registry re-audit. `_shared/reference/tool-registry.md` carries the registry tables (the rejected table
  at :119 = tool|SPDX|capability|reason).
- Deterministic where possible (a maintenance score from release/commit dates — Policy 5, NOT LLM
  judgment). Human-gated proposals only (ADR-012, never auto-swap).
- wh-hxt.2 (the retire→replace runbook + diversity policy) ALSO edits tool-registry.md → serialize.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
RED — tests pinning a maintained tool ADMITs and an EOL/archived one is rejected (or flagged) → GREEN — a
deterministic maintenance-score helper + the registry health column → REFACTOR — surgical.

## Steps
1. Add a maintenance/health column to the tool-registry.md table(s) (last-release age, archived/EOL flag,
   tracked successor).
2. Add a deterministic maintenance-score helper (release/commit recency → score; EOL/archived → reject) —
   stdlib-first, no LLM (Policy 5). Home decided at implement time (`_shared/scripts/` or sec-kb-refresh)
   per the package shape (ADR-015).
3. Wire the ADMIT criterion: an EOL/archived/feature-complete tool is rejected, OR admitted with a flagged
   caveat + a tracked successor (e.g. gitleaks→Betterleaks).

## Files to Create/Modify
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/tool-registry.md` — the health/maintenance column + admit criterion.
- a deterministic maintenance-score helper + its tests (home decided at implement time; `scripts/` package shape).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tool-registry gains a maintenance/health field; the ADMIT criterion rejects EOL/archived/feature-complete tools (or admits with a flagged caveat + a tracked successor).
- [ ] The maintenance check is DETERMINISTIC (release/commit dates → score), not an LLM judgment (Policy 5) — pinned by a test (a maintained tool passes `==`, an archived one fails `!=`).
- [ ] Never auto-swaps — proposals are human-gated (ADR-012).
- [ ] Package gate green · `validate_manifest.py` green · `claude plugin validate` green.

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/<skill>/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/<skill>/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

## Rollback
`git revert` the column + helper + tests.

## Risks / Dependencies
- SERIALIZE vs **wh-hxt.2** on `tool-registry.md` (the wh-xn0 columns / wh-nvk rows precedent) — NEVER co-wave.
- Keep the score deterministic (Policy 5); NO network call in the ADMIT gate — the live GitHub-API fetch is
  the MONITOR arm's job (child B / wh-5es), not this gate.

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md (ADMIT stage); ADR-025 (admissibility), ADR-012 (human-gated); wh-xn0, wh-hxt.2; `_shared/reference/tool-registry.md:119`. Split from wh-hxt.1.

title: sec-detect _iter_files bounds depth but not breadth — wide shallow tree still forces full walk (CWE-400)
description:
## Goal / Problem

`detect_tools._iter_files` now bounds DEPTH but not BREADTH. The wh-dv4 fix (in the current working tree) added a `max_depth` prune to the `os.walk` so deep descent is bounded, but the walk still visits **every file in every directory at depth ≤ cap**. A pathologically WIDE shallow tree (very high file counts in a few directories within the depth cap) re-opens the CPU/time DoS along the breadth / file-count axis. `detect_kernel_adjacency` consumes the full `_iter_files` generator on **every** `build_scan_plan`, so a single review of an untrusted, wide repo scales scandir/`is_file`/`open` work linearly with on-disk file count.

- **Threat model:** "review untrusted repos."
- **Class:** CWE-400 (Uncontrolled Resource Consumption), breadth/file-count axis.
- **Distinct from siblings:** wh-dv4 = depth-DoS (the depth cap, closing now); wh-qu0 = per-file read-cap / OOM (closed). This is the **3rd distinct class** — total-entry/file-count along breadth, orthogonal to both.

## Reproduction

The exact, copy-pasteable steps that trigger the bug on a **fresh checkout** (post-wh-8ci/wh-dv4 wave). The fixture is MODERATE — a few thousand to ~20k empty files in 1–2 shallow dirs (depth ≤ cap throughout), showing visit-count / wall-time scaling with file count while depth stays within the cap. The wh-dv4 depth prune does NOT bound this; nothing caps total entries.

```bash
# Demonstrate that _iter_files visits EVERY file in shallow dirs within the depth
# cap — breadth is unbounded even though depth is now capped. tmp dirs only.
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts python -c '
import os, tempfile, time
from pathlib import Path
import detect_tools as dt

for n in (2000, 8000, 20000):
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
        root = Path(td)
        # WIDE but SHALLOW: all files at depth 1 (well within _KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_DEPTH).
        d = root / "manifests"
        d.mkdir()
        for i in range(n):
            (d / f"f{i}.txt").write_text("")          # empty files, breadth only
        t0 = time.perf_counter()
        visited = sum(1 for _ in dt._iter_files(root, max_depth=dt._KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_DEPTH))
        dt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000
        print(f"n={n:>6}  visited={visited:>6}  depth<=cap=True  walk_ms={dt_ms:8.1f}")
'
# Observed: visited tracks n 1:1 (≈2000 → ≈8000 → ≈20000) and walk_ms grows with n,
# while every file sits at depth 1 (<= _KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_DEPTH). The depth cap never
# fires; the breadth axis is unbounded.

# End-to-end on the public entry point (detect_kernel_adjacency runs on every
# build_scan_plan) — same unbounded breadth scan, now also stat-ing each file:
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts python -c '
import tempfile, time
from pathlib import Path
import detect_tools as dt

with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    root = Path(td)
    d = root / "k8s"; d.mkdir()
    for i in range(20000):
        (d / f"m{i}.yaml").write_text("")           # 20k shallow YAMLs, no token
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    markers = dt.detect_kernel_adjacency(root)
    print("markers=", markers, " scan_ms=%.1f" % ((time.perf_counter()-t0)*1000))
'
# Observed: scan_ms scales with the 20k shallow files even though no token is present
# and depth never exceeds the cap — the breadth/file-count DoS the depth cap left open.
```

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** `_iter_files` (and therefore `detect_kernel_adjacency`) does a BOUNDED amount of work on an untrusted repo — when the on-disk entry count exceeds a configured cap, the bounded caller stops (truncates) the walk rather than visiting every file. Degrade-never-raise (ADR-003): hitting the cap silently truncates, never raises.
- **Actual:** With `max_depth` set, the `os.walk` prunes deep directories but still iterates **all** `filenames` in every visited directory with no total-entry / file-count bound (`detect_tools.py:312-318`). Visit count and `is_file()`/`open()` work scale linearly with the on-disk file count of shallow directories within the cap. A wide untrusted repo re-opens the CWE-400 CPU/time DoS the depth cap was supposed to close.

## Suspected Root Cause

A concrete hypothesis tied to code — `file:line` + the specific construct (line numbers are vs the **post-wh-dv4 working tree**, uncommitted; see References).

- `detect_tools.py:303-320` — the bounded branch of `_iter_files` (the uncapped inner loop at `:312-318`):
  ```python
  for dirpath_str, dirnames, filenames in os.walk(root):
      ...
      if depth >= max_depth:
          dirnames[:] = []            # bounds DEPTH only
      for filename in filenames:      # <-- visits EVERY filename, no total cap
          candidate = dirpath / filename
          try:
              if candidate.is_file():
                  yield candidate
          except OSError:
              continue
  ```
  The `for filename in filenames` inner loop (`:312-318`) has **no total-visited-entries cap**. `max_depth` prunes `dirnames` so descent is bounded, but breadth (file count per dir, summed across all dirs ≤ cap) is unbounded. `detect_kernel_adjacency` (`detect_tools.py:359`) consumes the full generator on every `build_scan_plan` (`detect_tools.py:566`), so the cost is paid on every scan-plan build for an untrusted repo.

## Files in Scope (the fix surface)

- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py` — the fix (`max_entries` param on `_iter_files`; `detect_kernel_adjacency` opts in; a `_KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_ENTRIES` constant mirroring `_KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_DEPTH` at `detect_tools.py:130`).
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests/test_detect_tools.py` — the regression tests (mirror the wh-dv4 within/beyond + walk-spy pattern at `test_detect_tools.py:750-866`).

> Source of truth for scope. A file not listed here will not be touched. The fix is **surgical** (Policy 3) — it adds one opt-in param + one constant + the bounded caller's opt-in; it does NOT alter the depth logic, the read-cap, or any other caller. Stale neighbours get a NEW ticket.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

1. **RED** — add regression tests to `test_detect_tools.py` (mirroring the wh-dv4 block at `:750-866`) that FAIL on the current tree:
   - A within-cap token test: a `privileged: true` token `[primary-sourced: detect_tools.py:207]` among entries within the entry cap IS still matched (no over-truncation).
   - An entry-count spy test (mirroring the `os.walk` spy at `:835-866`): on a wide shallow tree exceeding the cap, `_iter_files(root, max_entries=cap)` yields **no more than** `cap` entries (`== cap` when the tree exceeds it; `!= unbounded`). The unbounded default (`max_entries=None`) still yields all.
   - A shared-contract test: `_iter_files(root)` (default `None`) yields the full set; `_iter_files(root, max_entries=cap)` truncates — pinning the Liskov invariant that other callers are unchanged.
2. **GREEN** — minimum fix: add a per-caller opt-in `max_entries: int | None = None` param to `_iter_files`, mirroring how `max_depth` was added (default `None` = unbounded, so the SOLID/Liskov contract for other callers is preserved). When set, count yielded entries and **STOP** the walk (`return`) once the cap is reached — truncate, never raise (ADR-003 degrade-never-raise). `detect_kernel_adjacency` opts in with a new module constant `_KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_ENTRIES` (value chosen at implement time — generous enough to cover any realistic shallow manifest set, e.g. low-thousands, while bounding a pathological tree; sized and justified in the constant docstring like `_KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_DEPTH` at `:121-130`). Re-run the package tests.
3. **REFACTOR** — only what the fix needs; nothing speculative. Do NOT add a `max_entries` flag to any other caller (there is none — `detect_kernel_adjacency` is the sole `_iter_files` consumer; `detect_ai_agent_infra` has its own inline `os.walk` and is OUT of scope).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A regression test FAILS before the fix and PASSES after — the entry-count spy test asserting `_iter_files(root, max_entries=cap)` yields ≤ `cap` entries on a wide shallow tree.
- [ ] Both directions pinned where relevant (Policy 9):
  - **==** a token among entries WITHIN the cap IS matched (`"privileged-container" in markers`; token `privileged: true` `[primary-sourced: detect_tools.py:207]`).
  - **!=** the walk yields NO MORE than `cap` entries when the tree exceeds it (`yielded == cap`, `yielded != unbounded_count`) — mirroring the existing walk-spy at `test_detect_tools.py:835-866`.
- [ ] No regression: the touched package's existing tests stay green. The sec-detect baseline is ~94 collected tests post-wh-dv4 (verified by reading the wh-dv4 additions at `test_detect_tools.py:750-866`; the file currently defines 81 `def test_` symbols, several parametrized — count confirmed by reading; the implementer confirms the exact collected number at gate time). This bug's new tests raise that baseline.
- [ ] (Security — threat model "review untrusted repos") the breadth DoS probe is closed: on a wide shallow tree the bounded `_iter_files` truncates at `max_entries` and `detect_kernel_adjacency` returns in bounded time.
- [ ] Degrade-never-raise (ADR-003): hitting the cap STOPS the walk (truncates the generator) — it never raises; existing `OSError` truncation/skip semantics in `_iter_files` are unchanged.
- [ ] Other `_iter_files` callers unchanged — `max_entries` default `None` keeps the unbounded contract; the only caller, `detect_kernel_adjacency` (`detect_tools.py:359`), is the only one that opts in.

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

The white-hacker gates are **pytest + manifest + plugin validate — NOT ruff / mypy / coverage** (`.claude/CLAUDE.md` Policy 12). `nice -n 10`, serial (no `-n` — matches the canonical wave gate; the resource-discipline ceiling is `-n 4` if xdist is configured, but the package gate runs serial).

```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts --with pytest \
  pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

- [ ] Touched package tests pass · `validate_manifest.py` green · `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] Policy 12 — a non-zero gate keeps the bug `in_progress`; never check an AC whose probe is SKIP not PASS.

## Severity ↔ Priority

**Severity LOW / Priority P3.**

Justification: this is the **advisory scan-plan path**, not a VULN-FINDINGS emitter. `detect_kernel_adjacency` produces informational SCAN-PLAN metadata (spike-10 T-A, ADR-018 — no CVSS, never a `VULN-FINDINGS.json` entry; `detect_tools.py:323-339`). The DoS is **bounded by on-disk repo contents** (the reviewer already cloned/owns the tree), not by attacker-supplied request volume, and the depth axis is already capped — only the breadth axis remains. Same envelope as wh-dv4 (advisory path, repo-bounded). No behavioral impact on a real finding.

**Bump condition:** raise to P2 if `_iter_files` is ever wired into a VULN-FINDINGS-emitting path, or if profiling on a realistic untrusted-repo corpus shows `build_scan_plan` wall-time materially regressing the review pipeline (i.e. the breadth scan becomes a pipeline-blocking flow, template check 4 P2: "a DoS reachable on the standing threat model").

## Commit Message Format

```
fix: bound detect_kernel_adjacency _iter_files breadth (CWE-400)
```

Commit conventions per `.claude/CLAUDE.md` Policy 12 (author `Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>`, no AI attribution, never the corporate email). Security-relevant → gets a dogfood review (QA flow).

## Rollback

`git revert` the fix hunk in `detect_tools.py` (the `max_entries` param + truncation branch + `_KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_ENTRIES` constant + the `detect_kernel_adjacency` opt-in) and the matching test additions in `test_detect_tools.py`. No data/migration to undo — surgical bug fix.

## Risks / Dependencies

- **Serial-lane dependency:** `detect_tools.py` is a SERIAL lane. Sequence this **after** the wh-8ci/wh-dv4 wave lands (this fix builds on wh-dv4's `os.walk` + `max_depth` structure) **and** vs **wh-hxt.1 (OPEN)** which also touches `detect_tools.py`. NEVER co-wave any two `detect_tools.py` tickets — disjoint-file ownership cannot hold on the same file.
- **Shared-helper risk:** `_iter_files` is shared. Use **per-caller opt-in only** (`max_entries` default `None`) — never make the cap unconditional, or a future unbounded caller silently truncates. Mirror the `max_depth` opt-in precedent exactly (`detect_tools.py:283`, docstring `:284-296`).
- **Over-truncation risk:** the entry cap must be generous enough that a realistic shallow manifest set is never truncated before its tokens are seen (false negative). Pin the within-cap match test; size `_KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_ENTRIES` with margin and justify it in the constant docstring (as `_KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_DEPTH` is justified at `:121-130`).

## Notes / References

- **Discovering review:** white-hacker dogfood during the wh-8ci/wh-dv4 wave (2026-06-12); scoped OUT of that wave's diff as a distinct breadth-axis class.
- **Line numbers are vs the post-wh-dv4 WORKING TREE (uncommitted)** — read the tree, not git HEAD. Files actually read (Policy 8):
  - `detect_tools.py:283-320` — `_iter_files` post-wh-dv4 (`max_depth` prune; the uncapped inner `for filename in filenames` loop at `:312-318`).
  - `detect_tools.py:323-376` — `detect_kernel_adjacency`, the sole `_iter_files` caller (`:359`); read-cap from wh-qu0 at `:370` (`_AI_MANIFEST_READ_CAP`).
  - `detect_tools.py:117-136` — `_AI_INFRA_MAX_DEPTH=5` / `_KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_DEPTH=8` precedent constants (the docstring shape to mirror for `_KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_ENTRIES`).
  - `detect_tools.py:206-212` — `_PRIVILEGED_CONTAINER_TOKENS` (the `privileged:` token literal the within-cap test asserts).
  - `detect_tools.py:418-458` — `detect_ai_agent_infra`'s own inline `os.walk` (a SEPARATE walk; NOT an `_iter_files` caller; OUT of scope).
  - `detect_tools.py:566` — `build_scan_plan` calls `detect_kernel_adjacency` on every plan build (the per-build reachability).
  - `test_detect_tools.py:750-866` — the wh-dv4 within/beyond-depth + `os.walk`-spy tests this bug's breadth tests must MIRROR.
- **Sibling classes:** wh-dv4 (depth-DoS, closing) · wh-qu0 (per-file read-cap/OOM, closed) · this (breadth/entry-count, 3rd class).
close_reason: FIXED via TDD. Added per-caller opt-in max_entries param to _iter_files (default None=unbounded, Liskov-preserving) that truncates the walk once max_entries files are yielded (both the rglob and os.walk branches); detect_kernel_adjacency opts in via new _KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_ENTRIES=50000. Closes the BREADTH/file-count DoS axis (CWE-400) the wh-dv4 depth cap left open. RED test_iter_files_max_entries_truncates_* failed (TypeError: no max_entries) before, pass after; both directions pinned (truncates at cap; default None yields all; within-cap token still matched — no over-truncation). sec-detect 94->97; manifest + plugin-validate green. Degrade-never-raise: truncate, never raise (ADR-003). SERIALIZE: detect_tools.py shared with wh-hxt.1 (OPEN) — never co-wave; wh-dv4/wh-qu0/wh-e1d landed. No git commit (operator-gated).

title: supply_chain watchlist union: raise-safe + symmetric _as_set value normalization
description:
## Goal / Problem
Make `supply_chain.scan`'s always-on watchlist union (wh-ezc) **raise-safe** and **symmetric**, resolving
two review NITs surfaced by the post-wave `/review`:
- **NIT 2 (robustness):** the nested `_as_set` helper's `else: return {v}` branch raises `TypeError` on an
  unhashable caller-db value (e.g. a dict), so the inline comment "the union never raises (wh-ezc N-1)" is
  aspirational for that path.
- **NIT 1 (consistency):** the caller db is normalized via `_as_set` ONLY on the `elif bundled:` union path;
  when the bundled floor is empty (degraded curated file) the caller db is passed through un-normalized.

These are NIT-grade (not reachable from untrusted FILE input — the curated file's shape is controlled by
`load_malware_db`; only a malformed caller-API db hits it). Resolving them makes the "never raises" contract
true and the normalization uniform. Bundled per the operator's "resolve NITs" instruction (same function).

## Background / Context
- `supply_chain.scan` (wh-ezc) loads the bundled curated watchlist and UNIONs a caller-supplied `malware_db`
  into it (`supply_chain.py:~1250`). Version values may be a scalar (`"*"`), or a list/tuple/set/frozenset.
  `_as_set` normalizes them to a hashable set so the union never raises. The downstream consumer (`signal_s8`
  / `malware_db.is_known_bad`) matches an installed version against the value set.
- ADR-003 degrade-never-raise: `load_malware_db` returns `{}` on an absent/odd curated file → S8 still
  returns `[]` and `_build_doc` records `malware-db` unavailable.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — add a test: a caller `malware_db` whose value is UNHASHABLE (a dict) currently raises
   `TypeError` from `scan()`. It must FAIL before the fix.
2. **GREEN** — make `_as_set` coerce every value through `str()` (`{str(x) for x in v}` for collections,
   `{str(v)}` for a scalar) so it never raises; restructure the union so the caller db is normalized through
   `_as_set` on BOTH the bundled-present AND bundled-empty paths (symmetry — NIT 1).
3. **REFACTOR** — none beyond the restructure; keep `signal_s8` semantics identical for string values.

## Steps
1. Add the unhashable-value RED test + a bundled-empty symmetry test in `test_supply_chain.py`.
2. Rework `_as_set` to be `str()`-coercing and raise-safe; route bundled AND caller values through it; drop
   the `elif bundled:` asymmetry so the caller db is always normalized.
3. Confirm existing wh-ezc tests (union, list-typed, degrade) stay green — string values are unchanged by
   `str()`.

## Files in Scope (the fix surface)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py` — `scan()` union block + `_as_set`.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_supply_chain.py` — the regression tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A RED test (`test_s8_union_tolerates_unhashable_caller_value_no_raise`) FAILS before the fix and PASSES
      after (the dict-valued caller db no longer raises; scan returns a schema-valid doc).
- [ ] (Untrusted/odd input degrades cleanly) the union enumerates its exception surface — it must NOT raise
      `TypeError` on an unhashable value (a `str()`-coercing `_as_set` is the mechanism; no bare `except`).
- [ ] Both directions pinned (Policy 9): a real string-valued caller version still UNIONs + flags AND the
      unhashable value degrades to a (stringified) token without raising.
- [ ] NIT 1: the caller db is normalized through `_as_set` on the bundled-empty path too (a symmetry test
      with `load_malware_db` monkeypatched to `{}` + a list-typed caller value flags without raising).
- [ ] No regression: deps-scan package tests stay green (the 5 wh-ezc tests included).

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with pytest \
  pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```
- [ ] Touched package tests pass · `validate_manifest.py` green · `claude plugin validate` green

## Priority
**P3** — NIT-grade: a robustness/consistency hardening of an internal union API; not reachable from
untrusted file input, no behavioral change for real (string) watchlist data.

## Rollback
`git revert` the hunk. No data/migration.

## Risks / Dependencies
- **SERIALIZE vs the supply_chain.py serial lane** (epic wh-5ox Wave 2: wh-5ox.9/.10/.2/.5/.8/.11 + Lane D)
  — `supply_chain.py` is "one writer at a time." This fix lands FIRST; the lane re-grooms against the
  post-fix file (groom-right-before-doing). Touches the `scan()` union block, NOT `_DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS`
  (the lane's region), so the textual overlap is minimal.
- Risk: `str()`-coercion must not change matching for real string versions — pinned by the no-regression AC.

## Notes / References
- Discovering signal: post-wave `/review` of the watchlist-activation diff (wh-ezc), NIT 1 + NIT 2.
- Read: `supply_chain.py:~1246-1280` (`scan()` union + nested `_as_set`), `:44` (`load_malware_db` import).
close_reason: RESOLVED both review NITs via TDD. NIT 2 (raise-safe): _as_set now str()-coerces every value ({str(x) for x in v} / {str(v)}) so an unhashable caller-db value (dict) no longer raises TypeError — RED test_s8_union_tolerates_unhashable_caller_value_no_raise failed at supply_chain.py:1269 before, passes after. NIT 1 (symmetric): dropped the 'elif bundled:' asymmetry -> caller db is normalized through _as_set on the bundled-empty path too (test_s8_bundled_empty_path_normalizes_caller_db). 161 existing tests stayed green (str-coercion is identity for real string versions) + 2 new = 163 passed; manifest + plugin-validate green. SERIALIZE: touched the scan() union block only (NOT _DANGEROUS_API_PATTERNS); the supply_chain.py serial lane (wh-5ox.9/.10/.2/.5/.8/.11 + Lane D) re-grooms against post-fix HEAD. No git commit (operator-gated).

title: Harden config_persist_scan: metachar-aware dropper-ref split + drop unused severity tiers
description:
## Goal / Problem
Resolve two review NITs in the ide-hygiene config-persistence floor (`config_persist_scan.py`, wh-5ox.3),
surfaced by the post-wave `/review`:
- **NIT 4 (recall):** `_exec_tokens` splits exec strings on WHITESPACE only, so a shell-metachar-glued
  dropper reference with no preceding `/` or space — e.g. a `folderOpen` command `"benign&&setup.mjs"` —
  evades the exact-basename match and is not flagged.
- **NIT 3 (simplicity, Policy 2):** `_CONFIDENCE` carries unused `MEDIUM`/`LOW` tiers and `_renumber`
  carries a 3-tier `severity_rank`, but `_make_finding` always emits `HIGH` — speculative generality.

Floor + `ai-llm-review` §9 mitigate NIT 4 downstream, so both are NIT-grade; resolving them tightens the
detector and removes dead scaffolding. Bundled per the operator's "resolve NITs" instruction (same module).

## Background / Context
- `config_persist_scan.py` is the deterministic ide-hygiene floor (wh-5ox.3, CLOSED). `_referenced_dropper`
  exact-matches a token's basename against `DROPPER_TARGETS` — precision guard. Splitting on MORE separators
  cannot create a false positive (a benign `mysetup.mjs` / `setup.mjs.bak` still ≠ `setup.mjs`); it only
  raises recall. KB: `AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003`.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — add a test: a `.vscode/tasks.json` `folderOpen` command `"benign&&setup.mjs"` (and a settings
   `SessionStart` `"x;setup.mjs"`) currently yields NO finding. It must FAIL before the fix.
2. **GREEN** — `_exec_tokens` splits on whitespace AND shell separators (`;`, `&`, `|`, `<`, `>`, `(`, `)`,
   `$`, backtick) so the bare dropper basename surfaces as a token. Keep the exact-basename precision guard.
3. **REFACTOR** — collapse `_CONFIDENCE` to a single `_FLOOR_HIGH_CONFIDENCE` constant and simplify
   `_renumber` to sort by `file` (severity is always HIGH). Existing 35 tests verify no behavior change.

## Steps
1. Add the metachar-recall RED test(s) in `test_config_persist_scan.py` (both tasks.json + settings paths).
2. Rework `_exec_tokens` to split on a shell-separator character class; pin precision (a benign look-alike
   still does NOT match).
3. Remove the unused severity scaffolding (NIT 3); keep the public `scan()` output identical.

## Files in Scope (the fix surface)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/config_persist_scan.py`
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests/test_config_persist_scan.py`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A RED test (`test_metachar_glued_dropper_*`) FAILS before the `_exec_tokens` fix and PASSES after.
- [ ] Both directions pinned (Policy 9): `"benign&&setup.mjs"` → HIGH finding AND a benign look-alike
      (`"benign&&mysetup.mjs"`, `"setup.mjs.bak"`) → NO finding (precision preserved).
- [ ] NIT 3: no `_CONFIDENCE["MEDIUM"|"LOW"]` / unused `severity_rank` remains; `scan()` output byte-identical
      on existing fixtures (the 35 tests stay green — no finding's severity/confidence/order changes).
- [ ] No regression: ide-hygiene package tests stay green.

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts --with pytest \
  pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```
- [ ] Touched package tests pass · `validate_manifest.py` green · `claude plugin validate` green

## Priority
**P3** — NIT-grade: a recall edge on a floor detector (mitigated by §9 downstream) + a simplicity cleanup.
No behavioral impact on a real scan beyond the added recall.

## Rollback
`git revert` the hunk. No data/migration.

## Risks / Dependencies
- Risk: a broader token split could in theory add FPs — mitigated by the exact-basename guard (AC pins a
  benign look-alike still non-matching).
- No file contention: `config_persist_scan.py` has no open sibling ticket.

## Notes / References
- Discovering signal: post-wave `/review` of the wh-5ox.3 + watchlist-activation diff (NIT 3, NIT 4).
- Read: `config_persist_scan.py:140-162` (`_exec_tokens`/`_referenced_dropper`), `:56-57` + `:262`
  (`_CONFIDENCE`), `:321-330` (`_renumber`).
close_reason: RESOLVED both review NITs via TDD. NIT 4 (recall): _exec_tokens now splits on whitespace AND shell separators (new _EXEC_SEPARATORS regex) so a metachar-glued dropper basename (x&&setup.mjs) surfaces; RED test_tasks/sessionstart_metachar_glued_dropper_trips failed before, pass after; precision preserved (test_metachar_split_preserves_exact_basename_precision — mysetup.mjs/setup.mjs.bak still non-matching). NIT 3 (simplicity): _CONFIDENCE 3-tier dict -> single _FLOOR_HIGH_CONFIDENCE constant; _renumber sorts by file only (severity always HIGH). 35 existing tests stayed green (output byte-identical) + 3 new = 38 passed. manifest + plugin-validate green. No git commit (operator-gated).

title: detect_tools._iter_files rglob walk has no depth cap — CPU/time DoS sibling to wh-qu0 (CWE-400)
description:
## Goal / Problem
`_iter_files` (`detect_tools.py:272`) recurses the whole repo via `root.rglob("*")` with no depth
bound. On the standing threat model "review untrusted repos," a pathologically deep directory tree
makes every `_iter_files` caller (incl. `detect_kernel_adjacency`, run on every `build_scan_plan`)
walk unbounded — a CPU/time DoS (CWE-400), a DISTINCT class from the OOM that wh-qu0 closed
(`detect_tools.py:326` read-cap). LOW severity (bounded by on-disk contents; advisory scan-plan
path, not a VULN-FINDINGS emitter), but the depth-cap precedent already exists one function over.

## Reproduction
On a fresh checkout, a deeply nested tree with a privileged-container token at the leaf:
```bash
d=$(mktemp -d); p="$d"; for i in $(seq 1 5000); do p="$p/n"; done; mkdir -p "$p"
printf 'privileged: true\n' > "$p/pod.yml"
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts python -c "
import detect_tools as t; from pathlib import Path
print(t.detect_kernel_adjacency(Path('$d')))"   # walks all 5000 levels; wall time grows with depth
```
With a depth cap (parallel to `_AI_INFRA_MAX_DEPTH`) the walk stops at the cap instead of descending
the full tree.

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** `_iter_files` bounds recursion depth (parallel to `detect_ai_agent_infra`'s
  `_AI_INFRA_MAX_DEPTH=5`); a tree deeper than the cap is not descended past it.
- **Actual:** `for p in root.rglob("*")` descends to unbounded depth; the whole tree is walked on
  every `build_scan_plan` via `detect_kernel_adjacency` (and any other `_iter_files` caller).

## Suspected Root Cause
`plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py:272` — `_iter_files` does
`for p in root.rglob("*"): if p.is_file(): yield p` with no depth guard. The depth-cap precedent is
`detect_ai_agent_infra` (`:336`), which enforces `current_depth >= _AI_INFRA_MAX_DEPTH` (`:385`)
against `_AI_INFRA_MAX_DEPTH=5` (`:119`). `_iter_files` feeds `detect_kernel_adjacency` and other
callers without that bound, so the unbounded walk runs on every scan plan.

## Files in Scope (the fix surface)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py` — bound `_iter_files`' recursion depth.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests/test_detect_tools.py` — the regression test.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — a fixture tree deeper than the cap with a privileged-container token at a depth BEYOND
   the cap → `detect_kernel_adjacency` currently finds it (walks there); should NOT after the cap.
   A token WITHIN the cap is still found. (Mirror wh-qu0's within/beyond pinning, but on DEPTH not
   file-size — Policy 9, both directions.)
2. **GREEN** — bound `_iter_files`' depth. PREFERRED: add a `max_depth: int | None = None` param
   (default unbounded → other callers unchanged) and have `detect_kernel_adjacency` pass the cap;
   compute depth via `len(p.relative_to(root).parts)`. Keep the `is_file()` filter + the `OSError`
   guard. Decide the cap value at implement time (kernel-adjacency tokens sit in shallow manifests).
3. **REFACTOR** — none beyond the bound.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A regression test FAILS before / PASSES after: a privileged-container token DEEPER than the cap
      is not matched (`!=`), a token within the cap is matched (`==`).
- [ ] No regression: existing `detect_tools` tests stay green; other `_iter_files` callers' behavior
      preserved (per-caller param defaulting unbounded) OR intentionally bounded + tested.
- [ ] The threat-model probe is closed — a pathologically deep tree no longer forces an unbounded walk.

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts --with pytest \
  pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```
- [ ] sec-detect package tests pass · `validate_manifest.py` green · `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] Policy 12 — a non-zero gate keeps the bug `in_progress`; never check an AC whose probe is SKIP not PASS

## Severity ↔ Priority
P3 — CPU/time DoS bounded by on-disk contents (NOT a cheap single-file OOM trigger like wh-qu0's P2);
advisory scan-plan path, not a VULN-FINDINGS emitter. LOW severity per the discovering dogfood. May be
bumped to P2 if treated on par with the wh-qu0 OOM sibling as a live DoS on the standing threat model.

## Rollback
`git revert` the `_iter_files` depth-bound hunk + drop the regression test. No data/migration to undo.

## Risks / Dependencies
- **Risk:** `_iter_files` is SHARED — a global depth cap changes behavior for ALL callers and could
  drop legitimate deep matches for other detectors. Prefer a per-caller `max_depth` param (default
  unbounded, `detect_kernel_adjacency` opts in); confirm no legitimate case is dropped.
- **Dependency:** SERIAL lane on `detect_tools.py` — serialize vs `wh-hxt.1` + `wh-qu0`; NEVER co-wave.

## Notes / References
- Found by dev-wh-qu0 + the white-hacker dogfood during the wh-qu0 wave (2026-06-12); sibling to
  wh-qu0 (OOM, `:326`) and wh-e1d (manifest read, `:417`). QA/dogfood evidence local-only in
  `.notes/qa/20260612/` (gitignored).
- `file:line` read (Policy 8): `_iter_files` `:272` `rglob("*")`; `_AI_INFRA_MAX_DEPTH=5` `:119`;
  enforced at `:385`; `detect_ai_agent_infra` `:336`.
notes: GROOMED 2026-06-12 — VERDICT: READY. Template-conforming (bug). CITED file:line ACCURATE vs current HEAD (no drift): _iter_files :272 (rglob at :275); _AI_INFRA_MAX_DEPTH=5 :119; depth enforced at :385 (current_depth>=cap, computed :382 via relative_to :379); detect_ai_agent_infra :336 is the depth-cap PRECEDENT to mirror; detect_kernel_adjacency :282 is the _iter_files caller. COORDINATION: wh-qu0 now CLOSED (read-cap landed ~:327 within detect_kernel_adjacency) -> serialize partner on detect_tools.py reduces to wh-hxt.1 (OPEN) only; NEVER co-wave with it. BASELINE sec-detect 90 green. PARALLEL-SAFE with wh-8ci this wave (different package/file: sec-detect/detect_tools.py vs deps-scan/supply_chain.py). PRIORITY P3 defensible (CPU/time DoS bounded by on-disk contents; advisory scan-plan path, NOT a VULN-FINDINGS emitter) — bump to P2 only if treated on par with the wh-qu0 OOM sibling. Read: detect_tools.py:119/272/275/282/336/382/385.
close_reason: Phase-6 close: all 4 gates green (deps-scan 169/baseline 163, sec-detect 94/baseline 90, validate_manifest OK, plugin-validate passed). wh-8ci: (a)+(b) — _unpinned(_constraint(d)) at both S3 call sites + anchored _UNPINNED_RE; FP class (xlrd + extras/markers) dead, no FN. wh-dv4: _iter_files os.walk depth-prune (cap _KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_DEPTH=8) bounds descent (CWE-400 closed; prune proven). QA PASS 4 tiers + white-hacker CLEAN. Follow-up: breadth-DoS proposed to operator /design-ticket.

title: iac-scan skill: deterministic workflow_lint.py (the wh-5ox.7 deferred linter)
description:
## Goal / Problem

Create a new `iac-scan` skill with a deterministic `workflow_lint.py` that emits findings for the GitHub Actions secrets-exfil and OIDC-exchange patterns that wh-5ox.7 documented in `infra.md`. No iac-scan skill currently exists; `sec-vuln-scan` is SKILL.md-only and `sec-detect` is routing-only — neither has a findings-emitting `scripts/` package. Signal-not-block posture; repo-relative paths only.

## Background / Context

- wh-5ox.7 (DONE) added `toJSON(secrets)` whole-context serialization and OIDC token-exchange theft patterns to `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/infra.md:46-56` (agent-applied checklist rules).
- A deterministic linter was deferred from wh-5ox.7 (scope split). This ticket delivers that linter.
- `sec-detect/SKILL.md:62` routes the `iac` capability to read workflows + `infra.md` — the existing floor is agent-applied pattern matching, not a deterministic emitter.
- Parent epic wh-5ox GROOMING note: 'standalone linter = follow-up needing a new iac-scan skill.'
- Architecture: ADR-015 capability-interface pattern — tools behind capability ports, degrade-clean (ADR-003).
- DO-NOT-COPY posture (epic wh-5ox): literals re-derived from primary sources only (infra.md entries already cite the public GitHub Actions docs / OpenSSF).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

1. **RED** — Write failing tests first in `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts/tests/test_workflow_lint.py`:
   - `test_toJSON_secrets_emits_finding`: file with `${{ toJSON(secrets) }}` near a `curl` POST → finding emitted with `category == 'secrets-exfil'`, `file` repo-relative, schema-valid; assert `finding['file'] != ''` and `finding['category'] == 'secrets-exfil'` (pin both == and !=).
   - `test_clean_workflow_emits_no_finding`: file with `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` but no `toJSON` → zero findings.
   - `test_oidc_exchange_emits_finding`: file with `permissions: id-token: write` AND `steps.outputs.value` piped outbound → finding emitted.
   - `test_degrade_clean_empty_dir`: no `.github/workflows/` → zero findings, no raise.
   - Run `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts/tests -q` → FAIL.

2. **GREEN** — Implement minimum code:
   - Create `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/SKILL.md` (name, description, when_to_use ≤1,536 chars; ADR-005 caps).
   - Create `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts/pyproject.toml`, `conftest.py`, `uv.lock` matching the _shared package shape.
   - Implement `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts/workflow_lint.py`: enumerate `.github/workflows/*.yml` in `target_dir`, grep for the two signal-not-block patterns, emit repo-relative findings conforming to `finding-schema.json`. Stdlib + `yaml` only; NEVER raises (ADR-003 degrade).
   - Re-run tests → PASS.

3. **REFACTOR** — Surgical cleanup:
   - Ensure finding `file` field passes `^[^/~]` guard from `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/finding-schema.json`.
   - Cross-check `validate_manifest.py` and `claude plugin validate`.

## Steps (with description)

1. Create the `iac-scan` skill directory tree: `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/` with `SKILL.md` and `scripts/` sub-package (pyproject.toml, conftest.py, uv.lock, tests/).
2. Write RED tests in `tests/test_workflow_lint.py` covering the two patterns (toJSON/OIDC) plus degrade-clean, pinning both == and != for each invariant.
3. Implement `scripts/workflow_lint.py`: walk `.github/workflows/`, parse YAML, match the two signal-not-block patterns from infra.md:46-56, emit findings with repo-relative `file` paths and `tool_assisted: false`.
4. Run tests GREEN; ensure `test_degrade_clean_empty_dir` passes without raising.
5. Run quality gates (manifest + plugin validate).

## Files to Create/Modify

- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/SKILL.md` — CREATE
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts/pyproject.toml` — CREATE
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts/conftest.py` — CREATE
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts/uv.lock` — CREATE
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts/workflow_lint.py` — CREATE
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts/tests/test_workflow_lint.py` — CREATE

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts/workflow_lint.py` exists and detects `toJSON(secrets)` near outbound HTTP and OIDC token-exchange in synthetic `.github/workflows/` fixtures.
- [ ] Tests pin both `== expected_category` and `!= ''` for finding fields (Policy 9).
- [ ] `test_clean_workflow_emits_no_finding` passes (no FP on legitimate `secrets.TOKEN` usage).
- [ ] `test_degrade_clean_empty_dir` passes — no `.github/workflows/` dir → zero findings, no exception raised.
- [ ] Every emitted finding's `file` field matches `^[^/~]` (repo-relative; verified by `validate_findings.py` or equivalent assert in tests).
- [ ] `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/SKILL.md` exists with name/description/when_to_use within ADR-005 size caps.

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

- [ ] iac-scan package tests pass
- [ ] `validate_manifest.py` green
- [ ] `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] Outer-loop gate: N/A (new inner-loop skill, no KB/eval-corpus changes)

## Rollback

Delete the new `plugins/white-hacker/skills/iac-scan/` directory. No existing files are modified.

## Risks / Dependencies

- No iac-scan skill exists yet — entire directory must be created from scratch (no file-ownership conflicts).
- SKILL.md size cap (ADR-005): `description` + `when_to_use` ≤ 1,536; `description` alone ≤ 1,024; `name` ≤ 64.
- PyYAML must be declared in pyproject.toml and locked; it is NOT in the _shared package (check `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/pyproject.toml` before adding).
- wh-5ox.7 (DONE): infra.md patterns already exist at `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/infra.md:46-56` — the linter must match those exact two patterns (toJSON/OIDC); do not invent new ones.

## Notes / References

- No `iac-scan` skill directory exists as of 2026-06-11 (verified by `ls plugins/white-hacker/skills/`).
- infra.md exists at `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/infra.md` (verified 2026-06-11).
- Pattern: signal-not-block. `toJSON(secrets)` has rare legitimate debug uses (infra.md:50) — emit candidate, not block.
- ADR-015 capability interface: the iac capability is currently 'read workflows + infra.md' (agent floor); this ticket adds the deterministic emitter tier.

title: ide-hygiene: activation-grep on the editor-CLI enumeration path
description:
## Goal / Problem

Extend `ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py` so that the activation-code grep (Tier A + Tier B malware patterns) also runs on extensions enumerated via the editor CLI path. Currently, `ext_scan.py` skips the grep when `dir == ''` — which is the normal case for CLI-enumerated extensions, because the CLI output (`code --list-extensions --show-versions`) does not emit the on-disk path. The fallback on-disk walk always has a dir, so grep runs there. Goal: resolve each CLI-enumerated extension id to its on-disk dir so grep coverage is symmetric.

## Background / Context

- `ext_scan.py` (ide-hygiene skill) split from wh-5ox.4 per Policy 3 (surgical changes — no bundling).
- Root cause: `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py` line ~60 sets `_DEFAULT_EDITOR_CLI = 'code'`; the CLI enumeration path parses `code --list-extensions --show-versions` stdout which yields `publisher.name@version` tokens but NOT the on-disk path, so the dir field is empty and the activation grep tier is SKIPPED (verified by reading ext_scan.py:1-60 and test_ext_scan.py:195-230).
- VS Code on-disk extension layout: `~/.vscode/extensions/<publisher>.<name>-<version>/` — this is the DOCUMENTED layout used by the on-disk fallback (ext_scan.py:9-13). The resolution is to probe this standard path from the CLI-enumerated id.
- ADR-003 degrade: if the resolved dir does not exist, skip silently (do not raise). Grep coverage is best-effort.
- The existing test `test_activation_probe_emits_lower_confidence_candidate` (test_ext_scan.py:195) tests the on-disk fallback path. A new test for the CLI-path resolution is needed.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

1. **RED** — Add failing tests to `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests/test_ext_scan.py`:
   - `test_cli_path_activation_grep_runs_when_dir_resolved`: mock the CLI to emit one extension id; stub the resolved on-disk dir to exist with an activation file containing Tier A + Tier B patterns → finding emitted; assert `finding['category'] == 'ide-extension-activation'` and `finding['file'] != ''` (pin both).
   - `test_cli_path_degrade_when_dir_not_found`: mock CLI emitting an id whose resolved dir does NOT exist → zero findings, no raise.
   - Run `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests -q` → FAIL.

2. **GREEN** — Minimal change in `ext_scan.py`:
   - After CLI enumeration resolves a `(publisher, name, version)` triple, compute the candidate on-disk path (`~/.vscode/extensions/<publisher>.<name>-<version>/`) and pass it as `dir` when it exists (Path.exists check). On-disk fallback path unchanged.
   - Degrade-clean: non-existent dir → empty dir → grep skipped, no raise.
   - Re-run tests → PASS.

3. **REFACTOR** — Surgical:
   - Ensure the home-path lookup is done via `Path.home() / '.vscode' / 'extensions'` (stdlib), NEVER hardcoded as a string literal in findings output (would leak host path — repo-relative locator `ext:<publisher>.<name>@<version>` is the emitted `file`).

## Steps (with description)

1. Read `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py` (around the CLI enumeration block) to identify the exact line where `dir` is left empty for CLI-enumerated entries.
2. Write RED tests (two new test functions in `test_ext_scan.py`) covering the resolution hit and the degrade-clean miss.
3. Add the dir-resolution step to `ext_scan.py`: after parsing the CLI `publisher.name@version` token, probe `Path.home() / '.vscode' / 'extensions' / f'{publisher}.{name}-{version}'` and set `dir` if it exists.
4. Run tests GREEN; verify existing tests still pass (no regressions).
5. Verify emitted finding `file` field still uses the marketplace locator format (`ext:<publisher>.<name>@<version>`), never the resolved on-disk path.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py` — MODIFY (add dir-resolution step in CLI enumeration path)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests/test_ext_scan.py` — MODIFY (add two new test functions)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Two new tests: `test_cli_path_activation_grep_runs_when_dir_resolved` and `test_cli_path_degrade_when_dir_not_found` are present and pass.
- [ ] Both new tests pin == and != for each invariant (Policy 9).
- [ ] CLI-enumerated extension with a resolvable on-disk dir now triggers the activation grep (Tier A + Tier B both required) — same logic as the on-disk fallback path.
- [ ] CLI-enumerated extension whose resolved dir does NOT exist degrades clean: zero findings, no exception.
- [ ] Emitted finding `file` field uses the marketplace locator `ext:<publisher>.<name>@<version>` format, NOT the resolved on-disk path (host-path leak guard).
- [ ] All 14 existing `test_ext_scan.py` tests still pass (no regression).

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

- [ ] ide-hygiene package tests pass (all existing + 2 new)
- [ ] `validate_manifest.py` green
- [ ] `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] Outer-loop gate: N/A (inner-loop code change only)

## Rollback

Revert the two-line addition to `ext_scan.py` (the dir-resolution block). No other files modified; tests can be left (they'll fail fast against the reverted code until re-removed).

## Risks / Dependencies

- Risk: the resolved path relies on `~/.vscode/extensions/` which is a documented VS Code layout but not guaranteed for forks (VSCodium, Cursor). Degrade-clean handles this (Path.exists check).
- Risk: home-path expansion must NOT appear in emitted findings — the marketplace locator is the canonical `file` value (finding-schema.json `^[^/~]` guard). Verify in tests.
- Depends on wh-5ox.4 (DONE): ide-hygiene skill and ext_scan.py already exist at `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py`.

## Notes / References

- Split from wh-5ox.4 per Policy 3 (surgical). The safe-degrade behavior is explicitly noted in the original wh-5ox.4 description: 'Safe degrade, not a crash.'
- ext_scan.py:9-13 (verified 2026-06-11): describes VS Code on-disk layout `~/.vscode/extensions/*/package.json`.
- ext_scan.py:59-60 (verified 2026-06-11): `_DEFAULT_EDITOR_CLI = 'code'`; CLI path leaves dir empty.
- test_ext_scan.py:195-230 (verified 2026-06-11): existing activation-probe and benign tests are on the on-disk fallback path (dir provided via tmp_path fixture).
notes: SERIALIZE vs ext_scan.py: wh-5ox.16 (CLOSED 2026-06-12) touched _read_manifest (except tuple :134 + docstring). This ticket's activation-grep on the editor-CLI enumeration path is a different code region but the same file — groom against the POST-FIX ext_scan.py; do not co-wave on ext_scan.py.

title: Test backfill (QA G2-G9) + LOW-2 prose clamp for ioc_scan/ext_scan
description:
## Goal / Problem

Backfill 9 QA test-gap cases (G2–G9 identified post-wh-5ox.1 QA) plus one security hardening item (WH LOW-2: length-clamp / control-char strip on untrusted publisher/name/version strings before they enter finding prose). The underlying `ioc_scan.py` and `ext_scan.py` code is CORRECT for the covered paths; these tests pin invariants that were verified manually but not yet encoded in tests.

## Background / Context

- wh-5ox.1 (DONE): `ioc_scan.py` shipped with 20 tests in `deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_ioc_scan.py`.
- wh-5ox.4 (DONE): `ext_scan.py` shipped with 14 tests in `ide-hygiene/scripts/tests/test_ext_scan.py`.
- Gap analysis: G2=symlink-not-followed invariant (ioc_scan), G3=multi-file unique finding ids (ioc_scan), G4=missing-severity default (ext_scan), G5=bare-dir-no-manifest degrade (ext_scan), G6=malformed-watchlist-rows (ext_scan), G7=binary-activation-grepped-intent (ext_scan), G8=signal-past-byte-cap pinned (ioc_scan+ext_scan), G9=extensions_dir-is-file degrade (ext_scan).
- WH LOW-2: a trojanized extension controls its own `package.json`. The `publisher`, `name`, `version` fields are read from the manifest (ext_scan.py:114 `_read_manifest`) and inserted into `exploit_scenario`/`recommendation` prose in findings. An attacker can craft these to be multi-KB strings or embed control characters. Clamp + strip before prose insertion.
- Verified: `test_ioc_scan.py:304-328` already has symlink tests, but the G2 invariant (symlink NOT followed even for in-tree symlinks) needs an explicit assert `!= followed_content` (Policy 9 dual-pin).
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/ioc_scan.py` and `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py` verified to exist (2026-06-11).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

1. **RED** — Add failing tests across the two packages:

   **ioc_scan (deps-scan package):**
   - `test_G2_symlink_invariant_dual_pin`: create a symlink in the tree pointing to a file containing a matching literal; assert zero findings emitted AND assert result does NOT contain the symlink target content (pin `== 0` and `!= symlink_target_findings`).
   - `test_G3_multi_file_unique_ids`: two files each containing the same IOC literal → two findings with DIFFERENT ids (assert `id_a == id_b` FAILS; assert `id_a != id_b`).
   - `test_G8_ioc_signal_past_byte_cap_not_loaded`: a file whose matching literal appears AFTER the read-cap offset → zero findings (pin `== 0` and `!= 1`).

   **ext_scan (ide-hygiene package):**
   - `test_G4_missing_severity_defaults_to_medium`: manifest with no `severity` field → finding severity defaults to a known value (assert `== 'MEDIUM'` and `!= 'HIGH'`).
   - `test_G5_bare_dir_no_manifest_degrade`: extension dir exists but has no `package.json` → zero findings, no raise.
   - `test_G6_malformed_watchlist_rows_skipped`: watchlist entry with missing `name` key → scan completes, no exception.
   - `test_G7_binary_activation_file_is_grepped`: extension dir has a binary `extension.js` → grep runs (or degrades cleanly, no raise); assert scan returns without exception (behavior pinned by intent).
   - `test_G8_ext_activation_signal_past_byte_cap`: activation file whose Tier-A pattern appears AFTER `_MAX_ACTIVATION_BYTES` → zero findings (pin `== 0` and `!= 1`).
   - `test_G9_extensions_dir_is_file_degrade`: the extensions directory path is a regular file, not a dir → zero findings, no raise.
   - `test_LOW2_long_publisher_name_clamped`: ext with publisher/name/version strings >256 chars → finding prose length ≤ 2048; control chars stripped (pin `len(prose) <= 2048` and `!= raw_long_string`).

   Run each package test suite → FAIL.

2. **GREEN** — Minimal fixes:
   - G2–G3, G8 (ioc_scan): likely already correct — tests may pass immediately; if not, fix the identified path.
   - G4: add `severity` default fallback in ext_scan's manifest reader.
   - G5–G7, G9: degrade-clean paths likely correct; tests pin the behavior explicitly.
   - LOW-2: add a `_clamp_prose_field(s, max_len=256)` helper in ext_scan.py that strips `\x00-\x1f` control chars and truncates; apply before inserting publisher/name/version into `exploit_scenario` and `recommendation` fields.
   - Re-run both packages → PASS.

3. **REFACTOR** — Surgical:
   - The LOW-2 clamp is the only logic addition; keep it as a single stdlib one-liner or tiny helper (Policy 2 — nothing speculative).

## Steps (with description)

1. Read `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/ioc_scan.py` (symlink handling and byte-cap sections) to confirm the G2/G3/G8 behaviors before writing tests.
2. Read `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py` (manifest reader ~line 114, activation grep byte-cap ~line 88) to confirm G4–G9 behaviors before writing tests.
3. Write RED tests for ioc_scan in `deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_ioc_scan.py` (3 new functions).
4. Write RED tests for ext_scan in `ide-hygiene/scripts/tests/test_ext_scan.py` (7 new functions including LOW-2).
5. Run both test suites — confirm RED.
6. Implement LOW-2 clamp helper in `ext_scan.py`; verify G4 default; confirm G5–G9 are correct-as-written (no code change needed if tests pass in GREEN immediately).
7. Run both test suites → GREEN.
8. Run full quality gates.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_ioc_scan.py` — MODIFY (add 3 new test functions: G2, G3, G8)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests/test_ext_scan.py` — MODIFY (add 7 new test functions: G4-G9 + LOW-2)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py` — MODIFY (LOW-2 clamp helper + G4 severity default if missing)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 10 new test functions present across the two test files (G2, G3, G8 in test_ioc_scan; G4, G5, G6, G7, G8, G9, LOW-2 in test_ext_scan).
- [ ] Every new test pins both `== expected` and `!= wrong_value` for its central invariant (Policy 9).
- [ ] LOW-2: `test_LOW2_long_publisher_name_clamped` passes — prose length ≤ 2048, control chars stripped; verified by assert on the finding dict.
- [ ] G2 symlink invariant: zero findings from a symlink pointing to a file with a matching literal; assert not-followed (dual-pin).
- [ ] G8 (both packages): literal appearing past the read-cap offset emits zero findings.
- [ ] All previously passing tests continue to pass (no regression).

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/ide-hygiene/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

- [ ] deps-scan package tests pass (all existing + 3 new)
- [ ] ide-hygiene package tests pass (all existing + 7 new)
- [ ] `validate_manifest.py` green
- [ ] `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] Outer-loop gate: N/A (inner-loop code/test only)

## Rollback

Revert test additions (no new test files — only modifications). Revert the LOW-2 clamp addition to ext_scan.py. Other code changes are minimal or non-existent.

## Risks / Dependencies

- Two packages touched in one ticket: deps-scan and ide-hygiene. File-disjoint within each package (test files + ext_scan.py); no intra-wave conflict if the developer sequences the two packages.
- G2 (symlink): `test_ioc_scan.py:304-328` (verified 2026-06-11) already has symlink tests — new test must ADD dual-pin, not duplicate. Read existing tests first to avoid collision.
- wh-5ox.4 (DONE) and wh-5ox.1 (DONE) must be closed before claiming this (their code must exist).

## Notes / References

- QA gaps G2–G9 identified post-wh-5ox.1/wh-5ox.4 QA cycle (local, gitignored; not in committed docs per QA convention).
- `deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_ioc_scan.py:304-354` (verified 2026-06-11): existing symlink and dotdot tests — G2 needs dual-pin addition.
- `ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py:88` (verified 2026-06-11): `_MAX_ACTIVATION_BYTES = 2_000_000` — G8 pinning target.
- `ide-hygiene/scripts/ext_scan.py:114` (verified 2026-06-11): `_read_manifest` — G5/G6/LOW-2 target.
- WH LOW-2 is a SECURITY hardening (injection via manifest field), not a functional gap. Treat as P3 / non-blocking for current wave, but must be in the same batch as G2–G9 (same test files).
notes: SERIALIZE vs ext_scan.py: wh-5ox.16 (CLOSED 2026-06-12) added RecursionError to _read_manifest's except tuple (ext_scan.py:134) + a regression test (test_enumerate_skips_deeply_nested_manifest_no_raise). Groom this ticket's prose-clamp/test-backfill against the POST-FIX ext_scan.py; do not co-wave on ext_scan.py.

title: Forged-Sigstore-provenance-generation SAST rule [supply_chain.py lane]
description:
## Goal
A SAST/floor static rule (supply_chain.py install-script scan OR a sec-detect floor grep, supply-chain-trust category) flagging provenance-bundle GENERATION refs — fulcio.sigstore.dev / rekor.sigstore.dev / media-type application/vnd.dev.sigstore.bundle.v0.3+json / call names generateProvenanceBundle / proposedContent / proofOfPossession — when they appear OUTSIDE recognized release/signing tooling (i.e. in ordinary app or postinstall code, NOT under .github/ CI). Emit a supply-chain design-note finding xref AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002. Restate the thesis as a 1-line core-checklist.md §8 rule: 'provenance present is necessary-not-sufficient; egress-block is the primary control.'
PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE: Sigstore endpoint hosts + bundle media-type are stable — verify vs official Sigstore docs (not the community rule); verify the call-name set vs the primary @sigstore/* API before hardcoding; scope the 'outside release tooling' predicate carefully to avoid FP on legit signing libraries.

## Files (File Ownership — SERIAL on supply_chain.py)
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py + tests  (OR a sec-detect floor grep — decide at groom)
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/core-checklist.md (§8 one-liner) [SHARED w/ T6,T8 -> serialize]
- MODIFY ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain-2.md (detection bullet)

## Acceptance Criteria
