- [ ] uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q → PASS
- [ ] signal-not-block; low volume / high precision

## Rollback

## References

title: sec-detect AI-pass under-triggers: root-manifest-only detection → ai_pass:false on nested/agent-infra repos (dogfood F-008)
description:
## Goal / Problem
From the Gate-2 DOGFOOD self-review (docs/qa/20260611/dogfood/SECURITY-REPORT.md, F-008 / "G-extra"): the
product's OWN `sec-detect` (`detect_tools.py`), run from repo root, returns **`ai_pass:false`** on THIS
13-package Python AI-security codebase — because AI detection is root-manifest-only and the manifests are
nested (none at the repo root declares an AI SDK). **A naive self-scan would SKIP the AI pass entirely** — the
AI-security tool failing to recognize AI-agent infrastructure as such. Real coverage gap (no sink → informational
in this run, exclusion-rule 14, but a true product weakness for any monorepo / nested-package / agent-config repo).

## Scope (fix options)
- **Bounded recursive manifest discovery:** scan N levels (cap depth + exclude .venv/node_modules/.git) for AI
  SDK markers, not just root — so a nested `pyproject.toml`/`package.json` naming an AI SDK triggers the AI pass.
- OR an **"AI pass may be under-triggered" advisory:** when root AI-detection is empty BUT the tree contains
  Python/JS + AI/agent signals (`.claude/`, `agents/`, `mcp.json`, `*.skill`, an AI SDK in a nested manifest),
  emit a SCAN-PLAN advisory recommending the AI pass rather than silently `ai_pass:false`.
- Cite the existing ai-llm.md §9 (the config-poisoning detection just added) as a signal: a repo with `.claude/`
  agent config IS AI infrastructure even if no SDK is in a manifest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A repo with a NESTED AI-SDK manifest (or `.claude/` agent infra) yields `ai_pass:true` OR a stated advisory.
- [ ] Bounded discovery excludes `.venv`/`node_modules`/`.git`/build output (resource discipline).
- [ ] `detect_tools.py` tests pin: root-only AI repo → ai_pass:true (unchanged); nested-AI repo → ai_pass:true (was false).
- [ ] sec-detect package suite green.

## Notes
Found by dogfooding the product on itself (the AI-security tool didn't recognize its own AI-agent infra). Refs:
docs/qa/20260611/dogfood/SECURITY-REPORT.md F-008; `sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py:207`; `ai-llm.md §9`.
notes:
Found by dogfooding the product on itself (the AI-security tool didn't recognize its own AI-agent infra).

GROOMED 2026-06-11 (pre-launch, next-wave batch ① with wh-bqn). VERDICT: READY (with 3 corrections).
- BUG CONFIRMED root-only: `detect_languages`:207 and `_match_signals`:299 both test `(root / signal).exists()` / `_read_manifest_text(root, f)`:290 — root manifests ONLY. `ai_pass` is driven by `_match_signals` over the AI_DEPS table (detect_tools.py:76-88: langchain/transformers/torch/openai/anthropic/mcp). A nested manifest naming an AI SDK is never read → ai_pass:false. Real bug.
- CORRECTION 1 (STALE PATH): the dogfood report is now `.notes/qa/20260611/dogfood/SECURITY-REPORT.md` (gitignored, LOCAL-ONLY — docs/qa was migrated to .notes/qa; a fresh clone won't have it). The body still says `docs/qa/...` (cited twice) — wrong path. The finding is described inline in this ticket, so the report file is provenance only, not required to implement.
- CORRECTION 2 (path precision): `ai-llm.md §9` is real at `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/ai-llm.md:117` ("# 9. AI-assistant config-file poisoning"). Cite the full path. A `.claude/` agent-config repo IS AI infra even with no SDK in a manifest — the §9 signal supports the advisory option.
- CORRECTION 3 (IMPLEMENTATION TRAP): a recursive walker already exists — `_iter_files`:227 (`root.rglob("*")`) — but it is UNBOUNDED + UNFILTERED (no .venv/node_modules/.git/build prune, no depth cap). Do NOT reuse it as-is for manifest discovery: the resource-discipline AC ("exclude .venv/node_modules/.git") would fail. Build a depth-capped, pruned variant (or add exclusions to a manifest-only walk). The existing INFRA_SIGNALS/.claude detection (detect_infra:219) is the cheap signal for the advisory path.
- FILE OWNERSHIP: detect_tools.py + tests/ (+ SKILL.md if the advisory is documented) — disjoint from wh-bqn (ci.yml). Serialize note: wh-7u7 ↔ wh-hxt.1 share detect_tools.py but wh-hxt.1 is a LATER wave (no conflict now). Must land BEFORE the wh-5ox sec-detect tickets.
Refs: .notes/qa/20260611/dogfood/SECURITY-REPORT.md F-008; detect_tools.py:207/:290/:299; _shared/reference/ai-llm.md:117 (§9).
close_reason: Fixed via option (b) advisory: detect_ai_agent_infra() emits ai_agent_infra markers + derived ai_pass_advisory (mirrors kernel_adjacency). Bounded/pruned os.walk (depth 5, prunes .venv/node_modules/.git/etc). ai_pass stays manifest-driven (no over-trigger). Phase-3 dogfood added F-1 (MEDIUM DoS) fix: is_file() guard + 64KiB bounded read on untrusted nested manifests; DEFECT-1 (Policy-9) advisory-suppression test (mutation now dies). Gate 82 passed; schema validates empty+non-empty; F-008 live case fixed (repo root now ai_pass_advisory:true). QA+white-hacker both CLEAN on re-verify. Root-level _read_manifest_text twin → follow-up wh-e1d.

title: KB schema: add typed campaign_family lineage field (appended ADR + schema + validate_kb + backfill -002/-003) — deferred from wh-hxt.10
description:
## Goal / Problem
DEFERRED from the wh-hxt.10 groom (2026-06-10). The Hades RCA (RC3) wants campaign LINEAGE to be queryable —
Shai-Hulud → Mini Shai-Hulud → Hades/Miasma is one evolving family, and a static per-entry IOC list + 90-day
review_by can't track it. wh-hxt.10 ships AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003 with lineage as PROSE + an xref, because
`campaign_family` does NOT exist in the closed KB schema (`kb-entry-schema.json` top-level
`additionalProperties:false`) — adding it FAILS validate_kb today, and per ADR-019 (docs/ARD.md:247 "the
closed vocabulary grows by an appended ADR, never silently") a closed-schema field addition needs an APPENDED
ADR, not an inline edit. This ticket adds the typed field properly.

## Scope (Files to Modify)
- `docs/ARD.md` — an APPENDED ADR (next-free number at write time): "KB entries carry an optional typed
  `campaign_family` field for lineage tracking" — the ADR-019-shaped decision.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/kb-entry-schema.json` — add `campaign_family` (optional string/enum).
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts/validate_kb.py` + tests — accept + validate the field.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/SKILL.md` — mirror the schema doc.
- Backfill `campaign_family: shai-hulud` into AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 + -003 (the prose lineage becomes typed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An appended ADR records the field decision (ADR-019 precedent shape).
- [ ] `kb-entry-schema.json` accepts `campaign_family`; validate_kb test pins BOTH a valid value AND that an
      entry without it still validates (optional). The KB suite stays green.
- [ ] SUPPLY-CHAIN-002/003 carry `campaign_family: shai-hulud`; a query/grep can enumerate the family.
- [ ] `additionalProperties:false` discipline preserved (the field is added to the schema, not bypassed).

## Notes
LOW priority — SUPPLY-CHAIN-003 (wh-hxt.10) ships fine with prose lineage meanwhile; this makes lineage a typed,
queryable field that the campaign-family re-poll trigger (folded into the wh-hxt.8 currency-arm impl) can key on.
Depends on wh-hxt.10 (the entries to backfill must exist). Groom before claiming. Refs: ADR-019 (docs/ARD.md:247),
wh-hxt.10 groom note, docs/research/20260610_hades_shai_hulud_pypi.md §5 RC3.

title: deps-scan S3: _unpinned matches literal 'x' anywhere — pypi specs embed the name, so 'xlrd==1.2.0' false-flags as an unpinned range
description:
## Goal / Problem
S3 ("unpinned ranges") false-positives on fully-pinned pypi deps whose NAME contains the letter x:
_unpinned's regex `[\^~*]|latest|x` matches a literal 'x' ANYWHERE, and the pypi adapter stores the
FULL PEP 508 line as spec (name embedded) — so 'xlrd==1.2.0' reads as "unpinned".

## Reproduction (commands)
cd plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts && nice -n 10 uv run --project . python -c "
import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.'); sys.path.insert(0,'../../_shared/scripts')
from supply_chain import _unpinned
print(_unpinned('xlrd==1.2.0'))   # True  <- the FP
print(_unpinned('boto3==1.0.0'))  # False <- control
"
End-to-end: a requirements.txt containing ONLY 'xlrd==1.2.0' (no lockfile) → scan() emits/feeds an S3
'unpinned ranges' signal for a fully-pinned dep.

## Expected vs Actual
Expected: a fully-pinned '==' dep is never "unpinned", regardless of its name. Actual:
_unpinned('xlrd==1.2.0') → True ('x' in the name matches the wildcard class).

## Suspected root cause
supply_chain.py _unpinned (~:733): the regex treats bare 'x' as a wildcard token without anchoring it
to a version segment, AND it receives the name-embedded pypi spec instead of the bare constraint.
PRE-EXISTING (not a wave-1a hunk — _unpinned was diff context in 0fa17a8); found by the wave-1a
verifier (workflow wsgfeszx6) while probing pypi spec shapes; filed separately per do-not-bundle.

## Files in scope
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py (_unpinned + its S3 call sites)
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/ (regression tests)

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
RED — the repro as failing tests → GREEN — fix → REFACTOR — align S3 with S8's constraint handling.

## Fix (either or both; surgical)
(a) feed _unpinned the name-stripped constraint — `_constraint(dep)` ALREADY EXISTS (added 0871c4f
    next to _exact_pin); pass `_constraint(d)` where S3 calls _unpinned (aligns S3 with S8);
(b) anchor the wildcard token: `[\^~]|\*|\blatest\b|(^|[.\s])x([.\s]|$)` so bare 'x' only matters as a
    version segment.

## Acceptance Criteria (regression tests)
- [ ] the FP class is dead AT THE CALL-SITE PATH: a requirements.txt 'xlrd==1.2.0' (no lockfile)
      produces no S3 signal from signal_s3 (:802) AND no per-dep corroborator hit (:1162). (Direct
      `_unpinned('xlrd==1.2.0') is False` asserts apply ONLY if fix (b) is chosen — under fix (a)
      _unpinned itself is unchanged, so assert through _constraint/the call sites instead.)
- [ ] _unpinned('1.x') / _unpinned('^1.0.0') / _unpinned('*') / _unpinned('latest') stay True (no FN)
- [ ] end-to-end: requirements.txt 'xlrd==1.2.0' (no lockfile) emits NO S3 signal; '^1.0.0' still does
- [ ] Package gate green (baseline 102), serial, nice -n 10, uv run --project

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
(white-hacker per-package gates — the repo's real gates, not the template's generic ruff/mypy example)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q` → green (baseline 102; serial, never `-n auto`)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` → OK

## QA Before Push
- [ ] Happy path: the ticket's probe cases pass end-to-end through `scan()` (not just unit helpers)
- [ ] Edge cases: empty/malformed/hostile manifest+lockfile inputs degrade, never raise
- [ ] Error handling: no new exception path; degrade contracts (`tools_unavailable`, empty struct) intact
- [ ] No regressions: the full deps-scan suite + the ==/!= pairs added by wh-4k9/0871c4f stay green

## Rollback
git revert — regex/call-site change + tests only.

## Commit Message Format
Repo rule (cite, don't restate): `fix(deps-scan): …` + `test:` for the RED commit; author `Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>`; NO AI attribution (CLAUDE.md Policy 12).

## Risks / Dependencies
- FILE OVERLAP: wh-7o1/wh-ln2/wh-0c2 also modify `supply_chain.py` — sequence, never the same wave.
- Fix (a) reuses `_constraint` (0871c4f) — if wh-7o1 lands first and reshapes constraint handling,
  rebase this on top (cheap).

## References
supply_chain.py (_unpinned, _constraint from 0871c4f, the pypi spec shape), the wave-1a verifier
finding (minor #3); sibling wh-7o1 (also touches pypi spec handling — coordinate, don't overlap files
in the same wave).
notes: GROOMED 2026-06-12 — VERDICT: READY. Template-conforming (bug). CITED LINE DRIFT (symbols all resolve; lines grew with the package): _unpinned :733->:866; signal_s3 :802->:938 (the _unpinned call at :942); per-dep S3 corroborator :1162->:1339 ('if has_name_signal and s3 and _unpinned(d["spec"])'); _constraint CONFIRMED at :904 (fix (a) feasible). BASELINE 102->163 (package grew via wh-7o1/wh-ezc/wh-5ox.18). COORDINATION RESOLVED: wh-7o1 CLOSED -> the 'rebase if wh-7o1 reshapes constraint handling' risk is moot; _constraint is in final shape. FILE-OVERLAP serialize STILL ACTIVE: wh-ln2 + wh-0c2 OPEN on supply_chain.py (NEVER co-wave); wh-5ox.18 CLOSED 2026-06-12 also touched the scan() union block -> groom against post-HEAD. PRIORITY P3 defensible: S3 is a GATED corroborator (line 1339 requires has_name_signal AND s3), so a false S3 cannot alone produce a finding; flag for operator if stricter P2 preferred. Read: supply_chain.py:866/904/938/942/1339.
close_reason: Phase-6 close: all 4 gates green (deps-scan 169/baseline 163, sec-detect 94/baseline 90, validate_manifest OK, plugin-validate passed). wh-8ci: (a)+(b) — _unpinned(_constraint(d)) at both S3 call sites + anchored _UNPINNED_RE; FP class (xlrd + extras/markers) dead, no FN. wh-dv4: _iter_files os.walk depth-prune (cap _KERNEL_ADJ_MAX_DEPTH=8) bounds descent (CWE-400 closed; prune proven). QA PASS 4 tiers + white-hacker CLEAN. Follow-up: breadth-DoS proposed to operator /design-ticket.

title: deps-scan: Gradle floor adapter — build.gradle/.kts + libs.versions.toml + gradle.lockfile (Java's other half has ZERO floor coverage)
description:
## Goal / Problem
A Gradle-only Java project gets ZERO dependency extraction on the stdlib floor: the deps-scan dispatch
table (supply_chain.py _DISPATCH) maps Java ONLY to pom.xml/parse_maven. sec-detect FINGERPRINTS
build.gradle/.kts (plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py:36-37 (CROSS-PACKAGE — read-only context, not in this ticket scope)) and normalize_deps maps a 'gradle' label for TOOL-ASSISTED
output (deps-scan/scripts/normalize_deps.py:22) — so the tool-assisted path works when a scanner is installed (ADR-003
degrade is honest) — but the floor sees no deps, so S1-S8 (incl. the known-compromised watchlist) are
blind for the majority of modern Java + all Android.

## Background / Context
Precedent for pragmatic regex extraction of a code-shaped manifest: the Gemfile adapter (_GEM_RE).
Gradle build scripts are arbitrary code executed at build — also an S6/S7 surface. Naming contract:
"group:artifact" (matches OSV Maven ecosystem + parse_maven).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
RED — fixture build.gradle/.kts + libs.versions.toml + gradle.lockfile cases failing through scan() →
GREEN — parse_gradle + dispatch entries → REFACTOR — share helpers with parse_maven where natural.

## Steps
1. parse_gradle: regex-extract implementation/api/compileOnly/runtimeOnly/testImplementation
   ("group:artifact:version") string coordinates + the map form (group:, name:, version:) from
   build.gradle AND build.gradle.kts. NOT a Groovy parser (the _GEM_RE precedent).
2. Version catalogs: gradle/libs.versions.toml via the defensive tomllib pattern; resolve libs.x refs
   where statically possible; else wildcard-only degrade.
3. gradle.lockfile (opt-in locking): parse 'group:artifact:version=configurations' lines as the
   RESOLVED source (mirrors package-lock; strongest signal).
4. Surface build.gradle/.kts via script_files so S6/S7 scan them (arbitrary build-time code).
5. Dispatch: add ("build.gradle", parse_gradle, "java", ...) + the .kts variant; never-raise/
   degrade-to-empty; settings.gradle multi-module = best-effort root only (document).

## Files to Create/Modify
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py (parse_gradle + dispatch entries)
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/ (new test module for the Gradle forms)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] string-coordinate, map-form, and .kts declarations extract name="group:artifact" (==/!= pairs)
- [ ] a known-bad coordinate in gradle.lockfile flags S8 at the locked version; a safe locked version
      does not (needs the wh-4k9 version-aware semantics — already shipped)
- [ ] a catalog-managed version resolves when the catalog defines it; unresolvable → wildcard-only
- [ ] build.gradle/.kts appear in script_files (S6/S7 coverage); no lifecycle false-claims
- [ ] malformed/hostile gradle files never raise (degrade to empty struct)
- [ ] Package gate green (baseline 102): nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
(white-hacker per-package gates — the repo's real gates, not the template's generic ruff/mypy example)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q` → green (baseline 102; serial, never `-n auto`)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` → OK

## QA Before Push
- [ ] Happy path: the ticket's probe cases pass end-to-end through `scan()` (not just unit helpers)
- [ ] Edge cases: empty/malformed/hostile manifest+lockfile inputs degrade, never raise
- [ ] Error handling: no new exception path; degrade contracts (`tools_unavailable`, empty struct) intact
- [ ] No regressions: the full deps-scan suite + the ==/!= pairs added by wh-4k9/0871c4f stay green

## Verification
AC probes as committed tests through scan(); plus one fixture mirroring a real Android-style module
(catalog + kts) to prove the floor produces value (ADR-003).

## Rollback
git revert — a new adapter + dispatch entries; no existing-adapter changes.

## Commit Message Format
Repo rule (cite, don't restate): `feat(deps-scan): …`; author `Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>`; NO AI attribution (CLAUDE.md Policy 12).

## Risks / Dependencies
- FILE OVERLAP: wh-7o1/wh-8ci/wh-ln2 also modify `supply_chain.py` — sequence, never the same wave.
- Regex extraction of Groovy/Kotlin DSL will miss exotic forms — ACCEPTED floor behavior (ADR-003:
  confidence-capped, tool-assisted path covers the rest); do not chase DSL completeness.
- Adds gradle script_files to the S6/S7 surface — wh-ln2's scrub (if landed first) must cover the new
  script_path values (it does by design: one helper at the builders).

## Notes
Storage/Schema: N/A. Out of scope: executing Gradle, parsing settings.gradle includes, Kotlin DSL
evaluation. Sequencing: independent of wh-7o1 (land either order).

## References
supply_chain.py (_DISPATCH dispatch, _GEM_RE precedent, parse_maven naming), detect_tools.py:36-37,
normalize_deps.py:22, ADR-003 (floor must produce value), wh-7o1 (version-extraction sibling), wh-k6l.
close_reason: Gradle floor adapter (build.gradle/.kts + libs.versions.toml + gradle.lockfile) shipped + verified (11c2996, dogfood 2026-06-13). Gradle code is correct (properly reuses the defensive _read_toml). Dogfood F-001 (deep-nested-TOML RecursionError) is a PRE-EXISTING _read_toml bug it merely gave a new path to — filed standalone as wh-0uy (P2), not a wh-0c2 defect.

title: deps-scan: scrub control chars/newlines from attacker-controlled strings embedded in exploit_scenario (spec/s4_target/s5_target)
description:
## Goal / Problem
Findings text is an egress surface to the human reader, and supply_chain.py embeds attacker-controlled
manifest strings RAW into it: _make_finding (:1199; raw interpolation at :1206-1209) interpolates name, dep['spec'] and
target (s5_target or s4_target) into exploit_scenario — and the SAME class exists in the other two
scenario builders: _make_kernel_finding (:1047, interpolates build_file) and _make_script_finding
(:1236, interpolates script_path). PoC (wave-1a security review, PoC3): a spec of '\n\x1b[31mFAKE-CRITICAL' leaks a
newline + ANSI into the rendered report — a hostile manifest can visually forge severity lines.
PRE-EXISTING surface (wh-4k9's new `resolved` value never reaches finding text — PoC'd); filed
separately per do-not-bundle.

## Background / Context
The reviewed repo is untrusted input (Agents Rule of Two). No sanitization precedent exists in the
package (fold_name/unicodedata is S5 typosquat folding, not output escaping) — this ADDS the missing
render-time scrub at the single point of embedding.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
RED — tests pinning ==scrubbed AND !=raw → GREEN — a pure _scrub helper applied in _make_finding →
REFACTOR — one embedding path, no scattered escaping.

## Steps
1. Add a pure `_scrub(s: str) -> str`: strip C0/C1 control chars + ANSI CSI/OSC escapes, collapse
   newlines/tabs to a single space, cap length (~200 chars with an ellipsis marker).
2. Apply it in ALL THREE scenario builders — _make_finding (:1206), _make_kernel_finding (:1047),
   _make_script_finding (:1236) — to every repo-derived value interpolated into exploit_scenario
   (name, dep['spec'], target/s4_target/s5_target, build_file, script_path). One helper, one
   concern, three call sites — same render-time-scrub surface, NOT a bundle.
3. Tests: the PoC string renders inert; legitimate specs ('^1.0.0', '==1.2.3', git+https URLs) pass
   through unchanged (the != pair).

## Files to Create/Modify
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py (_scrub + _make_finding)
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/ (the PoC as a regression test)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] the PoC spec ('\n\x1b[31mFAKE-CRITICAL') cannot inject a newline or ANSI into exploit_scenario
      (== scrubbed) AND a benign spec is byte-identical after scrub (!= over-scrubbed)
- [ ] every repo-derived interpolation across the THREE scenario builders goes through _scrub
      (grep-assertable: no bare f-string use of name / dep['spec'] / target / build_file /
      script_path in _make_finding, _make_kernel_finding, _make_script_finding)
- [ ] findings stay schema-valid (validate_findings green in the package tests)
- [ ] Package gate green (baseline 102), serial, nice -n 10, uv run --project

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
(white-hacker per-package gates — the repo's real gates, not the template's generic ruff/mypy example)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q` → green (baseline 102; serial, never `-n auto`)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` → OK

## QA Before Push
- [ ] Happy path: the ticket's probe cases pass end-to-end through `scan()` (not just unit helpers)
- [ ] Edge cases: empty/malformed/hostile manifest+lockfile inputs degrade, never raise
- [ ] Error handling: no new exception path; degrade contracts (`tools_unavailable`, empty struct) intact
- [ ] No regressions: the full deps-scan suite + the ==/!= pairs added by wh-4k9/0871c4f stay green

## Verification
The AC probes as committed tests; plus re-run the security review's PoC3 one-liner and confirm inert.

## Rollback
git revert — additive helper + call-site changes.

## Commit Message Format
Repo rule (cite, don't restate): `fix(deps-scan): …`; author `Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>`; NO AI attribution (CLAUDE.md Policy 12).

## Risks / Dependencies
- FILE OVERLAP: wh-7o1/wh-8ci/wh-0c2 also modify `supply_chain.py` — sequence, never the same wave.
- Over-scrubbing risk: legitimate specs (git+https URLs, `^1.0.0`) must pass byte-identical — pinned
  by the != AC; findings must stay schema-valid (validate_findings in the package gate).
- If wh-0c2 lands first, its gradle script_path values are covered by the same builders — no extra work.

## Notes
Storage/Schema: N/A. Out of scope: report-renderer (sec-report) hardening — this fixes at the source.

## References
Wave-1a security review (info finding, PoC3); supply_chain.py _make_finding (:1199-1230), _make_kernel_finding (:1047), _make_script_finding (:1236);
Agents Rule of Two posture; ADR-019.
notes: GROOMED 2026-06-12 — VERDICT: READY. Template-conforming (task). CITED LINE DRIFT (symbols resolve; package grew 102->169 tests): _make_kernel_finding :1047->:1204; _make_finding :1199->:1389 (raw interpolation now within :1389+); _make_script_finding :1236->:1423. _scrub to be created. BASELINE 102->169. COORDINATION: wh-7o1 + wh-8ci now CLOSED -> the ONLY remaining supply_chain.py overlap is wh-0c2 (OPEN, next in the serial lane; NEVER co-wave). wh-8ci's S3 _unpinned change does NOT touch the _make_* builders -> no conflict with this scrub. Scope OK (one _scrub helper, three same-surface call sites in one file = the render-time-scrub concern, not a bundle). Read: supply_chain.py:1204/1389/1423.
close_reason: Output-egress scrub shipped (ADR-019). Pure _scrub(s) (C0/C1 + ANSI CSI/OSC strip · newline/tab + U+2028/U+2029 collapse to single space · Cf bidi/zero-width/BOM drop · ~200-char cap; never raises, ADR-003) applied at all 3 exploit_scenario builders (_make_finding name/spec/target, _make_kernel_finding build_file, _make_script_finding script_path). TDD: round-0 9 tests; QA+white-hacker independently found D1/F-1 (MEDIUM — Unicode U+2028/2029 + bidi/Cf survived the ASCII-only strip, re-enabling forge/Trojan-Source); round-1 fix (unicodedata category pass) closed it + 5 regression tests incl. e2e through scan(). deps-scan 169->183; manifest+plugin green. Independent TL adversarial re-probe: all D1 vectors neutralized, benign non-ASCII byte-identical, never raises. No git commit (operator-gated).

title: Tool-lifecycle codification: reusable retire→replace runbook + diversity-by-design registry policy (references wh-hxt.3's strategy ADR)
description:
## Goal
Codify the reusable RETIRE→REPLACE runbook + the DIVERSITY-BY-DESIGN registry policy. This ticket does
NOT append the strategy ADR — **wh-hxt.3 owns THE single strategy ADR** (CONTAIN-primary + the lifecycle
as defense-in-depth; the §9.3 ADR-collision decision, recorded 2026-06-09). This ticket REFERENCES that
ADR and turns two of its stages into operational artifacts. Append a new ADR ONLY if the runbook
mechanics genuinely need their own decision record (number computed at write time, never pre-claimed).

## Deliverable 1 — the reusable RETIRE→REPLACE runbook
Document the path: QUARANTINE (demote behind the capability interface; degrade to the floor — ADR-003)
→ SELECT a replacement (re-run the wh-xn0 ADMIT gates) → RECORD the decision → RETIRE (remove from
SCANNER_PREFERENCE + registry; handle the lock-test pin regexes). **Trivy is the worked first example:
wh-d5b (quarantine) → wh-nvk (select + permanent removal).** IMPORTANT correction (supersedes the
original framing): the quarantine stage is a STOPGAP BEFORE PERMANENT RETIREMENT, not a reversible
demotion — wh-nvk's drop-Trivy decision superseded wh-d5b's "returns when cleared" leg. The runbook must
state both exits: (a) retire permanently (the Trivy path), (b) restore only if the replacement
selection is abandoned. The next tool failure must be ROUTINE, not a fresh investigation.

## Deliverable 2 — DIVERSITY-BY-DESIGN as registry policy
A stated policy in tool-registry.md: >=2 vendors per high-value capability where a compliant second
source exists; cross-check sources (OSV-Scanner sanity-checks Grype). HONEST FRAMING (per the strategy +
the 2026 evidence): diversity is BLAST-RADIUS REDUCTION, not prevention — it is cited as the fix in zero
2026 postmortems; a backdoor you can't see hits all vendors equally; every added tool is another
supply-chain surface that must pass ADMIT + PIN+VERIFY + CONTAIN. Overlaps to reconcile: wh-nvk RQ3
validates the diversity thesis (cite its scorecard, don't re-derive); the policy text lands in
tool-registry.md — the same file wh-xn0 (columns) / wh-nvk (rows) / wh-d5b (caveat) / wh-hxt.1 (health
column) touch: SEQUENCE this ticket AFTER wh-nvk's registry rewrite to avoid a 5-writer collision.

## Dependencies (now wired in bd, not just prose)
- wh-hxt.3 — the strategy ADR this runbook cites (blocked-by).
- wh-nvk — the worked example completes there; and its registry rewrite must land before this ticket's
  policy text touches the same file (blocked-by).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The RETIRE→REPLACE runbook documented (quarantine→select→record→retire), Trivy worked through it
      as the example, with the stopgap-not-reversible correction and both exit paths stated
- [ ] Diversity-by-design stated as registry policy WITH the honest blast-radius-not-prevention framing
- [ ] References wh-hxt.3's ADR (no competing lifecycle ADR; a runbook-mechanics ADR only if genuinely
      needed, next-free number at write time)
- [ ] Reconciled with wh-nvk's diversity-thesis scorecard + sequenced after its registry rewrite

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md (RETIRE + DIVERSIFY stages, the CONTAIN
reframe); docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (§9.3 ADR ownership); wh-hxt.3 (THE
strategy ADR), wh-d5b + wh-nvk (the worked example), wh-xn0 (ADMIT gates), wh-hxt.1 (health column);
ADR-003/015.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-10 (pre-claim, post wave-2-keystone + ADR-026/027/028 + wh-hxt.5/.6/.7 creation — corrections BIND): VERDICT READY, but WAVE MOVED 3→4. (1) KEY SEQUENCING (the body's own intent, now enforceable): Deliverable 2's policy text lands in tool-registry.md — the SAME file the registry rewrite wh-hxt.7 (created 2026-06-10) rewrites under the single-writer rule (ADR-025 §5/ADR-027 §5). bd dep WIRED 2026-06-10: wh-hxt.2 ← wh-hxt.7. The old 'sequence after wh-nvk's registry rewrite' pointed at the SPIKE (closed); the impl is wh-hxt.7. → wh-hxt.2 is NOT wave-3-parallel; it runs wave 4 (with wh-hxt.6/wh-hxt.1). (2) Cites resolve to RATIFIED ADRs now: 'wh-hxt.3's strategy ADR' = ADR-024 (CONTAIN primary; §1 demotes DIVERSIFY to blast-radius — the body's honest framing is now ADR text: cite ADR-024 §1 + ADR-027 §3, don't re-derive). The stopgap-not-reversible correction is FORMALIZED: ADR-027's Supersedes explicitly supersedes wh-d5b's temporary 'returns when cleared' framing — the runbook cites it. (3) The worked example is now COMPLETE end-to-end and richer than the body knew: QUARANTINE wh-d5b ✓ → SELECT the wh-nvk scorecard (RQ2/RQ3 — 5 dimensions; GO/EXTEND/REJECT) ✓ → RECORD ADR-027 ✓ → RETIRE mechanics = wh-hxt.7's steps (SCANNER_PREFERENCE drops + registry rows/Rejected subsections + the test_registry_lock.py:51 pin-regex retirement — the 'handle the lock-test pin regexes' step the body names is concretely specified there; cite, don't re-derive). (4) SELECT's ADMIT gates are now the ratified swap-criteria chain: admissibility ADR-025 (License-gate/Egress-gate) → the 5-dimension scorecard (wh-nvk report §2, incl. the AC#6 wrapper-shape check + AC#5 in-repo checksum-value pin for unsigned releases) → ADR-024 §5 admission (pin+verify) — diagrammed in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md §7.1 (the four-gates interaction + swap-criteria chain); the runbook codifies THAT chain. (5) NEW runbook step since the body was written: a tool retired FOR COMPROMISE also gets a Gate-2-validated watchlist entry (ADR-026; the Trivy entry is wh-k6l's instantiation, blocked on wh-hxt.5/.6 — the HARD ORDERING: gate_data_edit registered before the watchlist file exists). (6) Deliverable-1 home: a docs/ runbook (ADR-024 §7 precedent — operational guidance stays OUT of append-only ADR prose; the CI-hardening runbook from the contain-doc draft (c) is a SEPARATE artifact — do not merge them). 'A runbook-mechanics ADR only if genuinely needed' stands; last written = ADR-028, numbers at write time. (7) Deps state: wh-hxt.3 ✓ closed, wh-nvk ✓ closed, wh-hxt.7 OPEN (the new blocker). Scope OK: one runbook doc + (post-.7) one registry policy section; no overlap with wh-hxt.4's research doc.

title: Maintenance/staleness health: cadence/EOL gate on ADMIT + a staleness signal in the MONITOR feed
description:
## Goal
Add the MAINTENANCE/STALENESS health dimension to the tool lifecycle — the trivy-mcp lesson (an
unmaintained wrapper pinning a stale binary is a supply-chain risk distinct from compromise). THREE parts
(extended 2026-06-09 per loop-leverage G6 — RETIRE today has no tool/watchlist codepath):
(1) ADMIT gate — a tool must be actively maintained (release cadence, last-commit recency, NOT
EOL/feature-complete/archived); extends wh-xn0's admissibility gates + adds a registry health column.
(2) MONITOR signal — a staleness poller arm (latest-release age, archived/EOL/feature-complete flags via
the GitHub API) → proposes a human-gated swap BEFORE a tool is a liability (would have caught
gitleaks→Betterleaks and trivy-mcp). NOTE: this poller arm does NOT exist in code today —
poll_feeds.py:69 PARSERS={osv,atlas,atom}, no GitHub-release/staleness parser; this ticket DEFINES the
staleness signal; wh-5es owns the poller PLUMBING (one feed mechanism, don't fork it).
(3) RETIRE/SUPERSEDE paths (NEW — loop-leverage G6): today staleness_check.py:26-30,50-52 --archive ages
out KB entries ONLY — there is no codepath to retire a stale/compromised TOOL from
SCANNER_PREFERENCE/tool-registry, nor to supersede a WATCHLIST entry on a newer advisory (e.g.
gitleaks→Betterleaks). Add both: a tool-retire path (consumes the wh-hxt.2 runbook) and a
watchlist-entry supersede/remove path (in the shared OSV shape; Gate-2-validated).

## Notes
Deterministic where possible (a maintenance score from release/commit dates — Policy 5, not LLM judgment).
Human-gated proposals only (ADR-012, never auto-swap). Registry-file coordination: the health column
lands in tool-registry.md, which wh-xn0 (columns) / wh-nvk (rows) / wh-hxt.2 (policy) also edit —
sequence after wh-nvk's rewrite.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tool-registry gains a maintenance/health field; ADMIT rejects EOL/archived/feature-complete tools (or admits with a flagged caveat + a tracked successor, e.g. gitleaks→Betterleaks)
- [ ] a staleness signal DEFINITION (thresholds + the GitHub-API fields) handed to the wh-5es feed arm; poller plumbing not duplicated here
- [ ] a tool RETIRE path + a watchlist-entry SUPERSEDE path exist (extending staleness_check.py's KB-only --archive precedent), human-gated, Gate-2-validated for watchlist edits
- [ ] deterministic maintenance check (release/commit dates), not an LLM judgment
- [ ] reconciled with wh-xn0 (gates) + wh-5es (feed) + wh-hxt.2 (runbook); never auto-swaps

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md (ADMIT + MONITOR + RETIRE stages);
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G6; staleness_check.py:26-30,50-52 is KB-only);
poll_feeds.py:69 (no staleness parser yet); wh-xn0, wh-5es, wh-hxt.2, wh-562 (Gate-2); ADR-012.
notes: GROOMED 2026-06-12 — VERDICT: NEEDS SPLIT. Spike deps wh-xn0/wh-5es/wh-nvk/wh-562 all CLOSED (unblocked on research). BUT it is a 3-PART ticket spanning multiple files/concerns -> Policy 3 split: (1) ADMIT gate + tool-registry health column; (2) MONITOR staleness-signal DEFINITION handed to wh-5es (poller plumbing NOT here); (3) RETIRE/SUPERSEDE paths extending staleness_check.py's KB-only --archive. Part (3) DEPENDS on wh-hxt.2 (OPEN — the retire->replace runbook it consumes). CORRECTION: order.md misclassified this as the 'detect_tools.py lane' — it does NOT touch detect_tools.py (touches tool-registry.md + staleness_check.py + poll_feeds.py); the earlier detect_tools.py serialize note was MISPLACED and is superseded by this. REAL coordination: tool-registry.md shared with wh-hxt.2 (serialize). ACTION: re-design via /design-ticket into 3 child tickets before launch; NOT a clean next-wave pick. Read: poll_feeds.py:69 (no staleness parser), staleness_check.py:26-30/50-52 (KB-only archive).
close_reason: SPLIT (NEEDS-SPLIT groom verdict) into 3 focused children under wh-hxt: wh-hxt.17 (ADMIT maintenance/health — tool-registry column + deterministic score), wh-hxt.18 (MONITOR staleness-signal definition for wh-5es), wh-hxt.19 (RETIRE/SUPERSEDE paths extending staleness_check.py --archive; deps wh-hxt.2). 3-part umbrella replaced; each child is template-conforming + file-disjoint. A+B launchable now (A serializes vs wh-hxt.2 on tool-registry.md); C blocked on wh-hxt.2.

title: Spike: GDPR PII data-minimization in the review pipeline (findings/TRIAGE/KB/report) (→ next-available ADR)
description:
## Research Question

The agent reads **untrusted code that may contain personal data** (PII in source, comments, fixtures,
logs, seed/test data). GDPR data-minimization asks: **what personal data may end up in the agent's OWN
artifacts, and what rule/redaction keeps it out?** This is a POSTURE decision on the agent's pipeline
output — disjoint from the *tool* admissibility gates (**Spike A → ADR-024**). Each RQ → a decision:

- **RQ1 — Enumerate the PII leak surface.** Which output fields can carry copied source / personal
  data: `VULN-FINDINGS.json` (`exploit_scenario`, `first_link` snippets), `TRIAGE.json`,
  `ai-attack-kb/reference/*` entries, `SECURITY-REPORT.md`? Map each to its risk.
- **RQ2 — The rule (extend, don't duplicate; don't mis-state).** The posture already says "**never
  store credentials in output, logs, tickets, or KB**" (`agents/white-hacker.md:41`) and the triage
  decision-maker already sees only the **minimal** `{file,line,category,diff}` (`:69`). PII ⊋
  credentials, so this is a genuine **extension of :41** (credentials → all personal data). Decide the
  precise rule. NOTE: do **NOT** frame it as "extend `{file,line,category,diff}`" — that tuple is the
  minimal contract *by design* and excludes prose; the question is whether the **snippet/diff/
  exploit_scenario** fields need a **redaction/constraint** so PII isn't copied verbatim into an
  artifact (make it leak *less*, not carry *more*).
- **RQ3 — Enforcement: deterministic or posture-only?** Is a deterministic redaction pass warranted
  (reuse the `secrets-scan/scripts/redact.py` pattern — it already never emits the secret value), or
  is a posture rule + the minimal-context contract sufficient? If deterministic, where does it run
  (report render? finding emit?) and does it need a schema constraint?
- **RQ4 — Data-residency / retention.** `SECURITY-REPORT.md` is gitignored (transient) — good; the
  **KB is git-tracked → must carry ZERO PII** (already partly covered by ADR-012 "never store the
  security contact in the KB"). State the residency + no-retention posture for each artifact.
- **RQ5 — (OUT OF SCOPE — DOCUMENT ONLY) the agent's own model call.** Reviewed code → Anthropic for
  the LLM pass is a GDPR **processor** consideration: it runs on the user's Claude Code subscription
  under the user's lawful basis. **Document the data-flow** (what leaves the machine, the processor
  relationship) in the report; do **NOT** attempt to solve it here (it is inherent to the model).

## Timebox

**Timebox:** ~1 day (a posture decision + a small enforcement call; lighter than Spike A).

## Background / Context

- GDPR **data-minimization** + the **Agents Rule of Two** (`agents/white-hacker.md:39-41`: never
  co-locate untrusted input + secrets + egress). The existing posture covers **credentials**; PII is
  the strict superset this spike generalizes to.
- **Sibling of Spike A** (tool admissibility / ADR-024). This one touches the agent's OWN output
  (agent md posture + `sec-report` render + finding schema), **NOT** `tool-registry.md` — disjoint
  files, so the two can run in parallel (Policy 3).
- **Binding to cite, not re-debate:** ADR-008 (recall/precision separation — the minimal triage
  contract), ADR-012 (KB never stores the security contact — the PII precedent for the KB),
  the agent Posture (`:41`), the existing `secrets-scan/scripts/redact.py` (the redaction pattern).

## Investigation Approach

1. **Leak-surface map (RQ1).** Read `_shared/reference/finding-schema.json` + the agent's output
   contract; list every field that can hold a copied source snippet or free-text that could embed PII.
2. **Extend-vs-redact (RQ2/RQ3).** Compare: (a) posture rule only ("never copy PII verbatim; reference
   by `file:line`, not value"), vs (b) a deterministic redaction pass modeled on
   `secrets-scan/scripts/redact.py` (which proves the never-emit-the-value pattern works + is tested).
   Pick the lightest sufficient enforcement (Policy 2).
3. **KB residency (RQ4).** Confirm the KB-no-PII rule (extends ADR-012) + the transient/gitignored
   status of the live report; state the retention posture per artifact.
4. **Model-call note (RQ5).** Draft a short, accurate GDPR-processor data-flow note (document only).

## Research Guidelines for AI Agents

```markdown
1. Read the finding schema + agent output contract + secrets-scan/redact.py before deciding.
2. Web search (2026) only if needed for GDPR data-minimization best-practice phrasing.
3. Decide the lightest sufficient rule (posture vs deterministic redaction) — Policy 2.
4. Follow-up: ONE implementation ticket.
```

## Expected Deliverables

- [ ] Research report at `docs/research/20260609_gdpr_pii_data_minimization.md`.
- [ ] **ADR-025 appended to `docs/ARD.md`** (append-only): the PII data-minimization rule (extends
      `:41` credentials→PII), the enforcement decision (posture vs deterministic redaction), the
      KB/report residency + no-retention posture, and the documented model-call GDPR-processor note.
- [ ] ONE implementation follow-up ticket (the posture line in the agent md + any redaction helper/
      schema constraint) — no production code in this spike.

### Research Report File
```bash
touch docs/research/20260609_gdpr_pii_data_minimization.md
```

## Findings Template

```markdown
# Research: GDPR PII data-minimization in the review pipeline
**Date:** 2026-06-09  **Spike Ticket:** <id>  **Status:** Draft | Final
## Summary
## PII leak surface (per output artifact/field)
## The rule (extend :41 credentials→PII) + why NOT "extend {file,line,category,diff}"
## Enforcement: posture-only vs deterministic redaction (redact.py pattern)
## Data-residency / retention (KB git-tracked = ZERO PII; report gitignored/transient)
## Model-call GDPR-processor note (documented, not solved)
## Recommendation → ADR-025 + ONE impl ticket
## References
```

## Create Follow-up Tickets — EXIT CRITERIA

1. **ADR-025 appended to `docs/ARD.md`** — the PII data-minimization rule + enforcement + residency +
   the model-call note.
2. **THEN exactly ONE implementation ticket:** the posture line in `agents/white-hacker.md` (extend
   `:41` to PII), the chosen enforcement (a redaction pass or a schema/contract constraint), and tests
   that PII placed in a fixture finding does NOT appear verbatim in the emitted artifact.

- [ ] ADR-025 appended
- [ ] One implementation ticket created

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RQ1–RQ4 answered → a stated decision; RQ5 documented as out-of-scope (model-call note).
- [ ] The rule is framed as an **extension of `:41` (credentials→PII)** + a redaction/constraint —
      NOT as "extend `{file,line,category,diff}`".
- [ ] Enforcement decision justified by Policy 2 (lightest sufficient).
- [ ] KB-no-PII + report-residency posture stated.
- [ ] ADR-025 appended; ONE impl ticket filed; no production code changed in this spike.
- [ ] Research report written; timebox (~1 day) respected.

## Notes / Open Questions

- Likely outcome: a **posture rule** ("reference PII by `file:line`, never copy the value") + reuse the
  proven `secrets-scan/scripts/redact.py` pattern for the snippet/report path — rather than a new
  schema field. Confirm against the leak-surface map.
- The KB rule (no PII) extends ADR-012's "never store the security contact" — make the generalization
  explicit, append-only.
- Sibling: **Spike A (ADR-024)** owns tool license/data-egress admissibility; this ticket is disjoint.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-09: (1) DE-HARD-CODE ADR-025 → next-available at write time (wh-xn0 also pre-claims 024; both OPEN = race; first-to-write takes 024). (2) MISCITE: the 'never store the security contact in the KB' precedent is ADR-018 (docs/ARD.md:239), NOT ADR-012 (only the KB-vs-checklist structure) — re-point RQ4/Background/Notes to ADR-018. (3) the body assumes 024-is-taken but bd shows no dep on wh-xn0 — declare the coupling or drop the specific-number cross-refs. Posture (:41, :69) + redact.py reuse verified CORRECT.

title: resource_probe helper: deterministic cores/free-mem/load + suggested concurrency cap
description:
## Goal / Problem

Implement `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/resource_probe.py` per ADR-023 so the shipped agent can read a reliable, stdlib-only concurrency cap (`suggested_max_parallel`) instead of guessing from inline shell heuristics. Wire the result into `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md` section 'Execution budget' (lines ~150-164: currently uses inline getconf/nproc/vm_stat/free shell snippets).

## Background / Context

- ADR-023 (`docs/ARD.md:281-294`): accepted; binds RQ1-5 for this implementation.
- Spike wh-bob (DONE): PoC at `docs/research/poc-resource-probe/resource_probe.py` + `test_resource_probe.py` proves the stdlib approach (10 tests green). The PoC is THROWAWAY; this ticket ports it to the production location.
- `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md:150-164` (verified 2026-06-11): section 'Execution budget' currently instructs the agent to run `getconf _NPROCESSORS_ONLN || nproc || sysctl -n hw.ncpu` and `vm_stat / memory_pressure` shell commands. Replace with: run `python -c "import resource_probe; import json; print(json.dumps(resource_probe.probe()))"` and use `suggested_max_parallel` as the cap.
- `_shared/scripts/` already exists with: `degradation.py`, `policy_detect.py`, `validate_findings.py`, `validate_watchlist.py`, `pyproject.toml`, `conftest.py`, `uv.lock`, `tests/` (verified 2026-06-11). No `resource_probe.py` yet.
- RQ6 (GROOM note): REPORT-ONLY -- do NOT bump `finding-schema.json`. The probe result surfaces in the agent context as a number, not a finding.
- Agent target: SHIPPED agent `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md` ONLY. The old `.claude/agents/white-hacker.md` was DELETED (ADR-029, confirmed by git status `D .claude/agents/white-hacker.md`).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

1. **RED** - Write failing tests in `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/test_resource_probe.py` (NEW):
   - `test_cores_positive`: `probe()['cores'] > 0` AND `!= 0`.
   - `test_load_nonneg`: `probe()['load1'] >= 0.0` AND NOT negative.
   - `test_free_mb_sane_or_none`: free_mb is None OR `> 0`; never a string.
   - `test_suggested_at_least_one`: `suggested_max_parallel >= 1` AND `!= 0`.
   - `test_probe_shape`: result dict has exactly keys `{cores, load1, free_mb, suggested_max_parallel, constants}`.
   - `test_cap_never_exceeds_ceiling` (monkeypatch): mock cores=100, free_mb=100*1536 -> cap == HARD_CEILING.
   - `test_cap_drops_to_sequential_under_load` (monkeypatch): mock load1 >= cores -> cap == 1.
   - `test_cap_bounded_by_memory` (monkeypatch): mock free_mb=1536*2, cores=8 -> cap <= 2.
   - `test_cap_bounded_by_cores` (monkeypatch): mock cores=3, HEADROOM=2 -> cap <= 1.
   - `test_unknown_os_skips_memory_divisor` (monkeypatch): force free_mb=None -> cap computed without ZeroDivisionError.
   - Each test pins BOTH `== expected` AND `!= wrong_value` (Policy 9).
   - Run `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests -q` -> FAIL.

2. **GREEN** - Port PoC to production:
   - Create `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/resource_probe.py` from `docs/research/poc-resource-probe/resource_probe.py` (ADR-023-spec-compliant). Remove PoC header; keep constants env-overridable (WH_CAP_HEADROOM, WH_CAP_CEILING, WH_SUBAGENT_MB). Strip the `if __name__ == '__main__'` block.
   - No new third-party dependencies (stdlib-only; ADR-021).
   - Re-run tests -> PASS.

3. **REFACTOR** - Surgical agent-md update:
   - Edit `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md` section 'Execution budget' (~line 155-158): replace inline shell heuristic block with instruction to invoke `resource_probe.probe()` and read `suggested_max_parallel`. Keep surrounding prose intact.

## Steps (with description)

1. Port the 10 PoC tests from `docs/research/poc-resource-probe/test_resource_probe.py:12-64` into new `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/test_resource_probe.py`, adding Policy-9 dual-pin assertions to each.
2. Run the _shared test suite -> FAIL (RED confirmed).
3. Copy `docs/research/poc-resource-probe/resource_probe.py` to `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/resource_probe.py`; replace module docstring with production text; strip the PoC main block.
4. Run the _shared test suite -> PASS (GREEN).
5. Edit `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md:155-158` -- replace inline shell heuristic with a call to `resource_probe.probe()`.
6. Run the full quality gates.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/resource_probe.py` -- CREATE (port from PoC)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/test_resource_probe.py` -- CREATE (port + dual-pin)
- `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md` -- MODIFY (Execution budget section ~line 155-158: replace shell snippet with resource_probe call)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_shared/scripts/resource_probe.py` exists; stdlib-only (no psutil, no GNU parallel, no third-party imports).
- [ ] `probe()` returns a dict with keys `{cores, load1, free_mb, suggested_max_parallel, constants}`; `free_mb` may be None on unknown OS.
- [ ] All 10 tests in `test_resource_probe.py` pass, each with dual-pin `== expected` AND `!= wrong` assertion.
- [ ] `test_unknown_os_skips_memory_divisor` passes without ZeroDivisionError (ADR-003 degrade path).
- [ ] `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md` section 'Execution budget' references `resource_probe.probe()` and reads `suggested_max_parallel` (grep confirms).
- [ ] RQ6: `finding-schema.json` is NOT modified.
- [ ] No regression in existing `_shared/scripts/tests/` tests.

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

```
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```

- [ ] _shared package tests pass (all existing + 10 new resource_probe tests)
- [ ] `validate_manifest.py` green
- [ ] `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] Outer-loop gate: N/A (inner-loop code only; RQ6 forbids schema bump)

## Rollback

Delete `resource_probe.py` and `test_resource_probe.py`. Revert `agents/white-hacker.md` Execution-budget block to its prior shell-heuristic form via `git checkout`.

## Risks / Dependencies

- ADR-003 degrade: the `free_mb=None` branch must skip the memory divisor without ZeroDivisionError. Test `test_unknown_os_skips_memory_divisor` guards this.
- Agent-md edit is surgical: change ONLY the inline shell heuristic block (~line 155-158); do NOT alter surrounding concurrency-weight/mode prose.
- ADR-021 (zero-dep vendor): `resource_probe.py` must NOT introduce third-party imports; pyproject.toml must list NO new third-party packages.
- Depends on wh-bob (DONE).

## Notes / References

- `docs/research/poc-resource-probe/resource_probe.py:26-28` (verified 2026-06-11): constants WH_CAP_HEADROOM=2, WH_CAP_CEILING=8, WH_SUBAGENT_MB=1536 (env-overridable).
- `docs/research/poc-resource-probe/test_resource_probe.py:12-64` (verified 2026-06-11): 10 test functions to port.
- `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md:150-164` (verified 2026-06-11): section 'Execution budget'; inline shell heuristics at ~line 155-158 are the replacement target.
- GROOM NOTE (2026-06-09): agent-md target is `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md` ONLY (the SHIPPED agent). The old `.claude/agents/white-hacker.md` was deleted (ADR-029).
acceptance_criteria: resource_probe.py prints cores/free_mb/load1/suggested_max_parallel on macOS+linux; stdlib-only; unit tests via uv; agent md references it; leakage guard stays green if vendored
notes: GROOMED 2026-06-12 — VERDICT: READY. Template-conforming (task), spike-backed (wh-bob PoC, ADR-023). CITATIONS ACCURATE: PoC at docs/research/poc-resource-probe/resource_probe.py present; white-hacker.md 'Execution budget' at :150 (getconf/nproc snippet :156) is the wire-up target; _shared/scripts/resource_probe.py ABSENT (to create -> no conflict). BASELINE _shared 151 green. Files: NEW _shared/scripts/resource_probe.py + tests/test_resource_probe.py + agents/white-hacker.md wire-up. PARALLEL-SAFE with wh-ln2 (disjoint: _shared + agent-md vs deps-scan/supply_chain.py). RQ6 resolved (REPORT-ONLY, no finding-schema bump). Read: white-hacker.md:150-164, docs/research/poc-resource-probe/.
close_reason: resource_probe.py ported from the wh-bob PoC to _shared/scripts/ (stdlib-only, ADR-021/023): probe() returns {cores,load1,free_mb,suggested_max_parallel,constants}; env-overridable HEADROOM/HARD_CEILING/SUBAGENT_MB; degrade-never-raise (free_mb None / unknown OS, no ZeroDivisionError). white-hacker.md:155-160 Execution-budget wired to resource_probe.probe() (inline getconf/nproc/vm_stat snippets replaced). RQ6 honored (no finding-schema bump). 10 both-direction tests; _shared 151->161; manifest+plugin green. QA 4-tier PASS + white-hacker CLEAN (subprocess is fixed-argv vm_stat, no shell, read-only, no new egress). No git commit (operator-gated).

title: Refine installation: pinned + verified remote installer (curl|bash run in target)
description:
## Goal
A remote ONE-COMMAND installer (curl|bash) run INSIDE a target project that PINS a stable version tag,
shallow-clones to a temp dir, VERIFIES integrity, and vendors/installs white-hacker — and is itself an
exemplar of the supply-chain hygiene the agent preaches (ADR-006 pin-and-verify; 'the agent that scans
supply chains must not be a supply-chain victim').

## Prerequisites
- A SIGNED stable git tag exists to pin (plugin.json is v0.1.0 but no git tag yet — cut + sign one).
- .claude-plugin/marketplace.json + plugins/white-hacker/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (present, validates).
- git + (for the plugin lane) the 'claude' CLI.

## Steps
1. DECIDE THE MODEL (step 1; append an ADR to docs/ARD.md if the distribution model changes):
   (a) PLUGIN lane (ADR-017) — remote wrapper around 'claude plugin marketplace add jaigouk/white-hacker'
       + install, pinned to a tag; auto-updates; per-user.
   (b) VENDORED lane — temp-clone at tag -> copy payload into the target .claude/{agents,skills,commands,
       hooks} or a .white-hacker/ dir; self-contained/committed in the target (good for CI / no per-user
       install) BUT FORKS the skills (drift) and contradicts ADR-017's 'ship generic base + project
       companion'. If chosen, document the refresh path (re-run at a new tag).
   (c) BOTH lanes (plugin for shared use; vendored for committed/CI). Pick explicitly — do not silently fork.
2. Cut + GPG-sign a stable tag (e.g. v0.1.0); installer pins it; verify with 'git verify-tag'.
3. Build the installer (best practice, dogfooding ADR-006):
   - 'git clone --branch <TAG> --depth 1 <repo> "$(mktemp -d)"' + 'trap ... EXIT' cleanup;
   - INTEGRITY VERIFY: signed tag (git verify-tag) and/or a pinned SHA and/or a SHA-256 of the payload —
     the installer must fetch NOTHING unpinned;
   - IDEMPOTENT vendoring: back up / refuse-to-clobber an existing .claude/.white-hacker payload;
   - curl|bash SAFETY: FUNCTION-WRAPPER (whole body in a fn called on the LAST line so a truncated
     download defines functions but runs nothing) + 'set -euo pipefail' + prereq checks +
     '--dry-run'/'--unattended' flags + a review-before-run note;
   - then run sec-init -> writes <target>/.white-hacker/project-profile.json.
4. Update README 'Install & onboarding' with the remote one-liner + the security/pin-verify note.
5. AUDIT the installer itself (run the white-hacker review on it; assert no unpinned fetch).

## Files to Create/Modify
- install.sh (extend/supersede the just-added local one) OR a new install/ entrypoint + a hosted raw URL.
- README.md (Install & onboarding section).
- docs/ARD.md — an ADR IF the distribution model changes (step 1).
- packaging/ — N/A unless a packaged artifact/checksum is added.

## Verification
- 'bash -n install.sh' + shellcheck clean.
- A '--dry-run' in a scratch target prints the pinned clone + verify + idempotent copy WITHOUT mutating.
- Re-run is idempotent (backs up, never silently clobbers).
- grep the script: the ONLY fetched ref is the pinned tag/SHA — zero unpinned 'git clone <branch-less>'/curl of an unpinned URL.

## Rollback
Additive (install.sh + README + optional ADR) — revert the commit. Vendored files in a target are
user-owned; the installer backs up before overwrite, so no destructive change.
design: Dogfood narrative: white-hacker is a SECURITY tool, so its installer must EXEMPLIFY ADR-006 pin-and-verify + curl|bash best practices (function-wrapper for partial-download safety, signed-tag/SHA-256 verify, temp-clone+trap-cleanup, idempotent backup). Patterns: fzf (shallow/temp clone + markered edits), oh-my-zsh (--unattended + backup), rustup/starship (pinned release + checksum). KEY DECISION = vendored-copy vs ADR-017 plugin vs both; vendoring forks the skills (drift) so it needs an explicit ADR + a documented refresh path.
acceptance_criteria: (1) model decision recorded (ADR appended iff the distribution model changes); (2) remote installer pins a tag + verifies integrity (signed-tag/SHA) + is idempotent + uses the function-wrapper + vendors/installs into a target + runs sec-init; (3) installer is self-audited — NO unpinned fetch (ADR-006 dogfood); (4) README Install section updated; (5) bash -n + shellcheck clean
notes: Supersedes/extends the local install.sh added 2026-06-09 (marketplace-add-path + plugin install). User wants the REMOTE, run-in-target, pinned, vendoring flow. Step 1 (the model decision) may justify splitting a /design-ticket spike if the vendor-vs-plugin choice needs real research; otherwise resolve inline + append the ADR. References: ADR-017 (plugin distribution), ADR-006 (pin-and-verify), README Install section, docs/research/spike-07-agent-distribution-and-init-2026-06.md, README:215-218 (the legacy copy-into-user-scope fallback this generalizes).
close_reason: Pinned+verified install.sh DONE + SMOKE-VERIFIED on the now-PUBLIC repo @ v0.1.0. Anonymous curl|bash (fresh env, no creds): resolves latest release tag -> clones pinned -> vendors agent+15 skills+commands, .venv gitignored, clean exit. macOS/Linux detect + pinned-uv auto-install. ADR-021 + README. Smoke caught+fixed 3 bugs (log->stdout pollution, EXIT-trap unbound var, clone noise). Open follow-up wh- for vendor-lane hooks wiring if needed.

title: Category-alias-aware eval scorer (AI/auth/crypto classes)
description: The opus re-baseline (wh-aj6) + spike-11 both show the eval gate's WEAK point is score.py's exact category match, not agent recall. 8 of opus's 10 'FN' were the vuln FOUND at the exact line but labeled with a defensible-but-different vocab category (LLM05 improper-output-handling vs LLM06 excessive-agency; JWT crypto vs AuthN/AuthZ; secret crypto vs config; prompt-injection vs rag-poisoning). Detection-recall is 116/118=0.983 vs strict J=0.917. WHAT: add a category-alias map (or a --lenient mode) so defensible cross-class equivalences don't read as misses; OR calibrate the label vocab for the AI classes. This is spike-11's 'the lever is the scorer, not the corpus'. Do NOT silently inflate the score (that's what produced the prior fake J=1.0) — make the leniency explicit + audited. files: evals/score.py + the label vocab.
acceptance_criteria: score.py supports an explicit, audited category-alias/lenient mode; the 8 wh-aj6 disagreements no longer read as misses under it; strict mode unchanged; documented
close_reason: Opt-in alias-aware lenient scorer in score.py (4 audited symmetric aliases). Strict default UNCHANGED (gate=0.917, drift-guard green); lenient lens=0.975 recovers 7/8 defensible disagreements. py-llm05-ssrf un-aliased (too broad); no silent inflation. 14 score + 183 evals green.

title: Hardened Docker sandbox for deps-scan/S8 testing against untrusted manifests + the OSSF malware snapshot
description: WHY: the deps-scan supply-chain floor is the component that ingests UNTRUSTED package manifests + the real ossf/malicious-packages snapshot (wh-8qw). The floor is proven inert (pure stdlib JSON+regex, no install/exec/network), and the S8 smoke test (wh-2v5) is synthetic+offline — but cloning the real OSSF repo and especially testing against live-malware datasets (DataDog/pypi_malregistry) should NEVER touch the host. Agents Rule of Two: isolate untrusted input from egress. WHAT: a dedicated, opt-in Docker sandbox (Dockerfile + run.sh + README + .dockerignore) that runs the deps-scan tests + the floor with --network none, --read-only, --cap-drop ALL, no-new-privileges, non-root UID, no host writes (read-only mounts only). Base image PINNED by digest (ADR-006). Documents the wh-8qw real-snapshot flow: clone+pin in an isolated network-on step into a volume; analyze that volume network-OFF. The native offline workflow is unchanged (uv run pytest still works, zero host risk). NOTE: this de-risks but does not replace the native test.
acceptance_criteria: docker/ sandbox with Dockerfile (non-root, pinned base, copies only the floor+_shared+sec-detect), run.sh (build|test|scan with the full hardening flags), README (threat model + usage + wh-8qw snapshot flow), .dockerignore; shell scripts pass bash -n; adversarial hardening review (escape/egress/host-access) passes; native uv-run deps-scan tests still green (78)
close_reason: Hardened docker/deps-scan-sandbox/ shipped; 5-lens adversarial review (analysis lane sealed; blocker schema-COPY fixed; fetch container hardened); default test lane verified green (67 passed) via faithful Py3.13 layout sim. docker not built here (no daemon).

title: Fully-offline S8 end-to-end smoke test (on-disk OSSF-shaped snapshot -> load_malware_db -> scan)
description: WHY: S8 (known-bad match) has unit coverage for the loader (test_malware_db.py over the malware-db-sample fixture) and for scan() with a HAND-BUILT dict (test_supply_chain.py:312 db={'evil-pkg':'*'}), but NOTHING exercises the real operator flow end-to-end: an on-disk OSV snapshot shaped like ossf/malicious-packages' osv/malicious/<ecosystem>/<pkg>/MAL-*.json tree -> load_malware_db(dir) -> scan(project, malware_db=db) -> S8 finding. WHAT: add test_s8_offline_smoke.py that builds a tiny OSSF-shaped osv/ tree in tmp_path (real OSV format: schema_version/id/affected[].package.{ecosystem,name}/versions; one explicit-versions pkg + one wildcard/range-only pkg), loads it via the real loader, scans a manifest naming those pkgs + a clean control, and pins (Rule 9, both == and !=): S8 fires HIGH on the bad deps, control stays clean, malware-db NOT in tools_unavailable, doc validates; plus the degraded contrast (no snapshot -> S8 silent + malware-db unavailable, never raises) and that S8 is a conservative NAME match (fires regardless of version). Fully offline, stdlib-only, no network. Documents/derisks the wh-8qw wiring.
acceptance_criteria: new test_s8_offline_smoke.py: loader builds {name:set[versions]} from the OSSF-shaped tree; scan(malware_db=load_malware_db(dir)) emits a HIGH S8 supply-chain finding on the bad dep + control clean + malware-db not unavailable + doc validates; degraded path (no db) silent + records malware-db; name-match fires regardless of version; full deps-scan package gate green
close_reason: test_s8_offline_smoke.py added: end-to-end load_malware_db->scan S8 flow over an OSSF-shaped on-disk snapshot; 5 tests, 78 deps-scan pkg tests green. De-risks the wh-8qw wiring offline.

title: Add supply-chain-floor eval cases + re-score (validate S1-S8 end-to-end)
description: GROOM 2026-06-08. Corpus = evals/corpus/cases/<id>/ each {vulnerable_variant, benign_lookalike, label.json, target.md}; multifile cases supported (label-schema.json:88 multifile/support_files). A supply-chain case = a NEUTRALIZED project (package.json/pyproject + lockfile) as vulnerable_variant + a clean one as benign_lookalike; label category 'supply-chain'. SCORING PATH: the supply-chain floor (supply_chain.scan) is DETERMINISTIC, NOT the agent — score these via the floor's own output (deterministic regression ANCHORS), no LLM. ⚠ DRIFT-GUARD COUPLING: adding cases breaks baseline.n_cases==corpus size (baseline.json) — wh-qyf CANNOT land green alone; it must land ATOMICALLY with a baseline update.
notes: VERDICT=NEEDS DECISION (not a clean standalone). Corpus is SATURATED (J=1.0) so these are regression ANCHORS, not headroom. DECISION: (a) deterministic floor-scored anchors + bump baseline.json (n_cases + findings snapshot) in the SAME PR, or (b) fold into wh-aj6's full re-baseline. Either way it lands WITH the baseline (drift-guard). files: evals/corpus/cases/<new> + evals/baseline.json (coupled).
close_reason: Landed 3 floor-scored supply-chain anchors into evals/corpus (118->121); baseline tp/tn/n_cases=121, J=1.0; 178 evals green. Applied via verified apply.sh (landing-prep workflow GO).

title: si-10 borrowings (sec-learn): section-keyed patch-merge + {scene,wrong,correct} record
description: GROOM 2026-06-08. Two DETERMINISTIC sec-learn helpers (Rule 5: no LLM) borrowed from si-10/COLLEAGUE: a section-keyed Markdown patch-merge + an append-only {scene,wrong,correct} correction record. Helpers only — the reflective SKILL wires them in.
design: Files: (1) NEW sec-learn/scripts/patch_merge.py — merge_markdown_patch(base_md, patch_md): a patch section whose '##' heading matches base REPLACES that section; unmatched sections APPEND (append-only, COLLEAGUE map #3). ~30 lines, stdlib. (2) sec-learn/scripts/harvest.py — normalize the existing kind=='correction' rows (harvest.py:~25 already groups 'corrections') into {scene, wrong, correct} records (COLLEAGUE map #4). (3) NEW tests in tests/. All disjoint from the other batch tickets (sec-learn only).
notes: VERDICT=READY. AC (Rule 9 — == AND !=): merge_markdown_patch REPLACES a matching ## section AND APPENDS an unmatched one (pin both); harvest emits a {scene,wrong,correct} record from a correction trace row; pytest green. No LLM/network. Parallel-safe with wh-a49 + wh-j2f (disjoint files).
close_reason: First batch done (team: dev-wh-a49/0aq/j2f, parallel). TL re-verified independently: sec-detect 62, sec-learn 15, ai-attack-kb 63 pytest; downstream deps-scan 65 + shared 87 unaffected by the additive kernel_adjacency SCAN-PLAN field; wh-a49 advisory altitude (no CVSS/finding — comments-only); real KB entries still validate; manifest OK. Wave 5 complete; Wave 4 has wh-7rk+wh-953 left. NOT committed (operator owns git).

title: si-10 borrowing: validate_kb data-minimization lint (no verbatim third-party body)
description: GROOM 2026-06-08. Add a DETERMINISTIC data-minimization lint to validate_kb.py (si-10/COLLEAGUE map #9): KB entries hold paraphrased notes + provenance, NOT a verbatim third-party copyrighted/PII source dump. Heuristic (data-min is inherently fuzzy) — flag a likely pasted-source block.
design: Files: validate_kb.py — add a check in validate_file (after the summary-word-count check, ~:112): flag a single fenced ``` code block > 20 lines OR a blockquote (>) run > 20 lines as 'possible verbatim third-party body — paraphrase + cite via metadata.url'. exit-1 like other validate_kb errors; threshold tunable (module constant). + tests/test_validate_kb.py cases. Reuses split_front_matter/body (validate_kb.py:54).
notes: VERDICT=READY (HEURISTIC — the 20-line threshold is a reasoned default, tunable; data-min can't be exact). AC (Rule 9): an entry with a 25-line fenced block -> INVALID with the data-min message; a normal entry (short grep-hint detections, ≤120-word summary) -> valid; existing 5 entries still pass; pin both. ⚠ touches validate_kb.py — coordinate with the (deferred) si-10 lint sibling if both ever run; none in THIS batch. Parallel-safe with wh-a49 + wh-0aq.
close_reason: First batch done (team: dev-wh-a49/0aq/j2f, parallel). title: Kernel/container trust-boundary awareness signal (eBPF/module/privileged-container) + Dockerfile rationale — spike-10
description: GROOM 2026-06-08. Testable, deterministic kernel/container awareness at ADVISORY altitude (ADR-018 — like the absent-SECURITY.md hygiene note: informational, NO CVSS, NEVER in VULN-FINDINGS.json). Recognize kernel-adjacent / privileged-container code in the detect path + enrich infra.md with the shared-kernel->host-escape rationale (the Docker use case). NO kernel/eBPF memory-safety auditing (Rule 5).
design: Files: (1) sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py — add detect_kernel_adjacency(root) returning markers found, surfaced as an ADDITIVE optional 'kernel_adjacency' list in ScanPlan.to_dict (after detect_infra ~:169). Markers: eBPF (*.bpf.c, libbpf/cilium-ebpf or bpf2go in go.mod, bpftrace), kernel-module (Kbuild, 'obj-m' in Makefile, *.ko, dkms.conf), driver, privileged-container (compose 'privileged:', k8s 'privileged: true'/hostPID/hostNetwork/hostPath, CAP_SYS_ADMIN). (2) sec-detect/scan-plan-schema.json — add the optional 'kernel_adjacency' property (additive = backward-compatible) + update the lock test test_scan_plan_schema.py. (3) tests/test_detect_tools.py — fixtures. (4) _shared/reference/infra.md — add the shared-kernel->host-escape rationale behind the existing privileged/hostPath/runAsNonRoot checklist + the eBPF/module/DKMS markers in 'What to grep for'. The field DRIVES an agent advisory note; it is not a finding.
notes: VERDICT=READY. ⚠ SCOPE wider than first listed: adds scan-plan-schema.json + its lock test (the SCAN-PLAN contract is locked by test_scan_plan_schema.py). ⚠ OVERLAP: touches detect_tools.py — so NOT parallel-safe with wh-7rk (also detect_tools); fine for THIS batch (wh-7rk not in it). AC: eBPF/module/privileged fixtures populate kernel_adjacency; plain app repo -> empty; infra.md carries the shared-kernel rationale; advisory altitude (not in VULN-FINDINGS, no CVSS); tests green incl. updated schema-lock. ALT (lighter, your call): Option B = infra.md + agent advisory only, no schema change (ADR-018-consistent) but not unit-testable — chose Option A (deterministic/testable, Rule 5 + spike-10 T-A wanted fixtures).
close_reason: First batch done (team: dev-wh-a49/0aq/j2f, parallel). TL re-verified independently: sec-detect 62, sec-learn 15, ai-attack-kb 63 pytest; downstream deps-scan 65 + shared 87 unaffected by the additive kernel_adjacency SCAN-PLAN field; wh-a49 advisory altitude (no CVSS/finding — comments-only); real KB entries still validate; manifest OK. Wave 5 complete; Wave 4 has wh-7rk+wh-953 left. NOT committed (operator owns git).

title: Supply-chain floor: PyPI/RubyGems/Go/Cargo/Maven adapters
description: GROOM 2026-06-08: add per-ecosystem adapters behind the existing generic signal core (S1-S8 unchanged). The npm adapter (parse_npm, supply_chain.py:201) is the pattern. Add parse_pypi (pyproject.toml/requirements.txt + poetry.lock/uv.lock), parse_gem (Gemfile + Gemfile.lock), parse_go (go.mod + go.sum), parse_cargo (Cargo.toml + Cargo.lock), parse_maven (pom.xml) — each produces the SAME normalized struct {deps:[{name,spec,source_type}], lifecycle_scripts, lockfile_present, script_files}. Refactor scan() to DETECT the ecosystem (which manifest exists) and dispatch to the right adapter, setting manifest_path + scanned_langs per ecosystem. Per-ecosystem source classification + build hooks (PyPI setup.py = hook; Cargo build.rs; gem extconf.rb; Go has no install hook). Existing 29 npm tests MUST stay green.
design: Files (disjoint from wh-0o7): deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py (add the 5 adapter fns + ecosystem dispatch in scan(); keep parse_npm + the S1-S8 core intact), deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_adapters.py (NEW: one fixture repo per ecosystem — e.g. a pyproject typosquat, a Cargo build.rs trigger — asserting scan() emits the right candidate), deps-scan/SKILL.md (document the per-ecosystem adapters + that npm is the lead). NEUTRALIZED fixtures only. AC: each ecosystem fixture -> correct candidate; npm path unchanged (existing tests pass); pytest green; manifest OK.

title: Add AI-native supply-chain KB technique-class (slopsquatting) + append-ADR
description: RESCOPED after groom 2026-06-08 (x2). ai-attack-kb is the AI/LLM/agent/MCP attack KB (all 5 classes AI-specific). Generic package-malware MECHANICS belong in deps-scan/reference (spike-09) + OSSF DB, NOT here. The AI-NATIVE technique DOES belong: slopsquatting (LLM-hallucinated package names) + AI-SDK typosquatting. CRITICAL: the technique is ECOSYSTEM-AGNOSTIC - npm is just ONE example; it applies equally to PyPI/pip (much slopsquat research is Python), RubyGems, Go modules, crates, Maven. The KB entry documents the cross-ecosystem TECHNIQUE; per-ecosystem detection greps live in the detector (wh-07w adapters), not here. Optional currency - NOT required by wh-07w.

title: T-12.9b: Restore eval headroom — multi-file / real-CVE-in-context corpus
description:
## Goal
The 115-case corpus is SATURATED (J=1.0; the current agent aces single-file pairs — see t-12.9-report.md
+ the ablation showing only a weakened agent drops to 0.8). Add cases that need:
- cross-file taint (source and sink in DIFFERENT files -> the reviewer must build a call graph),
- real un-patched CVE diffs in surrounding noise (distractors, business logic; not minimal snippets),
- genuinely-ambiguous severity/category (test triage, not just detection).
So the CURRENT agent scores < 1.0 (real measurable headroom). May need a multi-file case shape +
score.py file-set matching.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] >= 3 multi-file / CVE-in-context cases the current agent does NOT trivially ace (J < 1.0 on the subset), neutralized filenames
- [ ] corpus count + baseline + drift-guard updated; the eval can again MEASURE improvement
Carry from Phase-12 T-12.9. Pairs with T-12.5 (opus re-baseline).

title: Fix cwd-fragile manifest test (test_real_repo_marketplace_has_no_unknown_top_level_keys uses relative '.')
description:
## Goal / Problem
`packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py:361` (test_real_repo_marketplace_has_no_unknown_top_level_keys)
calls `vm.validate_marketplace(".")` with a RELATIVE path — the ONLY real-repo test not using the
module-level `REPO_ROOT = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]` (line 25). It passes from repo
root, FAILS from any other cwd (reproduced from /tmp: 1 failed, 39 passed). CI runs from root so it is
green there, but the test is cwd-fragile. CONFIRMED real (wh-4ym.4 verify + grooming 2026-06-08).

## Workflow (TDD)
- RED: `cd /tmp && uv run --project <repo>/packaging --with pytest pytest <repo>/packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py::test_real_repo_marketplace_has_no_unknown_top_level_keys -q` -> FAIL.
- GREEN: change `vm.validate_marketplace(".")` -> `vm.validate_marketplace(REPO_ROOT)` (match siblings).
- Re-run from /tmp AND repo root -> both pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] line 361 uses REPO_ROOT, no literal "." — probe: `grep -n 'validate_marketplace(".")' packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py` returns nothing.
- [ ] packaging suite passes from ANY cwd — probe: `cd /tmp && uv run --project <repo>/packaging --with pytest pytest <repo>/packaging/tests -q` -> exit 0, 40 passed.

## File Ownership
`packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py` (line 361) — single file.

## Quality Gate
`uv run --project packaging --with pytest pytest packaging/tests -q` (40 passed, from any cwd).

Ready — no deps. Found by wh-4ym.4 (QA-3) verify.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix poll_feeds ElementTree DeprecationWarning (line 61) + YAML-injection regression test
description:
## Goal / Problem
Two items from wh-4ym.6 (QA-7) verify; grooming 2026-06-08 refined the scope:
1. **REAL — ElementTree truth-value DeprecationWarning** at `poll_feeds.py:61`
   (`idc = kids.get("id") or kids.get("guid")` — `or` on an Element). Under
   `-W error::DeprecationWarning`, test_parse_atom FAILS (1 failed, 7 passed). Fix to explicit `is not None`.
2. **YAML injection — ALREADY SAFE (no rewrite)**: `render_entry` (poll_feeds.py:105) uses
   `yaml.safe_dump(entry, ...)`, so feed text -> dict -> safe_dump is escaped; a crafted feed cannot
   inject YAML keys. Scope reduced to ADD a regression test that PINS this (cheap guard).

## Workflow (TDD)
- RED: `pytest -W error::DeprecationWarning .../test_poll_feeds.py -q` -> test_parse_atom FAILS.
- GREEN: `poll_feeds.py:61` -> `idc = kids.get("id"); idc = idc if idc is not None else kids.get("guid")`.
  Add a poisoned-feed test: a feed item whose title/summary contains `: `, newlines, and `injected_key: x`
  -> `render_entry` output `yaml.safe_load`s to a dict whose top-level keys == the intended schema keys
  (no feed-injected key).
- REFACTOR: keep minimal; confirm line 59 (`link is not None and link.get(...)`) is already safe.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] no DeprecationWarning — probe: `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest .../tests/test_poll_feeds.py -q -W error::DeprecationWarning` exit 0.
- [ ] poisoned-feed YAML regression test — probe: new test asserts no feed-injected top-level YAML key in render_entry output; exit 0.
- [ ] sec-kb-refresh suite green, 0 warnings.

## File Ownership
`plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts/poll_feeds.py` (line 61) + `tests/test_poll_feeds.py`.

Ready — no deps. Found by wh-4ym.6 (QA-7) verify. The original "harden YAML rendering" framing is
RESOLVED-as-already-safe (safe_dump); the load-bearing fix is the DeprecationWarning.
close_reason: Closed
