title: gate_data_edit structural net: 'patch' verb not enumerated (ADR-016 residual)
description: QA (wh-hxt.15 review) noted the structural net (_is_write_or_interp_command / _WRITE_VERBS, gate_data_edit.py:135-152) does NOT enumerate 'patch'. A 'patch reference/known-compromised.osv.json < poison.diff' uses input-redirect '<' (not a '>' write) and 'patch' is not a write/interp verb, so the token-scan does not fire on a DATA-path arg. Classified by QA + WH as a NON-BLOCKING ADR-016 residual for wh-hxt.15 (the structural net closed the >|/xargs/python-c/ed class; patch-apply is separately covered by ADR-016 layer-2 permissions.deny when enabled, plus hard-ordering + the ADR-012 human-PR gate moot it pre-wh-k6l). Follow-up: optionally add 'patch' to _WRITE_VERBS (one line + a BLOCK test) OR confirm permissions.deny coverage is sufficient. Low priority; operator/next-wave decision.
notes: REVIEW 2026-06-11 (dogfood verify of the wh-hxt.15 close): the 'patch' channel is ALREADY hard-blocked — independent of gate_data_edit — by the always-on sibling hook confine_patch_writes.py:142 (`verb == "patch"` → deny "patch applies a diff to the working tree"). EMPIRICALLY confirmed: `patch <watchlist> < poison.diff` → exit 2 BLOCKED. confine_patch_writes also denies git apply/am/push/clean (:51) and any `$VAR`/`$(...)`/backtick indirection (:79, can't prove safe). All 5 confinement hooks (guard_bash, confine_patch_writes, confine_self_writes, gate_kb_edit, gate_data_edit) are registered as one PreToolUse `Bash|Write|Edit` entry in hooks.json — active whenever the plugin loads (NOT operator-opt-in; correct the body's "permissions.deny when enabled" → it's a hook, always on). So this is PURE defense-in-depth redundancy in gate_data_edit's structural net, NOT a hole; confirmed safe to leave at P4. Fix-if-bored: add 'patch' to _WRITE_VERBS (one line + a BLOCK test).

title: Gate-2 PostToolUse content-binding backstop (defense-in-depth detect for un-parseable Bash watchlist writes) — optional, split from wh-hxt.15
description:
## Goal / Problem
SPLIT from the wh-hxt.15 groom (2026-06-11). An OPTIONAL **defense-in-depth** PostToolUse content-binding
backstop for the Gate-2 watchlist DATA file. wh-hxt.15's PreToolUse fix PREVENTS all 4 known Bash bypass shapes
(>|, python -c, xargs, ed); this adds a post-hoc DETECT layer that catches ANY write shape regardless of how the
command is spelled — useful against an un-anticipated future shape. NOT the primary net (PostToolUse fires AFTER
the write — it detects + fails loud, it does not prevent), so this is strictly belt-and-suspenders.

## Scope
- A NEW PostToolUse hook (`gate_data_edit_post.py` + `.sh`) registered in `hooks.json` (a CONTROL-file edit):
  after any tool touches `_shared/reference/known-compromised.osv.json`, assert the file's current bytes ==
  the sha256 of the last CONSUMED data-verdict's validated bytes; mismatch → fail loud (exit 2 / surface).
- Requires a **last-consumed-verdict side-channel**: `gate_data_edit` (PreToolUse) must record the consumed
  verdict's sha256 somewhere the PostToolUse hook can read (e.g. `evals/.last-data-admit.json`, also FROZEN).
- Tests: a write that bypasses PreToolUse (simulated) → PostToolUse detects the mismatch + fails.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A watchlist write whose bytes != the last consumed verdict → PostToolUse fails loud.
- [ ] A legitimate admitted write (bytes == consumed verdict) → PostToolUse passes.
- [ ] hooks suite + manifest + plugin validate green.

## Notes
OPTIONAL — **NOT a wh-k6l blocker** (wh-hxt.15 PreToolUse is the blocker). Low priority; do after wh-hxt.15.
The side-channel file must be in `confine_self_writes.FROZEN_BASENAMES` (the outer loop must not forge it).
Refs: wh-hxt.15 groom note; docs/qa/20260611/dogfood/SECURITY-REPORT.md F-001 recommendation (B).

title: stale .claude/skills/sec-detect path — 4 same-class refs (tool-registry.md:15, SKILL.md:92, 2 test comments; predates the plugins/ move)
description:

- plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/SKILL.md:92

      `grep -rn ".claude/skills/sec-detect" plugins/` returns 0 hits

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

## QA Before Push

- [ ] No regressions: both gates green; no other lines changed in any of the 4 files

## Commit Message Format
Repo rule (cite, don't restate): `docs: …`; author `Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>`; NO AI attribution (CLAUDE.md Policy 12).

## Risks / Dependencies
- May be ABSORBED by the wh-nvk/wh-xn0 registry rewrite — check before fixing; close as superseded
  with the commit cite if so.
- Workflow (Red/Green/Refactor): N/A — doc/comment-only edits, no behavior to test-drive.

## Rollback
git revert — four doc/comment lines.

## Notes
CHECK FIRST: the wh-nvk/wh-xn0 registry rewrite may absorb this — if their impl landed, verify the
path there and close this as superseded (cite the commit). Steps/Workflow: N/A beyond the single edit.

## References
tool-registry.md:15; f8179f9 (the stale-path precedent); wh-nvk/wh-xn0 (the absorbing rewrite);
wave-1a QA report (pre-existing finding).

title: Stale .claude/skills/ dev-path strings in skill test docstrings/comments (post plugins/ migration)
description:
## Goal / Problem
The skill packages moved `.claude/skills/ → plugins/white-hacker/skills/`, but many test docstrings/comments still print the OLD dev path, so copy-pasting their `Run:` command (or trusting the comment) fails. Doc/cosmetic only — no scan behavior is wrong; the cost is a broken copy-paste and a misleading comment. Threat model: N/A (no untrusted input; string hygiene).

## Reproduction
On a fresh checkout, copy a `Run:` command out of a stale test docstring and run it verbatim:
```bash
# e.g. the path printed in plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests/test_detect_tools.py docstring
cd .claude/skills/sec-detect/scripts   # → "no such file or directory" (it's now plugins/white-hacker/skills/…)
```

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** every `Run:`/comment DEV-path string points at `plugins/white-hacker/skills/…` (the current source tree).
- **Actual:** some still print `.claude/skills/…` (the pre-migration path) → the command/instruction is dead.

## Suspected Root Cause
Docstrings/comments were not swept when the packages moved out of `.claude/skills/` into `plugins/white-hacker/skills/`. Pure string staleness — no code path depends on it. Re-verify each `file:line` at claim (RED) — numbers predate later edits.

Genuinely-stale DEV-path strings (fix → `plugins/white-hacker/skills/`):
- `_shared/scripts/tests/test_degradation.py` (`Run:`)
- `sec-detect/scripts/tests/test_detect_tools.py`, `test_scan_plan_schema.py` (`Run:`)
- `sec-detect/SKILL.md` (`Run:`/checklist path)
- `deps-scan/scripts/tests/test_normalize_deps.py` (`Run:`)
- `secrets-scan/scripts/tests/test_redact.py` (`Run:`)
- `ai-attack-kb/scripts/tests/test_validate_kb.py` (`Run:`), `ai-attack-kb/scripts/lint_skill.py`, `ai-attack-kb/SKILL.md`
- Audit (decide per-ref dev-vs-deployed): `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/DDD.md`, agent profiles (`qa-engineer.md`, `tech-lead.md`, `researcher.md`), `_shared/reference/tool-registry.md`, `_shared/reference/ai-llm.md`

## Files in Scope (the fix surface)
The stale-path files above (docstrings/comments/`.md` only — NO code logic). **DO NOT blanket find-replace.**

**DO-NOT-TOUCH — intentional `.claude/skills/` refs (deployment/vendor/hook/migration), MUST stay:**
- `install/scrub_vendored.py` + `install/tests/test_scrub_vendored.py` — the scrub NEUTERS the path to the vendored `.claude/skills/` install location BY DESIGN.
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests/test_confine_self_writes.py` + `test_gate_kb_edit.py` — assert hook ALLOW/BLOCK behavior on the DEPLOYED `.claude/skills/` path.
- migration-note comments (`test_normalize_deps.py`, `test_ci_gate.py` — "survives the plugin migration (.claude/skills → plugins/white-hacker/skills)") — intentional.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
1. **RED** — re-verify each `file:line` against the current tree; confirm the `.claude/skills` segment is a DEV-path string (not a do-not-touch ref). A docstring change has no test that fails, so the gate is "affected package tests stay green" (no behavior change) — pin nothing new; this is a string sweep.
2. **GREEN** — surgical, per-file: swap ONLY the `.claude/skills` path segment → `plugins/white-hacker/skills`, keeping each command/comment style identical. Leave deployment/vendor/hook/migration refs untouched.
3. **REFACTOR** — none.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every stale DEV-path `Run:`/comment string points at `plugins/white-hacker/skills/`.
- [ ] Intentional deployment/vendor (scrub), hook-behavior, and migration-note refs are UNCHANGED (grep confirms they still read `.claude/skills`).
- [ ] No regression: each affected skill package's tests stay green (`uv run`, bounded `-n 4`/`nice`).

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
```bash
# for each touched skill package:
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/<skill>/scripts --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/<skill>/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```
- [ ] Touched package tests pass · manifest green · plugin validate green (Policy 12 — non-zero keeps it `in_progress`).

## Severity ↔ Priority
**P4** — cosmetic/doc; no behavioral impact on a real scan (only a dead copy-paste / misleading comment).

## Commit Message Format
`docs: repoint stale .claude/skills dev-path strings to plugins/white-hacker/skills` (author Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>, no AI attribution).

## Rollback
`git revert` the sweep (docstring/comment-only; nothing depends on it).

## Risks / Dependencies
- Risk: a blanket replace would break the DO-NOT-TOUCH scrub/hook/migration refs — fix per-file, grep-verify the intentional refs survive.
- Related: discovered while reconciling the two white-hacker agents (wh-g2v). Sibling stale-path ticket wh-4rb (sec-detect specifics) — consider MERGE per `.notes/order.md` serialize track.

## Notes / References
INCREMENT 1 DONE 2026-06-09 (already swept; do not redo): `_shared/scripts/conftest.py:11` comment + `_shared/scripts/tests/test_registry_lock.py:6` `Run:` path → `plugins/white-hacker/skills/` (gate: `_shared/scripts` 87 passed). REMAINING = the stale list above minus those two.
design: Surgical, per-file. Keep each command/comment style identical - only swap the `.claude/skills` path segment to `plugins/white-hacker/skills` where the string refers to the DEV/source tree. Leave deployment/vendor/hook/migration refs untouched. No behavior change (docstrings/comments only) - gate = affected package tests stay green.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every stale DEV-path `Run:`/comment string points at plugins/white-hacker/skills/.
- Intentional deployment/vendor (scrub), hook-behavior, and migration-note refs are UNCHANGED.
- Affected skill package tests pass (uv run, bounded -n<=4 / nice).
notes: GROOMED to beads-bug-template.md 2026-06-11. Body restructured into bug-template sections; content preserved (the stale-path list, the DO-NOT-TOUCH intentional-refs list, the INCREMENT-1-DONE note). Per-file:line must be re-verified at claim (RED step) — the line numbers predate later edits; treat the lists as the dev's checklist, not gospel. P4 (cosmetic/doc — no behavioral impact on a real scan; bumped down from P3 per the severity↔priority rubric: stale docstrings don't break a scan, only copy-paste of a Run: line).

title: k>=3 opus bootstrap re-baseline before first tagged release
description:
## Goal / Problem

Re-run the eval baseline at k>=3 paired bootstrap to get a confidence interval (CI) on Youden's J before the first tagged release. The existing baseline (wh-aj6, k=1, J=0.917, FPR=0, n_cases=121) was produced with a user-chosen k=1 scope. For a hardened release gate, the original wh-aj6 AC required k>=3 to bound variance. This ticket triggers that run.

## Background / Context

- wh-aj6 (DONE): produced the honest opus baseline. Current state: `evals/baseline.json` has `n_cases: 121`, `youden_j: 0.917355`, `model: claude-opus` (verified 2026-06-11). The run findings are in `evals/runs/baseline-findings.json`.
- Corpus: 121 cases in `evals/corpus/cases/` (verified 2026-06-11 by count).
- The k>=3 run re-uses the wh-aj6 harness workflow. Cost estimate: ~3x the k=1 cost (~2M tokens total).
- Output: updated `evals/baseline.json` and `evals/runs/baseline-findings.json` with k>=3 bootstrap CI (lower and upper J bounds).
- Gate: `evals/keep_or_revert.py` (deterministic, never auto-merge) -- a k>=3 baseline that scores LOWER than the current k=1 baseline triggers a review, not an auto-revert.
- `evals/score.py` is the scoring entry point (deterministic; never an LLM for scoring -- Policy 5).
- Drift-guard: `baseline.n_cases` must equal the corpus case count (121 as of 2026-06-11). If the corpus grows before this runs, re-run wh-aj6 on the full corpus first.
- Trigger: before the first tagged release (not on a fixed date -- operator decides when to trigger).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

N/A -- this is an OUTER-LOOP eval re-baseline, not an inner-loop code task. The workflow is: run the k>=3 bootstrap via the wh-aj6 harness, score, gate, record. No code is written. Do NOT apply the Red/Green/Refactor TDD pattern here. However, the gate scripts are the verification mechanism:
- `evals/score.py` (deterministic) scores each run.
- `evals/keep_or_revert.py` (deterministic, no auto-merge) gates the candidate baseline.

## Steps (with description)

1. Confirm the corpus case count equals `baseline.n_cases` (121). If the corpus has grown, re-run wh-aj6 on the full corpus first.
2. Neutralize `vulnerable_variant`/`benign_lookalike` filenames in the corpus before the eval run (filenames leak the answer; canonical: `.claude/CLAUDE.md` QA-flow section).
3. Run the k>=3 paired bootstrap using the wh-aj6 harness workflow (local `claude -p` on subscription; no API key needed for local use).
4. For each of the k>=3 runs: `uv run python evals/score.py --findings <run-findings.json> --corpus evals/corpus/cases` and record J, FPR, FNR.
5. Compute the bootstrap CI across the k runs.
6. Run `uv run python evals/keep_or_revert.py --baseline evals/baseline.json --candidate <candidate.json>` to gate before updating.
7. If the gate passes (KEEP): update `evals/baseline.json` with the new J, CI bounds, k, and run metadata. Update `evals/runs/baseline-findings.json`.
8. Record the total token cost in `.notes/qa/<YYYYMMDD>/README.md` (local-only, gitignored -- never committed).

## Files to Create/Modify

- `evals/baseline.json` -- MODIFY (update with k>=3 J, CI bounds, run metadata)
- `evals/runs/baseline-findings.json` -- MODIFY (update with k>=3 run findings)

NOTE: QA evidence (raw run artifacts, cost, neutralized-name mapping) goes in `.notes/qa/<YYYYMMDD>/` (local-only, gitignored). Only `baseline.json` and `baseline-findings.json` are committed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `evals/baseline.json` contains a k>=3 bootstrap result: `k >= 3`, a `ci_lower` and `ci_upper` bound on Youden's J, and an updated `n_cases` equal to the corpus case count at run time.
- [ ] `evals/score.py` passes with zero errors on the new findings (no schema violations, no crash).
- [ ] `evals/keep_or_revert.py` returns KEEP for the candidate (or a REVERT decision is documented with a human review note before updating baseline.json).
- [ ] Token cost and run verdict are recorded in `.notes/qa/<YYYYMMDD>/README.md` (local, not committed).
- [ ] `baseline.n_cases == len(corpus cases)` drift-guard satisfied at the time of the run.

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)

```
uv run python evals/score.py --findings evals/runs/baseline-findings.json --corpus evals/corpus/cases
uv run python evals/keep_or_revert.py --baseline evals/baseline.json --candidate <candidate.json>
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
```

- [ ] `score.py` green (deterministic; never auto-merged via an LLM judge)
- [ ] `keep_or_revert.py` returns KEEP (or REVERT documented + human-approved before baseline update)
- [ ] `validate_manifest.py` green
- [ ] `claude plugin validate` green
- [ ] Inner-loop package tests: N/A (no code changed)

## Rollback

Restore `evals/baseline.json` and `evals/runs/baseline-findings.json` from git (the previous k=1 wh-aj6 baseline is the rollback state).

## Risks / Dependencies

- Token cost: ~2M tokens for k>=3. Operator decides when to trigger based on budget.
- Corpus drift: if new cases are added before this runs, the drift-guard will fail. Sequence: corpus additions first, then re-baseline at the new count.
- A lower J at k>=3 vs k=1 is expected variance (k=1 may have been lucky). Human review required before updating baseline if J drops significantly.
- `evals/keep_or_revert.py` is deterministic and NEVER auto-merges -- Policy 5 and Policy 12.
- Trigger: operator-gated (before first tagged release, not a date).

## Notes / References

- `evals/baseline.json` (verified 2026-06-11): `n_cases: 121`, `youden_j: 0.917355`, `model: claude-opus`. File is the current source of truth for the release gate.
- `evals/corpus/cases/` (verified 2026-06-11): 121 case directories.
- `evals/score.py` and `evals/keep_or_revert.py` (verified to exist 2026-06-11): the deterministic outer-loop gates.
- DEFERRED -- manual smoke: the eval run is operator-triggered, not CI-triggered. Mark Status->done only when baseline.json is updated with k>=3 CI AND keep_or_revert.py passed.
acceptance_criteria: baseline.json regenerated from a k>=3 opus bootstrap with a reported CI; drift-guard green; cost recorded

title: Make repo-fixture deps-scan tests skip-if-fixture-absent (sandbox-portable)
description: test_normalize_deps.py (docs/research/poc-trivy-sca) and test_supply_chain.py::test_demo_poc_scans_and_validates (docs/research/poc-supply-chain) reach OUTSIDE the deps-scan package for fixtures via a .git repo-root walk, so they cannot run in the minimal Docker sandbox (wh-4l1) — the container --ignore/-k excludes them. Make them pytest.skip() when the fixture/.git is absent (or move the fixtures under the package) so the sandbox can run the FULL suite. Non-blocking; the sandbox already runs the self-contained floor/S8 suite green (67 passed).
acceptance_criteria: both tests skip cleanly (not error/fail) when the repo fixture is absent; sandbox CMD can drop the --ignore/-k exclusions and still pass
close_reason: 2 repo-fixture tests skip-if-absent (pytestmark.skipif + pytest.skip); sandbox Dockerfile drops --ignore/-k. Verified native 78 passed; sandbox/in-container 67 passed+11 skipped.

title: ADR note: subdir corpus-case layout for floor-scored multifile cases
description: WHY: wh-qyf introduced a NEW eval-corpus fixture layout where vulnerable_variant/ and benign_lookalike/ are DIRECTORIES holding an npm project tree (package.json + lockfile + scripts/), vs the existing flat vulnerable_variant.<ext>/benign_lookalike.<ext> files (or flat _support_*.py siblings for mf-* cases). Justified — an npm/floor-scored fixture needs a real on-disk project dir — and leak-neutral (the floor ignores dir names; verified content-driven). Per Rule 11, a new convention gets an ADR appended to docs/ARD.md. WHAT: append a short ADR documenting the subdir-case layout + that promote_finding.py multifile mode (wh-705) builds it. Flagged by the wh-qyf landing-prep workflow (leak-neutralization lens). Non-blocking; docs-only.
acceptance_criteria: docs/ARD.md gains an ADR entry describing the subdir corpus-case layout + its rationale + the promote_finding.py multifile path
close_reason: ADR-020 appended to docs/ARD.md (append-only, 9/0); team GO (QA+white-hacker both go, empirical floor reproduction). Documents the subdir corpus-case layout + score.py suffix-match + promote multifile path.

title: Pin + wire the offline OSSF malicious-packages snapshot for S8 (operator/CI)
description: Take S8 from degraded->active: clone github.com/ossf/malicious-packages, PIN the commit (ADR-006), and point malware_db.load_malware_db (built by wh-0o7) at its ./osv dir. Absent -> S8 degrades cleanly (already wired). Operator/CI step — needs network + a pin decision. See deps-scan/reference/MALWARE-DB.md. Pre-release.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-08 VERDICT=READY (operator/CI — the only independently-actionable Wave-6 ticket). Fully documented: deps-scan/reference/MALWARE-DB.md (clone OSSF malicious-packages, PIN a SHA per ADR-006, point load_malware_db at ./osv). Loader (wh-0o7) done+tested; absent→degrades. Mostly OPERATOR (needs network + pin decision) — NOT headless-dev-runnable here. Residual dev/CI: wire scan(malware_db=load_malware_db(<pinned>)) at the deps-scan call site + record the SHA. AC: pinned SHA recorded; S8 active (not degraded) in the wired invocation.
close_reason: OSSF snapshot pinned (174a862) + S8 VERIFIED ACTIVE on real data (a-constructor.js/MAL-2024-1708 → HIGH S8). SHA+verification+caveats in MALWARE-DB.md; S8 marked active in SKILL.md; fetch-snapshot.sh entrypoint bug fixed.

title: Real-CVE-in-noise eval corpus for headroom (research, uncertain)
description:
## Goal
T-12.9 (single-file) + T-12.9b (cross-file) proved synthetic paired cases are ACED by the frontier
agent (J=1.0). The only realistic lever to restore measurable headroom: real un-patched CVE diffs in
real business-logic noise / many-file indirection / obfuscation.
## DEFERRED / RESEARCH
Research-scale + uncertain (the agent may recognize known CVEs). Pairs with wh-aj6 (opus re-baseline).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] >= 3 real-CVE-in-noise cases where the CURRENT agent scores < 1.0 (neutralized; baseline + drift-guard updated)
      OR a documented spike concluding synthetic+real headroom is infeasible against the frontier agent.
See docs/qa/20260607/t-12.9b-report.md for the finding that motivates this.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-08 VERDICT=RESEARCH/DEFER. Corpus is saturated (J=1.0; Phase-13 found synthetic headroom infeasible) → real-CVE-in-noise value is UNCERTAIN (likely more anchors, not headroom). Same drift-guard coupling as wh-qyf (lands atomically with a re-baseline). Keep deferred (P4); if pursued, pair with wh-aj6. files: evals/corpus/ + baseline.
close_reason: Path B: spike-11 (docs/research/spike-real-cve-headroom-2026-06.md) concludes real-CVE headroom infeasible under the current scorer (presence-not-rank); the lever is the scorer, not the corpus. Unblocks wh-aj6.

value: Epic convention: the 'Execution Waves' section in an epic description is MANDATORY (>1 child) and rendered as a TABLE (wave | ticket | one-line | type | blocked-by) — one wave = one /launch-team run; within-wave tickets must be parallel-safe (no intra-wave dep + disjoint File Ownership; verify vs bd deps). Refine tickets via /groom (one at a time), never hand-edit; bodies follow docs/beads_templates/{beads-epic,beads-ticket,beads-spike}-template.md.

When a design-ticket SPIKE's grounding/researcher CONFIRMS load-bearing threat-intel (a real CVE/GHSA, e.g. the TeamPCP/Trivy compromise), MATERIALIZE the verification (RQ1) + exposure (RQ2) to docs/research/<date>_<topic>.md AT GROOMING — do not leave confirmed, sourced findings only in the ticket body + the gitignored .claude/agent-memory/. The full design can still be the spike's deliverable; but verified threat-intel belongs in docs/research/ immediately (repo convention: research .md under docs/). Example: docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md (wh-562), 20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md (wh-5es).

Floor-scored eval cases (supply-chain, S1-S8) differ structurally from agent-scored cases: (1) scored by deterministic supply_chain.scan, NOT the LLM agent — an agent run marks them FN and depresses J, so their findings_snapshot entries must come from the floor; (2) need PER-VARIANT PROJECT SUBDIRS (vulnerable_variant/ + benign_lookalike/ each with package.json+lockfile) because the floor scans a DIRECTORY, not a flat file; (3) promote_finding.py builds only the flat 4-file case, so it CANNOT promote subdir cases — human hand-places or extends it; (4) floor findings are manifest-level (line 0) so the label uses line=1 as a tol=3 placeholder. Worked example + reproduce: PATCHES/wh-qyf/ (wh-qyf proposal).

OPEN QUESTION (wh-sml 2026-06-14, operator-driven test needed): does the harness ENFORCE the plugin-subagent tools: allowlist at runtime? DEFINITIONS ARE CLEAN — working-tree agents/white-hacker.md:16 AND the loaded v0.2.0 cache snapshot both grant Read,Grep,Glob,Bash,SendMessage,ToolSearch (NO Write/Edit); both plugin.json v0.2.0; NO drift. Conflicting RUNTIME signals are UNVERIFIED/untrustworthy: (i) the agent-registry display listed Write/Edit; (ii) one WH subagent self-reported issuing a Write that hit confine_self_writes — but it had read confine_self_writes.py so the message may be synthesized, NOT a real dispatch. A SECOND WH subagent correctly REFUSED the injection-shaped capability probe (injection-resistance held) and asserted Write removed. CANNOT be resolved by a delegated-reviewer probe (itself injection-shaped + self-reports untrustworthy) -> needs an OPERATOR-DRIVEN controlled test. If tools: is NOT enforced for plugin subagents, C4 capability-removal is weaker than ADR-010/016 claims and the teammate-mode gap escalates to unconfined Write/Edit (contingent). LESSON: operator capability-tests of the agent's own tool-scoping belong in a controlled operator session, not delegated-reviewer instructions. Ref: docs/research/20260614_plugin_hooks_subagent_scope.md §5.0/§5.2, follow-up (d).

Plugin hooks.json PreToolUse guards (Bash|Write|Edit: guard_bash/confine_patch_writes/confine_self_writes/gate_kb_edit/gate_data_edit) fire by RUNTIME MODE (verified wh-sml, CC 2.1.177): TOP-LEVEL fires; SUBAGENT fires (2026-06-13 dogfood + hooks-doc agent_id 'present only when the hook fires inside a subagent call'); TEAMMATE does NOT fire (live: teammate curl to non-feed host + Write to docs/research/ both succeeded; confound ruled out — plugin ENABLED + CLAUDE_CODE_CHILD_SESSION=1 + top-level enforces). DISTINGUISH frontmatter hooks: field (ignored for plugin subagents) from plugin-scope hooks.json (ADR-017; fires subagents, not teammates). CONSEQUENCE: in white-hacker's PRIMARY teammate mode the ENTIRE Bash-vector tripwire (egress/secret-read/git-mutation/self-disablement) is un-gated; ONLY tools capability-removal survives (white-hacker has NO Write/Edit — agents/white-hacker.md:16; teammate honors the tools allowlist). CRITICAL: there is NO permissions.deny configured anywhere today (plugin.json has none; .claude/settings.json only bd-prime hooks; settings.local.json has allow-only) — so teammate inheritance buys nothing now; the deny-set must be CREATED. Fix levers: (1) create permissions.deny mirroring the Bash backstops (teammates inherit lead perms); (2) run white-hacker as a SUBAGENT (hooks fire). Loading the plugin confines the WHOLE top-level session + subagents (blocks ordinary source edits) — teammates are the only unconfined writers. Report: docs/research/20260614_plugin_hooks_subagent_scope.md.
